# Introduction

En este Markdown se genera la primera parte de la pipeline, la cual contiene los siguientes apartados generales:

- Selección y tratamiento de los datos.

- Selección de las pathways a incluir.

- Clustering basado en transcriptómica e identificación de clusters con mayor severidad.

- Generación de severity scores Motor y No Motor mediante agrupación de las señales obtenidas.

- Relación del severity score con las covariables clínicas.

- Predicción de severity score utilizando transcriptómica.

Los cambios introducidos en este documento son la **inclusión de un modificador para el score**, de manera que cada señal contribuye de manera proporcional a la diferencia entre los clusters comparados. También se añaden apartados con **análisis de la relación del score con el valor de covariables clínicas** y con la **predicción del severity score utilizando la transcriptómica** como predictores.

Sobre estos cambios se realizaron además:

- El **cálculo del severity score modificado para covariables numéricas** pasa de: sev_score_mod <- abs(median(cluster_i[[variable]]) - median(cluster_j[[variable]])) / abs(median(cluster_i[[variable]])) + abs(median(cluster_j[[variable]])) a sev_score_mod <- abs(median(cluster_i[[variable]]) - median(cluster_j[[variable]])) / max(median(cluster_i[[variable]]), median(cluster_j[[variable]]))

- Al calcular la **relación entre los resultados de severity score y el valor clínico** de las distintas covariables ahora se incluyen los resultados para severity score **con y sin modificación**, así como resultados desglosados **por visitas** (a su vez se presentan los resultados para el score acumulado de visitas anteriores y para el score puramente de la visita en cuestión).

- En el apartado de predicción se utilizan modelos ridge regression, lasso y random forest para **predecir el valor de severity score de las visitas futuras** utilizando la transcriptómica de visitas anteriores. Se incluyen resultados para severity score modificado y sin modificar.


- Por último se incluye la predicción de los SRG mediante transcriptómica. Se incluye la predicción del SRG de la misma visita y el acumulado de la misma con la siguiente.

# Data Selection and Preprocessing


```{r}

# Patients metadata (covariates)
Covariates <- as.data.frame(readRDS(file.path(base_dir, "metadata_reduced", "PPMI_covariates_extra")))
rownames(Covariates) <- Covariates$sample_id


# Visit to include in the analysis
Visit <- c("M0","M12","M24","M36") # "M0","M12","M24","M36", "M6"

cohort <- c("Sporadic") #  "Healthy Control", "Sporadic", "GBA - Aff", "LRRK2 - Aff", "LRRK2 - Unaff", "SNCA - Aff", "SNCA - Unaff", "GBA - Unaff"

Diagnosis <- c("Case") # "Case", "Control"

# Pathways - Genes
# Subsets_pathways <- readRDS(file.path(base_dir, "Pipeline", "Subsets_pathways_msigdb_names"))

Subsets_pathways <- readRDS(file.path(base_dir, "Pipeline", "Subsets_pathways_Reactome"))


# Calcular la longitud (número de genes) de cada pathway
pathway_sizes <- sapply(Subsets_pathways, length)

# Pathway con mayor número de genes
max_pathway <- names(which.max(pathway_sizes))
max_size <- max(pathway_sizes)

# Pathway con menor número de genes
min_pathway <- names(which.min(pathway_sizes))
min_size <- min(pathway_sizes)

# Mostrar resultados
cat("Pathway con mayor número de genes:\n", max_pathway, " (", max_size, " genes)\n\n")
cat("Pathway con menor número de genes:\n", min_pathway, " (", min_size, " genes)\n")

```



```{r}

Medication <- readRDS("PD_Medical_History") %>% filter(sample_id %in% Covariates$sample_id) %>% select(c("participant_id", "sample_id", "visit_name", "use_of_pd_medication", "pd_medication_recent_use_months_after_baseline", "on_levodopa", "on_dopamine_agonist", "on_other_pd_medications"))

# saveRDS(Medication, "Medication")


library(combinat)

# 1. Filtrar datos relevantes y asegurar formato consistente
df_clean <- Covariates %>% filter(mutation == "Sporadic") %>%
  filter(visit_name %in% unique(Covariates$visit_name)) %>%
  mutate(upd23a_medication_for_pd = ifelse(upd23a_medication_for_pd == "Yes", 1, 0))

# 2. Crear dataframe ancho para analizar por paciente
df_wide <- df_clean %>%
  select(participant_id, visit_name, upd23a_medication_for_pd) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = visit_name,
    values_from = upd23a_medication_for_pd,
    values_fill = NA  # Considerar NA como visita no asistida
  )

# 3. Función MEJORADA para evaluar combinaciones y guardar participant_ids
evaluate_combination <- function(visits, data) {
  # Filtrar columnas de interés
  subset_data <- data %>% select(participant_id, all_of(visits))
  
  # Identificar pacientes que:
  # - Tienen datos en TODAS las visitas seleccionadas (sin NAs)
  # - NUNCA tuvieron medicación (valor = 0) en esas visitas
  complete_cases <- complete.cases(subset_data %>% select(-participant_id))
  
  # Filtrar datos completos
  complete_data <- subset_data[complete_cases, ]
  
  # Encontrar pacientes nunca medicados (suma = 0)
  medication_sum <- rowSums(complete_data %>% select(all_of(visits)))
  never_medicated <- medication_sum == 0
  
  # Obtener los participant_ids de estos pacientes
  clean_patient_ids <- complete_data$participant_id[never_medicated]
  
  return(list(
    visits = visits,
    n_patients = length(clean_patient_ids),
    n_complete_cases = sum(complete_cases),
    participant_ids = clean_patient_ids
  ))
}

# 4. Generar todas las combinaciones posibles
all_visits <- unique(Covariates$visit_name)

# Combinaciones de 3 visitas
comb_3 <- combn(all_visits, 3, simplify = FALSE)

# Combinaciones de 4 visitas
comb_4 <- combn(all_visits, 4, simplify = FALSE)

# 5. Evaluar todas las combinaciones
results_3 <- lapply(comb_3, evaluate_combination, data = df_wide)
results_4 <- lapply(comb_4, evaluate_combination, data = df_wide)

# 6. Convertir resultados a dataframe
results_df_3 <- do.call(rbind, lapply(results_3, function(x) {
  data.frame(
    visits = paste(x$visits, collapse = "-"),
    n_patients = x$n_patients,
    n_complete_cases = x$n_complete_cases,
    n_visits = 3,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
}))

results_df_4 <- do.call(rbind, lapply(results_4, function(x) {
  data.frame(
    visits = paste(x$visits, collapse = "-"),
    n_patients = x$n_patients,
    n_complete_cases = x$n_complete_cases,
    n_visits = 4,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
}))

# 7. Combinar todos los resultados
all_results <- rbind(results_df_3, results_df_4)

# 8. Encontrar las mejores combinaciones
best_combinations <- all_results %>%
  arrange(desc(n_patients)) %>%
  head(10)

# 9. Crear una lista con los participant_ids para cada combinación top
top_combinations_list <- list()

# Para combinaciones de 3 visitas
for(i in 1:length(results_3)) {
  combo_name <- paste(results_3[[i]]$visits, collapse = "-")
  top_combinations_list[[combo_name]] <- results_3[[i]]$participant_ids
}

# Para combinaciones de 4 visitas
for(i in 1:length(results_4)) {
  combo_name <- paste(results_4[[i]]$visits, collapse = "-")
  top_combinations_list[[combo_name]] <- results_4[[i]]$participant_ids
}

# 10. Mostrar resultados principales
print("Top 10 combinaciones con más pacientes sin medicación:")
print(best_combinations)

# 11. Función para explorar una combinación específica
explore_combination <- function(visit_combo, results_list) {
  combo_str <- paste(visit_combo, collapse = "-")
  patient_ids <- results_list[[combo_str]]
  
  cat("Combinación:", combo_str, "\n")
  cat("Número de pacientes:", length(patient_ids), "\n")
  cat("Participant IDs:", paste(patient_ids, collapse = ", "), "\n\n")
  
  return(patient_ids)
}

# 12. Ejemplo: explorar las 3 mejores combinaciones
cat("=== DETALLE DE LAS MEJORES COMBINACIONES ===\n")
for(i in 1:3) {
  best_combo <- strsplit(as.character(best_combinations$visits[i]), "-")[[1]]
  explore_combination(best_combo, top_combinations_list)
}

# 13. Guardar todos los resultados en un objeto para uso futuro
final_results <- list(
  summary = best_combinations,
  patient_lists = top_combinations_list,
  all_results = all_results
)

# 14. También puedes crear un dataframe con toda la información
detailed_results <- data.frame()
all_results_list <- c(results_3, results_4)

for(result in all_results_list) {
  combo_name <- paste(result$visits, collapse = "-")
  detailed_results <- rbind(detailed_results, 
    data.frame(
      visits = combo_name,
      n_patients = result$n_patients,
      n_complete_cases = result$n_complete_cases,
      n_visits = length(result$visits),
      participant_ids = paste(result$participant_ids, collapse = "; "),
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
  )
}

# Ordenar por número de pacientes
detailed_results <- detailed_results %>% arrange(desc(n_patients))

print("Resultados detallados con todos los participant_ids:")
print(detailed_results %>% head(10))


pacientes_ideales <- top_combinations_list[["M0-M12-M6"]] 

# 
# Covariates <- Covariates %>% 
#   filter(participant_id %in% pacientes_ideales & visit_name %in% c("M0", "M6", "M12"))


```





### Filtering metadata

This filtering can be based on visit, diagnosis and mutation status

```{r}


Covariates %>%
  filter(!is.na(mutation)) %>%        # solo filas con valor en mutation
  group_by(mutation) %>%
  summarise(visitas = paste(unique(visit_name), collapse = ", "),
            n = n()) %>%
  arrange(mutation)


Covariates <- Covariates %>%
  group_by(participant_id) %>%
  mutate(mutation = ifelse(all(is.na(mutation)), NA, first(na.omit(mutation)))) %>%
  ungroup()


# Identificar los participant_id que tienen todos los valores de Visit
valid_participants <- Covariates %>%
  group_by(participant_id) %>%
  filter(all(Visit %in% visit_name)) %>%
  pull(participant_id) %>%
  unique()

# Filtrar los datos según las condiciones adicionales
Covariates <- Covariates %>%
  filter(participant_id %in% valid_participants &
         visit_name %in% Visit &
         case_control_other_latest %in% Diagnosis &
         mutation %in% cohort)


# Mostrar el dataset resultante
datatable(Covariates, options = list(
  pageLength = 10,
  searching = TRUE,
  scrollX = TRUE
))


```



```{r}
# Tratamiento de valores faltantes:

# Identificar las columnas numéricas
numerical_columns <- sapply(Covariates, is.numeric)

# Extraer los nombres de las columnas numéricas
Covariates_num_names <- names(Covariates)[numerical_columns]


# Calcular %NA
percent_na <- function(df) {
  # Calcular el porcentaje de NAs en cada columna
  na_percent <- colMeans(is.na(df)) * 100
  return(na_percent)
}


# Eliminar aquellas variables con % NA > 10%
PercentNA_Df <- as.data.frame(percent_na(Covariates), nm = "Percent.NAs")

PercentNA_names_clean <- rownames(subset(PercentNA_Df, Percent.NAs > 10))

Covariates_clean <- Covariates[,!colnames(Covariates) %in% PercentNA_names_clean]


Covariates <- Covariates_clean

PercentNA_Df <- as.data.frame(percent_na(Covariates), nm = "Percent.NAs")


# Imputar el resto de NAs con la mediana para variables numéricas y con el valor más común para las variables factor.
## Seleccionar las columnas numéricas
numeric_columns <- Covariates[, sapply(Covariates, is.numeric)]

## Calcular la mediana de cada columna numérica, ignorando los NA
medianas <- apply(numeric_columns, 2, function(x) median(x, na.rm = TRUE))

## Reemplazar los NA con la mediana de la columna solo en las columnas numéricas
numeric_imputed <- as.data.frame(lapply(1:ncol(numeric_columns), function(i) {
  x <- numeric_columns[, i]
  x[is.na(x)] <- medianas[i]
  return(x)
}), col.names = colnames(numeric_columns))


## Seleccionar las columnas no numéricas
non_numeric_columns <- Covariates[, !sapply(Covariates, is.numeric)]

## Función para calcular la moda
calculate_mode <- function(x) {
  ux <- unique(x)
  ux[which.max(tabulate(match(x, ux)))]
}

# Reemplazar los NA con la moda de la columna en las columnas no numéricas
non_numeric_imputed <- as.data.frame(lapply(1:ncol(non_numeric_columns), function(i) {
  x <- non_numeric_columns[, i]
  moda <- calculate_mode(x[!is.na(x)])
  x[is.na(x)] <- moda
  return(x)
}), col.names = colnames(non_numeric_columns))

# Combinar las columnas numéricas imputadas con las columnas no numéricas imputadas
Covariates_imputado <- cbind(non_numeric_imputed, numeric_imputed)

# Opcional: Si deseas mantener el orden original de las columnas
Covariates_imputado <- Covariates_imputado[, colnames(Covariates)]

rownames(Covariates_imputado) <- Covariates_imputado$sample_id


# Comprobar si todas las variables están libres de NA
table(percent_na(Covariates_imputado) == 0)

Covariates <- Covariates_imputado

```

## Transcriptomics data {#sec:transcriptomics}

In this first section, the file containing the expression values of the selected genes in PPMI patients will be loaded.
It includes data for all visits. Additionally, it contains columns with the diagnosis and sample ID, which will be filtered out.

```{r warning=FALSE}

# This dataset contains the samples resulting from previous data selection (2832 samples × 58,780 genes)
PPMI_exp <- readRDS(file.path(base_dir, "1aFase_ReducirGenes", "PPMI_exp"))

dim(PPMI_exp)

# Patients from the selected visit are retained
PPMI_exp_case <- PPMI_exp %>%
  filter(row.names(.) %in% Covariates$sample_id)


# Case samples (sample_id = "Case") are selected. A merge is then performed with the Covariates dataset, which has been previously filtered.
PPMI_exp_case$sample_id <- rownames(PPMI_exp_case)
case <- Covariates[, c("case_control_other_latest", "sample_id")]

PPMI_exp_case <- merge(PPMI_exp_case, case, by = "sample_id")

# The sample_id is set as the row names, and non-expression variables are removed.
rownames(PPMI_exp_case) <- PPMI_exp_case$sample_id
PPMI_exp_case$sample_id <- NULL
PPMI_exp_case$case_control_other_latest <- NULL

PPMI_exp <- PPMI_exp_case

```

### Gene filtering 

To reduce the number of genes, those presenting a minimum expression threshold in at least 80% of the samples were selected.

A series of thresholds were tested, and the one retaining fewer than 20,000 genes was chosen.

```{r eval=FALSE}

# Create a vector to store the number of columns to retain for each threshold.
num_columnas <- numeric()

# Iterate over different threshold values and store the number of columns to retain.
for (umbral in seq(0, 99, by = 1)) {
  # Calculate the percentage of high-expression samples for the current threshold.
  porcentaje_muestras_altas <- colSums(PPMI_exp > umbral) / nrow(PPMI_exp)
  
  # Retrieve the names of the columns that meet the criterion.
  columnas_a_mantener <- names(porcentaje_muestras_altas[porcentaje_muestras_altas >= 0.8 & !is.na(porcentaje_muestras_altas)])
  
  # Count the number of columns to retain.
  num_columnas <- c(num_columnas, length(columnas_a_mantener))
}

saveRDS(num_columnas, "num_columnas")

```

```{r eval=TRUE, echo=FALSE}

num_columnas <- readRDS(file.path(base_dir, "Pipeline", "num_columnas"))

```

```{r}

# Plot the number of columns to retain as a function of the threshold.
plot(seq(0, 99, by = 1), num_columnas, type = "l", xlab = "Umbral", ylab = "Número de columnas a mantener"); abline(h = num_columnas[which(seq(0, 99, by = 1) == 6)], col = "red", lty = 2, lwd = 2)

```



```{r eval=FALSE}

# Vector de umbrales
umbrales <- seq(0, 99, by = 1)

# --- Función para encontrar el codo (distancia máxima a la recta extremos)
find_elbow <- function(x, y) {
  # extremos
  x1 <- x[1]; y1 <- y[1]
  x2 <- x[length(x)]; y2 <- y[length(y)]
  
  # distancias perpendiculares de cada punto a la recta (x1,y1)-(x2,y2)
  denom <- sqrt((y2 - y1)^2 + (x2 - x1)^2)
  d <- abs((y2 - y1)*x - (x2 - x1)*y + x2*y1 - y2*x1) / denom
  
  idx <- which.max(d)
  list(index = idx, x = x[idx], y = y[idx], distancias = d)
}

# Aplicar al caso de num_columnas
res_elbow <- find_elbow(umbrales, num_columnas)

# Mostrar resultado
print(res_elbow)

# Graficar con el punto del codo marcado
plot(umbrales, num_columnas, type = "l", xlab = "Umbral", ylab = "Número de columnas")
points(res_elbow$x, res_elbow$y, col = "red", pch = 19, cex = 1.5)
abline(v = res_elbow$x, col = "red", lty = 2)



```



Genes meeting the condition are selected using a threshold value of 8.

```{r}

# A threshold value is chosen, which must be reached by at least 80% of the samples for each gene expression.
umbral <- 6

# The number of samples exceeding the threshold in each column is counted, and the corresponding percentage is calculated.
porcentaje_muestras_altas <- colSums(PPMI_exp > umbral) / nrow(PPMI_exp)

# Retrieve the names of the columns that meet the criterion.
columnas_a_mantener <- names(porcentaje_muestras_altas[porcentaje_muestras_altas >= 0.8])
columnas_a_mantener <- subset(columnas_a_mantener, columnas_a_mantener != "NA")

# saveRDS(columnas_a_mantener, "18686_genes")

# Filter the dataframe to retain only the selected columns.
PPMI_min <- PPMI_exp[, columnas_a_mantener]

PPMI_exp <- PPMI_min

dim(PPMI_exp)

```

## Gene type stratification

```{r}

# ==============================================
# 1️⃣ Preparación
# ==============================================
if (!requireNamespace("biomaRt", quietly = TRUE))
  install.packages("biomaRt")
library(biomaRt)

# Conexión a Ensembl
mart <- useMart("ensembl", dataset = "hsapiens_gene_ensembl")

# Genes de tu matriz
genes_ppmi <- colnames(PPMI_exp)

# ==============================================
# 2️⃣ Extraer todos los genes de tus pathways
# ==============================================
# Aplanar todos los genes contenidos en la lista
genes_pathways <- unique(unlist(Subsets_pathways))

# ==============================================
# 3️⃣ Encontrar los que están en ambos conjuntos
# ==============================================
genes_intersect <- intersect(genes_ppmi, genes_pathways)

# ==============================================
# 4️⃣ Consultar información de biotipo desde Ensembl
# ==============================================
gene_info <- getBM(
  attributes = c("ensembl_gene_id", "external_gene_name", "gene_biotype"),
  filters = "ensembl_gene_id",
  values = unique(c(genes_ppmi, genes_pathways)),  # todos los posibles
  mart = mart
)

colnames(gene_info)[1] <- "Gene"

# ==============================================
# 5️⃣ Añadir información de coincidencia y recuento
# ==============================================
# Marcar si el gen aparece en alguna pathway
gene_info$Found_in_pathway <- gene_info$Gene %in% genes_pathways

# Contar en cuántas pathways aparece cada gen
pathway_counts <- table(unlist(Subsets_pathways))
pathway_counts <- as.data.frame(pathway_counts)
colnames(pathway_counts) <- c("Gene", "Pathway_count")

# Combinar con la información de biotipo
gene_info <- merge(gene_info, pathway_counts, by = "Gene", all.x = TRUE)

# Rellenar los genes que no aparecen en ninguna pathway con 0
gene_info$Pathway_count[is.na(gene_info$Pathway_count)] <- 0

# Añadir si pertenece a PPMI_exp
gene_info$In_PPMI_exp <- gene_info$Gene %in% genes_ppmi

# ==============================================
# 6️⃣ Resultado
# ==============================================
# Ver resumen de biotipos de los que están en pathways
table(gene_info$gene_biotype[gene_info$Found_in_pathway])

# Vista previa de la tabla final
head(gene_info)

# ==============================================
# 7️⃣ (Opcional) Guardar en CSV
# ==============================================
write.csv(gene_info, "Gene_biotypes_PPMI_and_pathways.csv", row.names = FALSE)


table(
  gene_info$gene_biotype[
    gene_info$In_PPMI_exp & gene_info$Found_in_pathway
  ]
)

library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

# Filtrar solo los genes de interés
biotipo_tabla <- table(
  gene_info$gene_biotype[
    gene_info$In_PPMI_exp & gene_info$Found_in_pathway
  ]
)

# Convertir a data.frame para ggplot
biotipo_df <- as.data.frame(biotipo_tabla)
colnames(biotipo_df) <- c("Gene_biotype", "Count")

# Crear etiqueta para la leyenda con número incluido
biotipo_df <- biotipo_df %>%
  mutate(Legend_label = paste0(Gene_biotype, " (", Count, ")"))

# -------------------------------
# Barplot con leyenda numerada
# -------------------------------
ggplot(biotipo_df, aes(x = reorder(Gene_biotype, -Count), y = Count, fill = Legend_label)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "",
    x = "Gene biotype",
    y = "Number oof Genes",
    fill = "Biotype (n)"
  ) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))


ggplot(biotipo_df, aes(x = "", y = Count, fill = Legend_label)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 1) +
  coord_polar(theta = "y") +
  theme_void() +
  labs(title = "Proporción de biotipos en genes PPMI_exp y pathways",
       fill = "Biotype(n)")

```




## Clinical Covariates for Dendrogram Labeling

The Covariates dataset will be used to label the samples in our dendrogram. For this purpose, covariates will be selected, each of which reflects some degree of Parkinson’s disease severity.


### Usar variables desagrupadas (No Eval):
Tremor_Right_UM = "code_upd2315a_postural_tremor_of_right_hand", "code_upd2316a_kinetic_tremor_of_right_hand", "code_upd2317a_rest_tremor_amplitude_right_upper_extremity", "code_upd2317c_rest_tremor_amplitude_right_lower_extremity"

# Pathways selection {#sec:pathway}

## Human MSigDB Collections

#### Selected pathways (Bandrés et al.)

```{r eval=FALSE}

pdrisk_df <- read.csv2(file.path(base_dir, "Pipeline", "pdrisk.training.csv"), sep = ",")

pdrisk_df_test <- read.csv2(file.path(base_dir, "Pipeline", "pdrisk.replication.csv"), sep = ",")


filtered_df <- pdrisk_df[as.numeric(pdrisk_df$P) <= 0.05/2199, ]

filtered_df_test <- pdrisk_df_test[as.numeric(pdrisk_df_test$P) <= 0.05/2199, ]

pathways_bandres <- unique(c(filtered_df$Set, filtered_df_test$Set))


# Retrieve human gene sets
geneset <- msigdbr(species = "Homo sapiens")

# Filter for the specific pathway.
geneset_filter <- geneset %>% filter(gs_name %in% pathways_bandres)

pathway_description_msigdb <- geneset_filter[,c("gs_name", "gs_description", "gs_subcat")] %>% distinct()

geneset_filter$gs_description_ont <- paste(geneset_filter$gs_subcat, geneset_filter$gs_description,  sep = ":")

# Assuming that geneset_filter is your dataframe.
lista_genes <- split(geneset_filter$human_ensembl_gene, geneset_filter$gs_description_ont)

# If you wish to obtain a vector of lists.
vector_listas <- lapply(lista_genes, unique)  # opcional: si quieres solo valores únicos


Subsets_pathways <- vector_listas

# saveRDS(Subsets_pathways, "Subsets_pathways_msigdb_names")

```

# *Clustering and Pairwise Cluster Comparison

Based on the gene subsets obtained in the previous section, the following process will be performed:

- The transcriptomic data will be divided into k clusters using hierarchical clustering.

- All clusters will be compared against each other in a pairwise manner (1 vs 1) to identify significant differences in any of the progression-related covariates. Statistical tests will be applied to each pairwise comparison, and the resulting p-values will be adjusted using the Bonferroni method.

- For each significant difference, the direction of the difference will be assessed. If the difference indicates greater disease progression, a score of +1 will be assigned to the patients in the more severe cluster.

- A final score will be calculated for each patient, obtained by summing the scores resulting from all pairwise comparisons in which the patient belongs to the more severe cluster.


A dataframe will be generated with the following columns:

- Pathway name

- Cluster label and total number of clusters (k) for that iteration

- Number of samples (n) in the cluster

- Name of the significant covariate

- Patient sample ID

- Label of the cluster used as reference in the pairwise comparison

The clinical covariates used to determine patient severity are listed below (*Risk_Var*) and also are included covariates for which higher values indicate lower severity  in *Inv_Var*.

```{r}
# Grouped Tremor & PIGD
Risk_var <- c("mds_updrs_part_i_summary_score","code_upd2101_cognitive_impairment","code_upd2102_hallucinations_and_psychosis","code_upd2103_depressed_mood","code_upd2104_anxious_mood","code_upd2105_apathy","code_upd2106_dopamine_dysregulation_syndrome_features", "mds_updrs_part_ii_summary_score","mds_updrs_part_iii_summary_score","Tremor_Right_UM","Tremor_Left_UM","Tremor_all_Um","PIGD_UM", "ess_summary_score", "moca_total_score", "Abeta", "p-Tau", "Tau", "rbd_summary_score", "code_upd2hy_hoehn_and_yahr_stage", "apoe", "mapt", "AGE_ONSET", "mod_schwab_england_pct_adl_score")

# Ungrouped Tremor & PIGD
# Risk_var <- c("mds_updrs_part_i_summary_score","code_upd2101_cognitive_impairment","code_upd2102_hallucinations_and_psychosis","code_upd2103_depressed_mood","code_upd2104_anxious_mood","code_upd2105_apathy","code_upd2106_dopamine_dysregulation_syndrome_features", "mds_updrs_part_ii_summary_score","mds_updrs_part_iii_summary_score","code_upd2315a_postural_tremor_of_right_hand", "code_upd2316a_kinetic_tremor_of_right_hand", "code_upd2317a_rest_tremor_amplitude_right_upper_extremity", "code_upd2317c_rest_tremor_amplitude_right_lower_extremity", "code_upd2315b_postural_tremor_of_left_hand", "code_upd2316b_kinetic_tremor_of_left_hand", "code_upd2317b_rest_tremor_amplitude_left_upper_extremity", "code_upd2317d_rest_tremor_amplitude_left_lower_extremity","code_upd2317e_rest_tremor_amplitude_lip_or_jaw", "code_upd2318_consistency_of_rest_tremor","code_upd2310_gait", "code_upd2311_freezing_of_gait", "code_upd2312_postural_stability", "code_upd2313_posture", "ess_summary_score", "moca_total_score", "Abeta", "p-Tau", "Tau", "rbd_summary_score", "code_upd2hy_hoehn_and_yahr_stage", "apoe", "mapt", "AGE_ONSET", "mod_schwab_england_pct_adl_score")


Inv_Var <- c("moca_total_score", "mod_schwab_england_pct_adl_score", "AGE_ONSET")

```


## Severity Score Generation {#sec:score}

```{r}


dividing_data_cluster <- function(df_covariates, df_transcript, Subsets_pathways, n_clust) {

  # First loop: Iterate over the different pathways, generating the corresponding transcriptomic subsets.
  for (pathway in names(Subsets_pathways)) {
    message("Pathway: ", pathway)
    valid_columns <- Subsets_pathways[[pathway]][Subsets_pathways[[pathway]] %in% colnames(df_transcript)] # Retrieve pathways that contain genes present in our dataset.
    
    if (length(valid_columns) == 0) {
      warning("No valid columns found for pathway: ", pathway)
      next
    } 
    
    # Generate the transcriptomic subset corresponding to each pathway.    
    df_transcript_pathway <- df_transcript[, valid_columns, drop = FALSE]

    # Calculation of the correlation matrix and distances: The Pearson method will be used for expression values.
    cor.pe <- cor(t(as.matrix(df_transcript_pathway)), method = "pearson")
    DissMatrix <- as.dist(1 - cor.pe)
    
    # Generation of the dendrogram
    hclust_object <- hclust(DissMatrix, method = "ward.D2")
    
    # Get cluster assignments
    cluster_assignments <- cutree(hclust_object, k = n_clust)
    
    # 1. Silhouette Width 
    sil_width <- silhouette(cluster_assignments, DissMatrix)
    avg_sil_width <- mean(sil_width[, "sil_width"])
    
    
    # 4. Calinski-Harabasz Index (Variance Ratio Criterion)
    ch_index <- calinhara(x = as.matrix(df_transcript_pathway), 
                         clustering = cluster_assignments)
    
    # 5. Davies-Bouldin Index - lower values indicate better clustering
    db_index <- index.DB(x = as.matrix(df_transcript_pathway), 
                        cl = cluster_assignments)$DB
    
    
# The specified number of clusters (n_clust) will be generated.

    # Hierarchical clustering cut
    hclust.k <- cutree(hclust_object, k = n_clust)
    
    df_covariates$cluster <- hclust.k # Añadir columna con el cluster que corresponde al conjunto de covariables
    
    # Retrieve all the classes present in the cluster and the rest of the data.
    full_cluster <- unique(df_covariates$cluster)
    
    # Generate all ordered cluster pairs: (1 vs 0), (2 vs 1), etc.
    for (i in 1:(length(full_cluster) - 1)) {
      for (j in (i + 1):length(full_cluster)) {
        
        cluster_i <- df_covariates[hclust.k == i, ] 
        cluster_j <- df_covariates[hclust.k == j, ]
        
        variables <- setdiff(colnames(cluster_i), "cluster") # Retain only the columns that are different from "cluster".
    
        Var_Scores <- c()
        # Iterate over the cluster variables and apply statistical tests.
        for (variable in variables) {
          
          # variable <- variables[1] # For debugging
          
          num_samples <- sum(!is.na(df_covariates[[variable]])) # Nº de muestras para cada variable
        
          # If the variable is numerical, apply:
          if (is.numeric(df_covariates[[variable]])) {
          
            # cat(variable, "num", "\n")
            
            # Mann-Whitney test
            num_test_result <- wilcox.test(cluster_i[[variable]], cluster_j[[variable]], exact=min(length(cluster_i[[variable]]),length(cluster_j[[variable]])) < 20)
    
          # Adjust the p-value using Bonferroni.
          num_test_result$p.value <- p.adjust(num_test_result$p.value, method = "bonferroni", n = n_clust * length(variables))
          
      
          # Median Check to Add Score
          if (!is.na(num_test_result$p.value) && num_test_result$p.value <= 0.05 && variable %in% Risk_var) {
            # If the clinical covariate has an inverse relationship with severity:
            if (variable %in% Inv_Var) {
              if (median(cluster_i[[variable]]) > median(cluster_j[[variable]])) {

                # cat("Severity Score +1: ", pathway, " ", j, "/", n_clust, "n = ", nrow(cluster_j), variable, "VS: Cluster", i, "\n")
                
                # sev_score_mod <- abs(median(cluster_i[[variable]]) - median(cluster_j[[variable]])) / abs(median(cluster_i[[variable]])) + abs(median(cluster_j[[variable]])) # Toni: Entre 0 y 1
                sev_score_mod <- abs(median(cluster_i[[variable]]) - median(cluster_j[[variable]])) / max(median(cluster_i[[variable]]), median(cluster_j[[variable]]))
                
                row <- data.frame(
                  Pathway = rep(pathway, nrow(cluster_j)),
                  Cluster = rep(j, nrow(cluster_j)),
                  k = rep(n_clust, nrow(cluster_j)),
                  n = rep(nrow(cluster_j), nrow(cluster_j)),
                  Covariate = rep(variable, nrow(cluster_j)),
                  sample_id = rownames(cluster_j),
                  p_value = rep(num_test_result$p.value, nrow(cluster_j)),
                  VS = rep(i, nrow(cluster_j)),
                  SevScore = 1,
                  SevScore_mod = sev_score_mod,
                  avg_sil_width = avg_sil_width,
                  ch_index = ch_index,
                  db_index = db_index                  
                )
                            
                Sev_score_detail <- rbind(Sev_score_detail, row)
              }
            } else { # In the case that the relationship between the value of clinical covariate and the severity is direct, use "<".
                
                # cat("Severity Score +1: ", pathway, " ", i, "/", n_clust, "n = ", nrow(cluster_i), variable, "VS: Cluster", j, "\n")
                

                sev_score_mod <- abs(median(cluster_i[[variable]]) - median(cluster_j[[variable]])) / max(median(cluster_i[[variable]]), median(cluster_j[[variable]]))
                
                row <- data.frame(
                  Pathway = rep(pathway, nrow(cluster_i)),
                  Cluster = rep(i, nrow(cluster_i)),
                  k = rep(n_clust, nrow(cluster_i)),
                  n = rep(nrow(cluster_i), nrow(cluster_i)),
                  Covariate = rep(variable, nrow(cluster_i)),
                  sample_id = rownames(cluster_i),
                  p_value = rep(num_test_result$p.value, nrow(cluster_i)),
                  VS = rep(j, nrow(cluster_i)),
                  SevScore = 1,
                  SevScore_mod = sev_score_mod,
                  avg_sil_width = avg_sil_width,
                  ch_index = ch_index,
                  db_index = db_index    
                )
                
                Sev_score_detail <- rbind(Sev_score_detail, row)
                
                # assign("Sev_score_detail", Sev_score_detail, envir = .GlobalEnv)
              
            }
          }
      
      
          # If the variable is a factor, apply:
          } else if (is.factor(df_covariates[[variable]])) {
            full_classes <- union(levels(df_covariates[[variable]]), unique(c(cluster_i[[variable]], cluster_j[[variable]])))
            full_classes <- sort(as.character(full_classes))
            
            for (upper_class in full_classes) {
              upper_numeric <- as.numeric(upper_class)
              
              # Filtrar clases inferiores
              lower_classes <- full_classes[as.numeric(full_classes) < upper_numeric]
              
              if (length(lower_classes) == 0) next
              
              numerador <- 0
              denominador <- upper_numeric - min(as.numeric(lower_classes))
              
              for (lower_class in lower_classes) {
                cluster_i_name <- paste0("Cluster_", i)
                cluster_j_name <- paste0("Cluster_", j)
                
                # Crear tabla 2x2
                tab <- matrix(0, nrow = 2, ncol = 2,
                              dimnames = list(
                                Clase = c(lower_class, upper_class),
                                Grupo = c(cluster_i_name, cluster_j_name)
                              ))
                
                tab[lower_class, cluster_i_name] <- sum(cluster_i[[variable]] == lower_class, na.rm = TRUE)
                tab[upper_class, cluster_i_name] <- sum(cluster_i[[variable]] == upper_class, na.rm = TRUE)
                tab[lower_class, cluster_j_name] <- sum(cluster_j[[variable]] == lower_class, na.rm = TRUE)
                tab[upper_class, cluster_j_name] <- sum(cluster_j[[variable]] == upper_class, na.rm = TRUE)
                
                if (all(rowSums(tab) > 0)) {
                  test_to_use <- if (any(chisq.test(tab)$expected < 5)) fisher.test else chisq.test
                  result <- test_to_use(tab)
                  
                  result$p.value <- p.adjust(result$p.value, method = "bonferroni", n = n_clust * length(variables))
                  
                  prop_upper_cluster_i <- tab[upper_class, cluster_i_name] / sum(tab[, cluster_i_name])
                  prop_upper_cluster_j <- tab[upper_class, cluster_j_name] / sum(tab[, cluster_j_name])
                  
                  if (result$p.value <= 0.05) {
                    if (prop_upper_cluster_i > prop_upper_cluster_j) {
                      numerador <- numerador + 1
                      target_cluster <- cluster_i
                      target_index <- i
                      target_vs <- j
                    } else if (prop_upper_cluster_j > prop_upper_cluster_i) {
                      numerador <- numerador + 1
                      target_cluster <- cluster_j
                      target_index <- j
                      target_vs <- i
                    }
                  }
                }
              }
              
              if (numerador > 0 && denominador > 0) {
                sev_score_mod <- numerador / denominador
                row <- data.frame(
                  Pathway = rep(pathway, nrow(target_cluster)),
                  Cluster = rep(target_index, nrow(target_cluster)),
                  k = rep(n_clust, nrow(target_cluster)),
                  n = rep(nrow(target_cluster), nrow(target_cluster)),
                  Covariate = rep(variable, nrow(target_cluster)),
                  sample_id = rownames(target_cluster),
                  p_value = rep(NA, nrow(target_cluster)),  # opcional: puedes registrar los p-values individuales si quieres
                  VS = rep(target_vs, nrow(target_cluster)),
                  SevScore = rep(1, nrow(target_cluster)),
                  SevScore_mod = rep(sev_score_mod, nrow(target_cluster)),
                  avg_sil_width = avg_sil_width,
                  ch_index = ch_index,
                  db_index = db_index    
                )
                Sev_score_detail <- rbind(Sev_score_detail, row)
              }
            }
            
            # assign("Sev_score_detail", Sev_score_detail, envir = .GlobalEnv)
          }


                    }  # <- if (all(rowSums(tab) > 0))
                  }  # <- for (j in ...)
                }  # <- for (i in ...)
              }  # <- else if (is.factor(...))
  assign("Sev_score_detail", Sev_score_detail, envir = .GlobalEnv)
            }  # <- for (variable in variables)



```

## Set as Categorical Variables (Options)

Clinical covariates representing specific symptoms are set as categorical.
The evolution of their levels over the visits is also displayed in a barplot.

```{r}

# Convert specific variables to factors
cols_to_factor <- c("Tremor_Right_UM", "Tremor_Left_UM", "Tremor_all_Um", "PIGD_UM")
PPMI_label[cols_to_factor] <- lapply(PPMI_label[cols_to_factor], as.factor)

# Verify the conversion
sapply(PPMI_label[cols_to_factor], function(x) is.factor(x))


# Set columns starting with "code" to factors
PPMI_label[, grep("^code", names(PPMI_label))] <- lapply(
  PPMI_label[, grep("^code", names(PPMI_label))],
  as.factor
)

sapply(PPMI_label[colnames(PPMI_label[, grep("^code", names(PPMI_label))])], function(x) is.factor(x) && "0" %in% levels(x))


# Select the factor columns that start with "code_".
factor_vars <- names(PPMI_label)[sapply(PPMI_label, is.factor)] 



# Pivot the data to long format.
PPMI_long_factors <- PPMI_label %>%
  dplyr::select(all_of(factor_vars), visit_name) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = all_of(factor_vars), names_to = "variable", values_to = "value")


```



## *Comprobando variación de las covariables por visita

```{r eval=FALSE}


# --- Separar variables ---
cols <- colnames(PPMI_label)
cols_to_plot <- cols[2:length(cols)]  # desde la segunda a la última columna

# --- Generar gráficos para variables numéricas ---
numeric_vars <- PPMI_label %>%
  dplyr::select(all_of(cols_to_plot), visit_name) %>%
  dplyr::select(where(is.numeric), visit_name) %>%
  pivot_longer(-visit_name, names_to = "variable", values_to = "value")

num_vars_list <- unique(numeric_vars$variable)

num_vars_list <- colnames(Covariates_score[, c(Motor, Non_Motor)])

numeric_vars <- Covariates_score %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = all_of(c(Motor, Non_Motor)),
               names_to = "variable",
               values_to = "value")

for (var in num_vars_list) {
  df_plot <- numeric_vars %>%
    filter(variable == var) %>%
    group_by(visit_name) %>%
    summarise(desviacion = sd(value, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")
  
  p <- ggplot(df_plot, aes(x = visit_name, y = desviacion, group = 1)) +
    geom_line(color = "steelblue") +
    geom_point(size = 2) +
    labs(title = paste("Desviación típica por visita:", var),
         x = "Visita", y = "Desviación típica") +
    theme_minimal()
  
  print(p)
  # ggsave(paste0("varianza_", var, ".png"), plot = p, width = 6, height = 4)
}

# --- Generar gráficos para variables categóricas ---
categorical_vars <- PPMI_label %>%
  dplyr::select(all_of(cols_to_plot), visit_name) %>%
  dplyr::select(where(~is.factor(.) || is.character(.)), visit_name) %>%
  pivot_longer(-visit_name, names_to = "variable", values_to = "value")

cat_vars_list <- unique(categorical_vars$variable)

for (var in cat_vars_list) {
  df_plot <- categorical_vars %>%
    filter(variable == var)
  
  p <- ggplot(df_plot, aes(x = fct_infreq(value))) +
    geom_bar(fill = "darkorange") +
    facet_wrap(~ visit_name, scales = "free_x") +
    labs(title = paste("Distribución por visita:", var),
         x = "Categoría", y = "Frecuencia") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
  
  print(p)
  # ggsave(paste0("distribucion_", var, ".png"), plot = p, width = 8, height = 4)
}



```

### Random Gene-sets severity score comparison

```{r eval=FALSE}


# Función para contar combinaciones únicas
contar_combinaciones_unicas <- function(df) {
  if (!is.null(df)) {
    df %>%
      distinct(Pathway, Cluster, k, Covariate) %>%
      nrow()
  } else {
    0
  }
}




# Timepoints
timepoints <- c("M0", "M12", "M24", "M36")


# Función mejorada para cargar múltiples iteraciones random
cargar_iteraciones_random <- function(timepoint) {
  # Listar todos los archivos en el directorio
  todos_archivos <- list.files(base_dir, full.names = TRUE)
  
  # Construir el patrón de expresión regular
  patron <- paste0("^Sev_score_detail_", timepoint, "_fake_pathways")
  
  # Filtrar los archivos que coinciden con el patrón
  archivos <- todos_archivos[grepl(patron, basename(todos_archivos))]
  
  if (length(archivos) == 0) {
    warning(paste("No se encontraron archivos para", timepoint, "con patrón:", patron))
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  # Leer todos los archivos y calcular combinaciones únicas
  resultados <- sapply(archivos, function(archivo) {
    df <- readRDS(archivo)
    contar_combinaciones_unicas(df)
  })
  
  return(resultados)
}

# Cargar datos originales (ajusta estos nombres según tus archivos reales)
datos_originales <- list(
  M0 = Sev_score_detail_M0,
  M12 = Sev_score_detail_M12,
  M24 = Sev_score_detail_M24,
  M36 = Sev_score_detail_M36
)

# Calcular combinaciones únicas para originales
conteo_originales <- sapply(datos_originales, contar_combinaciones_unicas)

# Calcular estadísticas para datos random
estadisticas_random <- lapply(timepoints, function(tp) {
  iteraciones <- cargar_iteraciones_random(tp)
  if (is.null(iteraciones)) return(c(mean = 0, sd = 0, n = 0))
  
  c(
    mean = mean(iteraciones),
    sd = sd(iteraciones),
    n = length(iteraciones)
  )
})

names(estadisticas_random) <- timepoints

# Crear dataframe para ggplot
conteo_largo <- data.frame(
  Timepoint = rep(timepoints, 2),
  Tipo = rep(c("Original", "Random"), each = length(timepoints)),
  Valor = c(conteo_originales, sapply(estadisticas_random, function(x) x["mean"])),
  SD = c(rep(0, length(timepoints)), sapply(estadisticas_random, function(x) x["sd"]))
)

# Gráfico de barras con error bars
p_barras <- ggplot(conteo_largo, aes(x = Timepoint, y = Valor, fill = Tipo)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(), alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = Valor - SD, ymax = Valor + SD),
                position = position_dodge(0.9), width = 0.25) +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(Valor, 1)), 
            position = position_dodge(width = 0.9), 
            vjust = -0.5, 
            size = 3.5) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Original" = "#2E86AB", "Random" = "#A23B72"),
                    labels = c("Original" = "PD Gene-Sets", "Random" = "Random Gene-Sets")) +
  labs(title = "Clinical Covariate Signals in Transcriptomic Clusters",
       subtitle = "PD-associated vs. random gene sets comparison",
       x = "Time Point",
       y = "Significant Clinical Signals",
       fill = "") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 14),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 12),
        axis.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),
        axis.text = element_text(size = 11),
        legend.text = element_text(size = 11),
        legend.position = "bottom")


# Alternativa en PNG (para revisión)
ggsave("clinical_signals_comparison.png",
       plot = p_barras,
       device = "png",
       width = 8,
       height = 6,
       dpi = 300,
       bg = "white")



# Gráfico de líneas para tendencias
p_lineas <- ggplot(conteo_largo, aes(x = Timepoint, y = Valor, color = Tipo, group = Tipo)) +
  geom_line(size = 1.5) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  # Para los puntos random, añadimos barras de error
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = Valor - SD, ymax = Valor + SD), 
                width = 0.2, 
                alpha = 0.7,
                data = subset(conteo_largo, Tipo == "Random")) +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(Valor, 1)), 
            vjust = -1, 
            size = 3.5) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Original" = "#2E86AB", "Random" = "#A23B72"),
                    labels = c("Original" = "PD Gene-Sets", "Random" = "Random Gene-Sets")) +
  labs(title = "Clinical Covariate Signals in Transcriptomic Clusters",
       subtitle = "PD-associated vs. random gene sets comparison",
       x = "Time Point",
       y = "Significant Clinical Signals",
       color = "") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 14),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 12),
        axis.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),
        axis.text = element_text(size = 11),
        legend.text = element_text(size = 11),
        legend.position = "bottom")


ggsave("clinical_signals_comparison_lines.png",
       plot = p_lineas,
       device = "png",
       width = 8,
       height = 6,
       dpi = 300,
       bg = "white")


# Mostrar ambos gráficos
print(p_barras)
print(p_lineas)



# Mostrar la tabla de conteos
cat("Combinaciones únicas por timepoint:\n")
print(data.frame(
  Timepoint = timepoints,
  Original = conteo_originales,
  Random_Mean = sapply(estadisticas_random, function(x) x["mean"]),
  Random_SD = sapply(estadisticas_random, function(x) x["sd"]),
  N_Iterations = sapply(estadisticas_random, function(x) x["n"])
))

# Calcular y mostrar estadísticas adicionales
cat("\n--- ESTADÍSTICAS ADICIONALES ---\n")
diferencias <- conteo_originales - sapply(estadisticas_random, function(x) x["mean"])
cat("Diferencia promedio entre Original y Random:", mean(diferencias), "\n")
cat("Porcentaje de diferencia promedio:", mean(diferencias / conteo_originales * 100), "%\n")


# Mostrar resultados en formato Original/Random para cada timepoint
cat("Resultados por timepoint (Original/Random):\n")
for (i in seq_along(timepoints)) {
  random_mean <- round(sapply(estadisticas_random, function(x) x["mean"])[i])
  cat(paste0(timepoints[i], ": ", conteo_originales[i], "/", random_mean, 
             " (Fold: ", round(conteo_originales[i]/random_mean, 2), ")\n"))
}

# Calcular y mostrar el fold change promedio
fold_changes <- conteo_originales / sapply(estadisticas_random, function(x) x["mean"])
cat("\nFold change promedio:", round(mean(fold_changes), 2), "\n")


```


### Densidad Sev Score visitas

```{r}

# Lista con tus dataframes y etiquetas
datasets <- list(
  M0  = Sev_score_detail_M0,
  M12 = Sev_score_detail_M12,
  M24 = Sev_score_detail_M24,
  M36 = Sev_score_detail_M36
)

# Procesar: sumar SevScore por paciente y añadir columna de tiempo
df_all <- bind_rows(
  lapply(names(datasets), function(name) {
    datasets[[name]] %>%
      group_by(sample_id) %>%
      summarise(SeverityScore = sum(SevScore, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
      mutate(Time = name)
  })
)

# Gráfico de densidad
p <- ggplot(df_all, aes(x = SeverityScore, fill = Time, color = Time)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.3, size = 1) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 16) +
  labs(
    x = "Severity Score (sum per patient)",
    y = "Density",
    title = "Distribution of Severity Score across visits"
  ) +
  theme(
    legend.title = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold")
  )

# # Guardar en PNG de alta calidad
# ggsave("SeverityScore_Density.png", p,
#        width = 10, height = 7, dpi = 300)
# 
# 
# 





# Definición de covariables

Motor <- c("code_upd2hy_hoehn_and_yahr_stage", 
           "mds_updrs_part_ii_summary_score",
           "mds_updrs_part_iii_summary_score", 
           "Tremor_Right_UM", 
           "Tremor_Left_UM",
           "Tremor_all_Um", 
           "PIGD_UM",
           "mod_schwab_england_pct_adl_score")

Non_Motor <- c("code_upd2106_dopamine_dysregulation_syndrome_features",
               "mds_updrs_part_i_summary_score", 
               "code_upd2102_hallucinations_and_psychosis",
               "code_upd2101_cognitive_impairment", 
               "code_upd2103_depressed_mood",
               "code_upd2104_anxious_mood", 
               "code_upd2105_apathy",
               "rbd_summary_score", 
               "ess_summary_score",
               "moca_total_score")

Non_Motor_Overall <- "mds_updrs_part_i_summary_score"

Cognitive <- c("code_upd2101_cognitive_impairment", 
               "moca_total_score")


Neuro <- c("code_upd2106_dopamine_dysregulation_syndrome_features",
           "code_upd2102_hallucinations_and_psychosis",
           "code_upd2101_cognitive_impairment", 
           "code_upd2103_depressed_mood",
           "code_upd2104_anxious_mood", 
           "code_upd2105_apathy")


Neuro_Cogn <- c(Cognitive, Neuro)


Sleep <- c("rbd_summary_score", 
           "ess_summary_score")


# Lista con datasets
datasets <- list(
  M0  = Sev_score_detail_M0,
  M12 = Sev_score_detail_M12,
  M24 = Sev_score_detail_M24,
  M36 = Sev_score_detail_M36
)

# Función para preparar datos (según grupo de covariables)
prep_data <- function(covariates, label){
  bind_rows(
    lapply(names(datasets), function(name) {
      datasets[[name]] %>%
        filter(Covariate %in% covariates) %>%
        group_by(sample_id) %>%
        summarise(SeverityScore = sum(SevScore, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
        mutate(Time = name, Group = label)
    })
  )
}

# Datos motor y no-motor
df_motor     <- prep_data(Motor, "Motor")
df_non_motor <- prep_data(Non_Motor, "Non_Motor")

# --- GRÁFICO MOTOR ---
p_motor <- ggplot(df_motor, aes(x = SeverityScore, fill = Time, color = Time)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.3, size = 1) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 16) +
  labs(
    x = "Severity Score (Motor, sum per patient)",
    y = "Density",
    title = "Distribution of Motor Severity Score across visits"
  ) +
  theme(
    legend.title = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold")
  )

ggsave("SeverityScore_Density_Motor.png", p_motor,
       width = 10, height = 7, dpi = 300)


p_motor_log <- ggplot(df_motor, aes(x = SeverityScore, fill = Time, color = Time)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.3, size = 1) +
  scale_x_log10() +  # Transformación logarítmica
  theme_minimal(base_size = 16) +
  labs(
    x = "Severity Score (Motor, sum per patient) - Log scale",
    y = "Density",
    title = "Distribution of Motor Severity Score across visits"
  ) +
  theme(
    legend.title = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold")
  )



# --- GRÁFICO MOTOR (limite x hasta 200) ---
p_motor_limited <- ggplot(df_motor, aes(x = SeverityScore, fill = Time, color = Time)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.3, size = 1) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 200)) +  # Limitar el eje x hasta 200
  theme_minimal(base_size = 16) +
  labs(
    x = "Severity Score (Motor, sum per patient) - limited to 200",
    y = "Density",
    title = "Distribution of Motor Severity Score across visits (up to 200)"
  ) +
  theme(
    legend.title = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold")
  )


p_motor
p_motor_log
p_motor_limited

# --- GRÁFICO NON-MOTOR ---
p_non_motor <- ggplot(df_non_motor, aes(x = SeverityScore, fill = Time, color = Time)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.3, size = 1) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 16) +
  labs(
    x = "Severity Score (Non-Motor, sum per patient)",
    y = "Density",
    title = "Distribution of Non-Motor Severity Score across visits"
  ) +
  theme(
    legend.title = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold")
  )

#        width = 10, height = 7, dpi = 300)
```

### Filtrando por calidad de los clusters

```{r eval=FALSE}

# Función para filtrar clusters de calidad
filtrar_clusters_calidad <- function(df) {
  require(dplyr)
  
  # Calcular percentiles para CH index (ya que no tiene umbral absoluto)
  ch_threshold_75 <- quantile(df$ch_index, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE)
  ch_threshold_90 <- quantile(df$ch_index, 0.90, na.rm = TRUE)
  
  # Aplicar filtros
  df_filtrado <- df %>%
    filter(
      avg_sil_width > 0.25,                # Mínimo aceptable
      ch_index > ch_threshold_75,          # Buen CH index
      db_index < 1                         # Buen DB index
    ) %>%
    mutate(
      calidad_cluster = case_when(
        avg_sil_width > 0.5 & ch_index > ch_threshold_90 & db_index < 0.5 ~ "Excelente",
        avg_sil_width > 0.5 & db_index < 0.75 ~ "Muy bueno",
        TRUE ~ "Bueno"
      )
    ) %>%
    arrange(desc(avg_sil_width), desc(ch_index), db_index)
  
  return(df_filtrado)
}


filtrar_clusters_calidad <- function(df) {
  
  # Aplicar filtros
  df_filtrado <- df %>%
    filter(
      avg_sil_width > 0.25,                # Mínimo aceptable
     )
  return(df_filtrado)
}

# Aplicar a cada momento temporal
Sev_score_detail_M0_filtrado <- filtrar_clusters_calidad(Sev_score_detail_M0)
Sev_score_detail_M12_filtrado <- filtrar_clusters_calidad(Sev_score_detail_M12)
Sev_score_detail_M24_filtrado <- filtrar_clusters_calidad(Sev_score_detail_M24)
Sev_score_detail_M36_filtrado <- filtrar_clusters_calidad(Sev_score_detail_M36)


Sev_score_detail_M0 <- Sev_score_detail_M0_filtrado
Sev_score_detail_M12 <- Sev_score_detail_M12_filtrado
Sev_score_detail_M24 <- Sev_score_detail_M24_filtrado
Sev_score_detail_M36 <- Sev_score_detail_M36_filtrado




```



Here we can see the severity score distribution in the different visits.

```{r}


# Añadir columna de tiempo a cada dataframe
Sev_score_detail_M0$Visit <- "M0"
Sev_score_detail_M12$Visit <- "M12"
Sev_score_detail_M24$Visit <- "M24"
Sev_score_detail_M36$Visit <- "M36"

# Unir todos en un solo dataframe
combined <- bind_rows(
  Sev_score_detail_M0,
  Sev_score_detail_M12,
  Sev_score_detail_M24,
  Sev_score_detail_M36
)

# Crear boxplot
ggplot(combined, aes(x = Visit, y = SevScore_mod, fill = Visit)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7, outlier.shape = NA) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.2, alpha = 0.3) +  # opcional: muestra puntos individuales
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Distribución de SevScore_mod por visita",
       x = "Visita",
       y = "SevScore_mod") +
  theme(legend.position = "none")


ggplot(combined, aes(x = Visit, y = SevScore_mod, fill = Visit)) +
  geom_violin(trim = FALSE, alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.1, fill = "white", outlier.shape = NA) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Distribución de SevScore_mod por visita (Violin plot)",
       x = "Visita",
       y = "SevScore_mod") +
  theme(legend.position = "none")


```






# Pathway Gene-set variation among visits


<!-- ## Valor de k? -->

```{r eval=FALSE}


# Añadir columna de tiempo a cada dataframe
Sev_score_detail_M0$Visit <- "M0"
Sev_score_detail_M12$Visit <- "M12"
Sev_score_detail_M24$Visit <- "M24"
Sev_score_detail_M36$Visit <- "M36"

# Unir todos los dataframes
Sev_score_all <- bind_rows(
  Sev_score_detail_M0,
  Sev_score_detail_M12,
  Sev_score_detail_M24,
  Sev_score_detail_M36
)

# Calcular número de señales únicas por k y por Visit
signals_per_k <- Sev_score_all %>%
  distinct(Visit, k, Pathway, Covariate) %>%
  count(Visit, k, name = "num_signals")

# Obtener todos los valores únicos de k
all_k_values <- sort(unique(Sev_score_all$k))

# Asegurarse de que todos los valores de k estén presentes para cada Visit
signals_per_k <- signals_per_k %>%
  complete(Visit, k = all_k_values, fill = list(num_signals = 0))

# Convertir k a numérico para que la línea sea continua
signals_per_k$k <- as.numeric(as.character(signals_per_k$k))

# Crear gráfico con una línea por Visit
ggplot(signals_per_k, aes(x = k, y = num_signals, color = Visit)) +
  geom_line(size = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  labs(
    title = "Evolución del número de señales según k por visita (Case)",
    x = "k",
    y = "Número de señales (Pathway-Covariate únicas)",
    color = "Visit"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

<!-- A partir de los datos obtenidos, podemos filtrar las señales utilizando los siguientes criterios: -->

<!-- - Señales únicas para cada combinación de Pathway y Covariate: Para cada combinación de Pathway y Covariate, se considera solo una señal única por paciente. Esto significa que, aunque un mismo Pathway puede contribuir con múltiples señales, estas señales deben estar asociadas a distintas covariables clínicas para ser contadas de forma independiente. -->

<!-- - Filtrado por consistencia de participación en clusters: Para asegurar que solo los pacientes que forman parte de los clusters de manera consistente acumulen un severity score para una señal concreta, se aplica un filtro basado en el percentil de las apariciones. Solo los pacientes que estén por encima de un umbral específico de aparición (por ejemplo, el cuartil 3 de las apariciones totales) serán considerados en el cálculo del severity score para esa señal. Esto permite identificar a aquellos pacientes cuya participación en los clusters no es fortuita, sino consistente. -->

<!-- ``` -->



# *Generation of Motor and Non-Motor Severity Components (Options)

Here we calculate the motor and non-motor severity scores based on the signals obtained in the previous section. We begin by generating partial scores (one per covariate), which are then grouped according to the nature of the covariate into motor and non-motor categories.


```{r}
 
create_score_summary <- function(Sev_score_detail_MSigDB, PPMI_label, visit_label) {

  # Convert rownames to the "sample_id" column
  PPMI_label <- PPMI_label %>%
    tibble::rownames_to_column(var = "sample_id")

  # Extract sample_ids and visit_name for the specific visit
  sample_ids_visit <- PPMI_label %>%
    filter(visit_name == visit_label) %>%
    dplyr::select(sample_id, visit_name)

  # ---- Sumar SevScore (original) por sample_id y Covariate ----
  score_orig <- Sev_score_detail_MSigDB %>%
    group_by(sample_id, Covariate) %>%
    summarise(score_sum = sum(SevScore, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
    pivot_wider(
      names_from = Covariate,
      values_from = score_sum,
      names_prefix = "Score_",
      values_fill = list(score_sum = 0)
    )

  # ---- Sumar SevScore_mod (modificado) por sample_id y Covariate ----
  score_mod <- Sev_score_detail_MSigDB %>%
    group_by(sample_id, Covariate) %>%
    summarise(score_mod_sum = sum(SevScore_mod, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
    pivot_wider(
      names_from = Covariate,
      values_from = score_mod_sum,
      names_prefix = "ScoreMod_",
      values_fill = list(score_mod_sum = 0)
    )

  # ---- Unir ambos y calcular Totales ----
  Sev_score_summary <- full_join(score_orig, score_mod, by = "sample_id") %>%
    rowwise() %>%
    mutate(
      Total_Score = sum(c_across(starts_with("Score_") & !starts_with("ScoreMod_")), na.rm = TRUE),
      Total_ScoreMod = sum(c_across(starts_with("ScoreMod_")), na.rm = TRUE)
    ) %>%
    ungroup()

  # ---- Añadir pacientes sin score y rellenar con ceros ----
  full_score_summary <- sample_ids_visit %>%
    left_join(Sev_score_summary, by = "sample_id") %>%
    mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~replace_na(., 0)))

  return(full_score_summary)
}
```

## *Visualization of Clinical Signal Results

Bar plots represent the percentage contribution of each covariate within the signal set.

The percentage of signals for each visit is displayed, helping to more clearly distinguish the differences within visits. The absolute value of the severity score per covariate is also shown.

Finally, the percentage of total signals across all visits is presented, allowing for the identification of the most important clinical covariates overall.


```{r}

# Función para calcular el número de señales por covariable y visita
calcular_señales_por_visita <- function(lista_datos, nombres_visitas) {
  señales_visitas <- list()
  
  for (i in seq_along(lista_datos)) {
    datos_visita <- lista_datos[[i]]
    visita <- nombres_visitas[i]
    
    señales <- datos_visita %>%
      distinct(Pathway, Covariate, Cluster, k) %>%
      group_by(Covariate) %>%
      summarise(Value = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
      mutate(Visit = visita)
    
    señales_visitas[[i]] <- señales
  }
  
  return(bind_rows(señales_visitas))
}

# Función para crear el gráfico de barras
crear_grafico_barras_señales <- function(data_completo, tipo_covariables, 
                                        color_palette = NULL, titulo = "") {
  
  # Filtrar por tipo de covariables
  if (tipo_covariables == "Motor") {
    data_filtrado <- data_completo %>% filter(Covariate %in% Motor)
  } else if (tipo_covariables == "Non-Motor") {
    data_filtrado <- data_completo %>% filter(Covariate %in% Non_Motor)
  } else {
    stop("tipo_covariables debe ser 'Motor' o 'Non-Motor'")
  }
  
  # Crear paleta de colores si no se proporciona
  if (is.null(color_palette)) {
    n_colores <- length(unique(data_filtrado$Covariate))
    color_palette <- scales::hue_pal()(n_colores)
    names(color_palette) <- unique(data_filtrado$Covariate)
  }
  
  # Crear el gráfico
  p <- ggplot(data_filtrado, aes(x = as.factor(Visit), y = Value, fill = Covariate)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), width = 0.7) +
    labs(
      title = titulo,
      x = "Visit",
      y = "Number of Signals",
      fill = "Covariate"
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
    theme(
      legend.position = "bottom",
      legend.title = element_blank(),
      legend.text = element_text(size = 8),
      axis.text.x = element_text(size = 10),
      panel.spacing = unit(1, "lines")
    ) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = color_palette) +
    geom_text(
      aes(label = Value),
      position = position_dodge(width = 0.8),
      vjust = -0.3,
      size = 3
    ) +
    ylim(0, max(data_filtrado$Value) * 1.1) # Ajustar límites para las etiquetas
  
  return(p)
}

# Preparar los datos para todas las visitas
lista_datos_visitas <- list(
  Sev_score_detail_M0,
  Sev_score_detail_M12,

# Calcular señales para todas las visitas
señales_todas_visitas <- calcular_señales_por_visita(lista_datos_visitas, nombres_visitas)

# Crear los gráficos
p_motor <- crear_grafico_barras_señales(
  data_completo = señales_todas_visitas,
  tipo_covariables = "Motor",
  titulo = "Number of Signals per Motor Covariate across Visits"
)

p_non_motor <- crear_grafico_barras_señales(
  data_completo = señales_todas_visitas,
  tipo_covariables = "Non-Motor", 
  titulo = "Number of Signals per Non-Motor Covariate across Visits"
)
```

```{r}

combined_numeric <- combined_severity_score_all_visits %>%
  dplyr::select(Visit, where(is.numeric)) %>%
  mutate(participant_id = NULL, Total_Score = NULL) %>%
  dplyr::select(-Motor_Score_mod, -Motor_Score_raw, -Non_Motor_Score_mod, -Non_Motor_Score_raw)  # Exclude Specific Variables

# Función para limpiar nombres de variables
clean_variable_names <- function(name) {
  name <- gsub("code", "", name)          # Remove "Score_code".
  name <- gsub("Score_", "", name)              # Remove "Score_".
  name <- gsub("ScoreMod_", "", name)           # Remove "ScoreMod_".
  name <- gsub("upd[a-zA-Z0-9]+_", "", name)  
  name <- gsub("_", " ", name)                  # Replace "_" with spaces.
  return(name)
}

# Configuración de colores (movido al inicio)
set3_colors <- RColorBrewer::brewer.pal(12, "Set3")
additional_colors <- "#E69F00"

required_colors <- 22
current_colors <- length(c(set3_colors, additional_colors))
needed_colors <- required_colors - current_colors

new_colors <- colorspace::rainbow_hcl(needed_colors)
combined_colors <- c(set3_colors, additional_colors, new_colors)

# Procesar datos Score_ y ScoreMod_ por separado
process_score_data <- function(data, prefix) {
  # Sumar los valores para cada visita
  summarized_data <- data %>%
    group_by(Visit) %>%
    summarise(across(starts_with(prefix), sum, na.rm = TRUE))
  
  # Convertir a formato largo y limpiar nombres
  combined_long <- summarized_data %>%
    pivot_longer(cols = -Visit, names_to = "Variable", values_to = "Value") %>%
    mutate(Category = case_when(
      Variable %in% Non_Motor ~ "Non-Motor",
      Variable %in% Motor ~ "Motor",
      TRUE ~ "Other"
    )) %>%
    mutate(Variable = sapply(Variable, clean_variable_names))
  
  # Calcular totales por categoría
  category_totals <- combined_long %>%
    group_by(Category) %>%
    summarise(Total_Category_Value = sum(Value, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")
  
  # Unir con los datos originales
  combined_long <- combined_long %>%
    left_join(category_totals, by = "Category")
  
  # Calcular porcentajes
  combined_long <- combined_long %>%
    group_by(Visit, Category) %>%
    mutate(Percentage = (Value / Total_Category_Value) * 100) %>%
    ungroup()
  
  combined_long <- combined_long %>%
    group_by(Visit, Category) %>%
    mutate(RelativePercentage = Percentage / sum(Percentage) * 100)
  
  # Ordenar variables por porcentaje
  combined_long <- combined_long %>%
    group_by(Visit) %>%
    mutate(Variable = factor(Variable, levels = unique(Variable[order(-RelativePercentage)]))) %>%
    ungroup()
  
  return(combined_long)
}

# Función para crear gráficos (ahora recibe combined_colors como parámetro)
create_plots <- function(data, prefix_name, color_palette) {
  # Gráfico de porcentaje relativo
  p_rel <- ggplot(data, aes(x = as.factor(Visit), y = RelativePercentage, fill = Variable)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), width = 0.7) +
    facet_grid(~ Category, scales = "free_x", space = "free") +
    labs(
      title = paste("Relative Contribution of Each", prefix_name, "Covariable per Visit"),
      x = "Visit",
      y = "Contribution to Score in % in a particular visit",
      fill = NULL
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
    theme(
      legend.position = "bottom",
      legend.title = element_blank(),
      legend.text = element_text(size = 5),
      axis.text.x = element_text(size = 9),
    ) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = color_palette) +
    geom_text(
      aes(label = round(RelativePercentage, 1)),
      position = position_dodge(width = 0.8),
      vjust = -0.3,
      size = 2
    )
  
  # Gráfico de valores absolutos
  p_abs <- ggplot(data, aes(x = as.factor(Visit), y = Value, fill = Variable)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), width = 0.7) +
    facet_grid(~ Category, scales = "free_x", space = "free") +
    labs(
      title = paste("Absolute Contribution of Each", prefix_name, "Covariable per Visit"),
      x = "Visit",
      y = "Absolute Contribution to Score",
      fill = NULL
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
    theme(
      legend.position = "bottom",
      legend.title = element_blank(),
      legend.text = element_text(size = 5),
      axis.text.x = element_text(size = 9),
    ) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = color_palette) +
    geom_text(
      aes(label = round(Value, 1)),
      position = position_dodge(width = 0.8),
      vjust = -0.3,
      size = 2
    )
  
  # Gráfico de porcentaje total
  p_tot <- ggplot(data, aes(x = as.factor(Visit), y = Percentage, fill = Variable)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), width = 0.7) +
    facet_grid(~ Category, scales = "free_x", space = "free") +
    labs(
      title = paste("Contribution of Each", prefix_name, "Covariable Across Visits"),
      x = "Visit",
      y = "Contribution to Score in %",
      fill = NULL
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
    theme(
      legend.position = "bottom",
      legend.title = element_blank(),
      legend.text = element_text(size = 5),
      axis.text.x = element_text(size = 9),
    ) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = color_palette) +
    geom_text(
      aes(label = round(Percentage, 1)),
      position = position_dodge(width = 0.8),
      vjust = -0.3,
      size = 2
    )
  
  return(list(relative = p_rel, absolute = p_abs, total = p_tot))
}

# Procesar datos Score_
score_data <- combined_numeric %>%
  dplyr::select(Visit, starts_with("Score_")) %>%
  process_score_data("Score_")

# Procesar datos ScoreMod_
scoremod_data <- combined_numeric %>%
  dplyr::select(Visit, starts_with("ScoreMod_")) %>%
  process_score_data("ScoreMod_")

# Crear gráficos para Score_

# Crear gráficos para ScoreMod_
scoremod_plots <- create_plots(scoremod_data, "ScoreMod", combined_colors)
```

### MOTOR y NON-MOTOR

```{r}

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(colorspace)

# --- Configuración de colores ---
set3_colors <- RColorBrewer::brewer.pal(12, "Set3")
additional_colors <- "#E69F00"

required_colors <- 22
current_colors <- length(c(set3_colors, additional_colors))
needed_colors <- required_colors - current_colors

new_colors <- colorspace::rainbow_hcl(needed_colors)
combined_colors <- c(set3_colors, additional_colors, new_colors)

# --- Definición de covariables ---

Motor <- c("code_upd2hy_hoehn_and_yahr_stage", 
           "mds_updrs_part_ii_summary_score",
           "mds_updrs_part_iii_summary_score", 
           "Tremor_Right_UM", 
           "Tremor_Left_UM",
           "Tremor_all_Um", 
           "PIGD_UM",
           "mod_schwab_england_pct_adl_score")

Non_Motor <- c("code_upd2106_dopamine_dysregulation_syndrome_features",
               "mds_updrs_part_i_summary_score", 
               "code_upd2102_hallucinations_and_psychosis",
               "code_upd2101_cognitive_impairment", 
               "code_upd2103_depressed_mood",
               "code_upd2104_anxious_mood", 
               "code_upd2105_apathy",
               "rbd_summary_score", 
               "ess_summary_score",
               "moca_total_score")


# --- Función de limpieza de nombres (adaptar según convenga) ---
clean_variable_names <- function(x) {
  gsub("Score_", "", x)
}

# --- Función para procesar datos (score raw) ---
process_score_data <- function(data, prefix, covariates) {
  summarized_data <- data %>%
    group_by(Visit) %>%
    summarise(across(starts_with(prefix), sum, na.rm = TRUE))
  
  combined_long <- summarized_data %>%
    pivot_longer(cols = -Visit, names_to = "Variable", values_to = "Value") %>%
    mutate(Variable = gsub(prefix, "", Variable)) %>%
    filter(Variable %in% covariates)
  
  combined_long <- combined_long %>%
    group_by(Visit) %>%
    mutate(Total = sum(Value, na.rm = TRUE),
           Percentage = (Value / Total) * 100) %>%
    ungroup()
  
  return(combined_long)
}

# --- Función para crear gráficos ---
create_plots <- function(data, prefix_name, group_name, color_palette) {
  # Relativo
  p_rel <- ggplot(data, aes(x = as.factor(Visit), y = Percentage, fill = Variable)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), width = 0.7) +
    labs(
      title = paste("Relative Contribution of Each", prefix_name, group_name, "Covariable per Visit"),
      x = "Visit",
      y = "Contribution to Score in %"
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
    theme(
      legend.position = "bottom",
      legend.title = element_blank(),
      legend.text = element_text(size = 6),
      axis.text.x = element_text(size = 9),
    ) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = color_palette)
  
  # Absoluto
  p_abs <- ggplot(data, aes(x = as.factor(Visit), y = Value, fill = Variable)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), width = 0.7) +
    labs(
      title = paste("Absolute Contribution of Each", prefix_name, group_name, "Covariable per Visit"),
      x = "Visit",
      y = "Absolute Contribution to Score"
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
    theme(
      legend.position = "bottom",
      legend.title = element_blank(),
      legend.text = element_text(size = 6),
      axis.text.x = element_text(size = 9),
    ) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = color_palette)
  
  return(list(relative = p_rel, absolute = p_abs))
}

# --- Procesar datos Score_ Motor ---
score_motor_data <- combined_numeric %>%
  dplyr::select(Visit, starts_with("Score_")) %>%
  process_score_data("Score_", Motor)

# --- Procesar datos Score_ Non-Motor ---
score_nonmotor_data <- combined_numeric %>%
  dplyr::select(Visit, starts_with("Score_")) %>%
  process_score_data("Score_", Non_Motor)

# --- Graficar ---
plots_motor <- create_plots(score_motor_data, "Score", "Motor", combined_colors)
plots_nonmotor <- create_plots(score_nonmotor_data, "Score", "Non-Motor", combined_colors)


plots_motor
plots_nonmotor
```

<!-- ## Signal co-occurrence within the same cluster -->


<!-- ```{r} -->
<!-- # Unir todos los datos en un solo dataframe -->
<!-- # Se combinan los dataframes correspondientes a cada visita en un único dataframe. -->
<!-- Sev_score_detail_allM <- rbind(Sev_score_detail_M0, Sev_score_detail_M12, Sev_score_detail_M24, Sev_score_detail_M36) -->

<!-- ``` -->


<!-- Vamos a dividir el análisis en **4 pasos** consecutivos: -->

<!-- **Paso 1: ** -->
<!-- Se va a empezar agrupando por Pathway, Cluster y K, cuya combinación determina cada cluster concreto. -->
<!-- A continuación se crea una lista de las covariables señal en cada grupo y se almacena en la columna $Covariates$. -->

<!-- **Paso 2: **  -->
<!-- Preparación para análisis de coocurrencia, se extrae la lista de combinaciones de covariables por grupo y se obtiene el conjunto total de covariables únicas que aparecen en todos los grupos -->

<!-- **Paso 3: ** -->
<!-- Cálculo de coocurrencias y test de independencia: Se comparan todas las parejas posibles de covariables. Para cada par de covariables, se recorre toda la lista de combinaciones y se contabiliza cuántas veces: -->

<!--   - Ambas aparecen juntas ($both$) -->

<!--   - Solo aparece la primera ($only\_cov1$) -->

<!--   - Solo aparece la segunda ($only\_cov2$) -->

<!--   - Ninguna aparece ($neither$) -->

<!-- Se construye una matriz de contingencia 2x2 y se aplica un test de independencia de Fisher, ya que hay casos en los que hay menos de 5 muestras. Los resultados se guardan en la lista $results$. -->

<!-- **Paso 4: ** -->

<!-- Los resultados del análisis de coocurrencia se convierten en un dataframe para facilitar su visualización. Se organizan los pares de covariables con la cantidad de veces que aparecen juntas, solo una de ellas o ninguna. Además, se incluyen el Odds Ratio (OR), que indica la fuerza y dirección de la asociación entre las covariables (Un OR > 1 sugiere que ambas covariables tienden a ocurrir juntas y OR < 1 sugiere que la presencia de una covariable disminuye la probabilidad de que la otra aparezca), y el PValue de cada par, que evalúa la significancia estadística de la relación. Se filtran los resultados para mantener solo aquellos con un PValue menor a 0.05, indicando una relación estadísticamente significativa entre las covariables. -->

## Visualization of Pathway results


### Barplot & Sankey plot

Número de apariciones de la pathway
```{r}


# M0
ggplot(Sev_score_detail_M0, aes(x = Pathway, fill = Pathway)) +
  geom_bar() +
  xlab("Pathways") +
  ylab("Número de ocurrencias") +
  ggtitle("Número de Pathways distintos en Sev_score_detail") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 3)) +
  scale_fill_discrete(name = "Pathway") +  # Escala para colores automáticos
  theme(legend.position = "none")  # Eliminar la leyenda



# M12

  geom_bar() +
  xlab("Pathways") +

  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 5)) +

  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 3)) +
  scale_fill_discrete(name = "Pathway") +  # Escala para colores automáticos
  theme(legend.position = "none")  # Eliminar la leyenda






generar_sankey <- function(data_input, score_column = "SevScore", titulo = "Diagrama Sankey") {
  # Validar que la columna de score exista en los datos
  if (!(score_column %in% c("SevScore", "SevScore_mod"))) {
    stop("La columna de score debe ser 'SevScore' o 'SevScore_mod'")
  }
  
  if (!(score_column %in% names(data_input))) {
    stop(paste("La columna", score_column, "no existe en los datos de entrada"))
  }
  
  # Agrupar por Pathway y Covariate, sumar los valores del score seleccionado
  contribution <- data_input %>%
    group_by(Pathway, Covariate) %>%
    summarise(value = sum(.data[[score_column]], na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")
  
  # Crear nodos únicos
  nodos <- unique(c(as.character(contribution$Pathway), as.character(contribution$Covariate)))
  nodos_df <- data.frame(name = nodos)
  
  # Crear enlaces con índices de los nodos (comienzan en 0 para sankeyNetwork)
  enlaces <- contribution %>%
    mutate(source = match(Pathway, nodos) - 1,
           target = match(Covariate, nodos) - 1) %>%
    dplyr::select(source, target, value)
  
  # Crear el gráfico Sankey
  sankey <- sankeyNetwork(Links = enlaces, Nodes = nodos_df,
                          Source = "source", Target = "target",
                          Value = "value", NodeID = "name",
                          units = "T", fontSize = 12, nodeWidth = 30)
  
  # Añadir título usando htmltools
  sankey_con_titulo <- htmltools::tagList(
    htmltools::tags$h2(titulo, style = "text-align: center; font-family: Arial; color: #333;"),
    sankey
  )
  
  # return(sankey_con_titulo)
  return(sankey)
}



# M0
sankey_M0_M <- generar_sankey(Sev_score_detail_M0 %>% filter(!Covariate %in% Non_Motor), score_column = "SevScore", 
               titulo = "Motor Covariate detection in M0 clustering")
sankey_M0_NM <- generar_sankey(Sev_score_detail_M0 %>% filter(!Covariate %in% Motor), score_column = "SevScore", 
               titulo = "Non-Motor Covariate detection in M0 clustering")
#M12
sankey_M12_M <- generar_sankey(Sev_score_detail_M12 %>% filter(!Covariate %in% Non_Motor), score_column = "SevScore", 
               titulo = "Motor Covariate detection in M12 clustering")
sankey_M12_NM <- generar_sankey(Sev_score_detail_M12 %>% filter(!Covariate %in% Motor), score_column = "SevScore", 
               titulo = "Non-Motor Covariate detection in M12 clustering")
#M24
sankey_M24_M <- generar_sankey(Sev_score_detail_M24 %>% filter(!Covariate %in% Non_Motor), score_column = "SevScore", 
               titulo = "Motor Covariate detection in M24 clustering")
sankey_M24_NM <- generar_sankey(Sev_score_detail_M24 %>% filter(!Covariate %in% Motor), score_column = "SevScore", 
               titulo = "Non Motor Covariate detection in M24 clustering")
#M36
sankey_M36_M <- generar_sankey(Sev_score_detail_M36 %>% filter(!Covariate %in% Non_Motor), score_column = "SevScore", 
               titulo = "Motor Covariate detection in M36 clustering")
sankey_M36_NM <- generar_sankey(Sev_score_detail_M36 %>% filter(!Covariate %in% Motor), score_column = "SevScore", 
               titulo = "Non-Motor Covariate detection in M36 clustering")
```
