```{r}
# List of datasets by visit
sev_list <- list(
  M0  = Sev_score_detail_M0,
  M12 = Sev_score_detail_M12,
  M24 = Sev_score_detail_M24,
  M36 = Sev_score_detail_M36
)

# Summary of number of pathways per type (Motor / Non-Motor)
pathway_summary <- bind_rows(
  lapply(names(sev_list), function(name) {
    df <- sev_list[[name]]
    data.frame(
      Visit = name,
      Type = c("Motor", "Non-Motor"),
      Num_Pathways = c(
        length(unique(df$Pathway[df$Covariate %in% Motor])),
        length(unique(df$Pathway[df$Covariate %in% Non_Motor]))
      )
    )
  })
)

# Plot showing the evolution of the number of distinct pathways
ggplot(pathway_summary, aes(x = Visit, y = Num_Pathways, group = Type, color = Type)) +
  geom_line(size = 1.2) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Motor" = "#1B9E77", "Non-Motor" = "#D95F02")) +
  labs(
    title = "Evolution of Distinct Pathways by Covariate Type",
    x = "Visit",
    y = "Number of Distinct Pathways",
    color = "Covariate Type"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12),
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "bottom"
  )
```

### Heatmap

```{r}

generar_heatmap_señales <- function(data_input, titulo = "Heatmap de Señales por Pathway y Covariable") {
  
  # Calcular el número de señales únicas por Pathway y Covariate
  señales_unicas <- data_input %>%
    distinct(Pathway, Covariate, Cluster, k) %>%
    group_by(Pathway, Covariate) %>%
    summarise(n_señales = n(), .groups = "drop")
  
  # Crear matriz para el heatmap
  heatmap_data <- señales_unicas %>%
    pivot_wider(names_from = Covariate, values_from = n_señales, values_fill = 0) %>%
    column_to_rownames("Pathway") %>%
    as.matrix()
  
  # Crear el heatmap (silent=TRUE para capturarlo en objeto)
  heatmap_plot <- pheatmap::pheatmap(
    heatmap_data,
    main = titulo,
    color = colorRampPalette(c("white", "darkolivegreen4", "darkolivegreen"))(50),
    cluster_rows = TRUE,
    cluster_cols = TRUE,
    clustering_distance_rows = "euclidean",
    clustering_distance_cols = "euclidean",
    clustering_method = "complete",
    display_numbers = TRUE,
    number_color = "black",
    fontsize_number = 8,
    fontsize_row = 8,
    fontsize_col = 8,
    angle_col = 45,
    silent = TRUE
  )
  
  # Forzar el dibujo en pantalla
  grid::grid.newpage()
  grid::grid.draw(heatmap_plot$gtable)
  
  return(heatmap_plot)
}

# Función para filtrar por tipo de covariable (Motor/Non-Motor)
generar_heatmap_filtrado <- function(data_input, tipo = "Motor", tiempo = "M0") {
  
  if (tipo == "Motor") {
    datos_filtrados <- data_input %>% filter(!Covariate %in% Non_Motor)
  } else if (tipo == "Non-Motor") {
    datos_filtrados <- data_input %>% filter(!Covariate %in% Motor)
  } else {
    stop("El tipo debe ser 'Motor' o 'Non-Motor'")
  }
  
  titulo <- paste(tipo, "Covariate detection in", tiempo, "clustering")
  
  return(generar_heatmap_señales(datos_filtrados, titulo))
}
```

### Dotplots Pathways-Covariates

```{r}

# Function to generate a dot plot summarizing Covariates
generate_dotplot_covariates <- function(data_input, timepoint = "M0", type = "All") {
  
  # Filter by covariate type if required
  if (type == "Motor") {
    data_filtered <- data_input %>% filter(!Covariate %in% Non_Motor)
  } else if (type == "Non-Motor") {
    data_filtered <- data_input %>% filter(!Covariate %in% Motor)
  } else {
    data_filtered <- data_input
  }
  
  # Compute summary metrics for each covariate
  covariate_summary <- data_filtered %>%
    group_by(Covariate) %>%
    summarise(
      # Total number of detected signals
      total_signals = n(),
      
      # Number of unique pathways generating signals
      unique_pathways = n_distinct(Pathway),
      
      # Number of unique patients contributing severity signals
      affected_patients = n_distinct(sample_id),
      
      # Average cluster size (based on n)
      mean_cluster_size = mean(n, na.rm = TRUE),
      
      # Number of unique clusters
      unique_clusters = n_distinct(Cluster),
      
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    arrange(desc(total_signals))
  
  # Create dot plot
  p <- ggplot(covariate_summary, aes(x = total_signals, y = reorder(Covariate, total_signals))) +
    geom_point(aes(size = unique_pathways, color = affected_patients), alpha = 0.7) +
    scale_color_gradient(low = "blue", high = "red", name = "Affected\npatients") +
    scale_size_continuous(range = c(2, 8), name = "Unique\npathways") +
    labs(
      title = paste("Summary of Signals per Covariate -", timepoint),
      subtitle = ifelse(type != "All", paste("Type:", type), ""),
      x = "Total number of signals",
      y = "Covariate",
      caption = "Size: Unique pathways | Color: Affected patients"
    ) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),
      axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8),
      panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "grey90"),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
    )
  
  return(list(plot = p, data = covariate_summary))
}

# Function to generate a dot plot summarizing Pathways
generate_dotplot_pathways <- function(data_input, timepoint = "M0", top_n = 20) {
  
  # Compute summary metrics for each pathway
  pathway_summary <- data_input %>%
    group_by(Pathway) %>%
    summarise(
      # Total number of detected signals
      total_signals = n(),
      
      # Number of distinct covariates generating signals
      unique_covariates = n_distinct(Covariate),
      
      # Number of unique patients with signals
      affected_patients = n_distinct(sample_id),
      
      # Mean cluster size
      mean_cluster_size = mean(n, na.rm = TRUE),
      
      # Number of unique clusters
      unique_clusters = n_distinct(Cluster),
      
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    arrange(desc(total_signals)) %>%
    head(top_n)  # Display only the top_n most active pathways
  
  # Simplify pathway names for better visualization
  pathway_summary <- pathway_summary %>%
    mutate(Pathway_short = str_replace(Pathway, "CP:REACTOME:", "") %>%
             str_replace("CP:WIKIPATHWAYS:", "") %>%
             str_replace("CP:KEGG:", "") %>%
             str_trunc(40))
  
  # Create dot plot
  p <- ggplot(pathway_summary, aes(x = total_signals, y = reorder(Pathway_short, total_signals))) +
    geom_point(aes(size = unique_covariates, color = mean_cluster_size), alpha = 0.7) +
    scale_color_gradient(low = "lightblue", high = "darkblue", 
                        name = "Mean\ncluster\nsize") +
    scale_size_continuous(range = c(2, 8), name = "Unique\ncovariates") +
    labs(
      title = paste("Top", top_n, "Most Active Pathways -", timepoint),
      x = "Total number of signals",
      y = "Pathway",
      caption = "Size: Unique covariates | Color: Mean cluster size"
    ) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),
      axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8),
      panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "grey90"),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
    )
  
  return(list(plot = p, data = pathway_summary))
}

# Function to generate all figures for a specific timepoint
generate_figures_for_paper <- function(data_input, timepoint = "M0", save_path = ".") {
  
  # Dot plot for all covariates
  dot_cov_all <- generate_dotplot_covariates(data_input, timepoint, "All")
  
  # Dot plot for motor covariates
  dot_cov_motor <- generate_dotplot_covariates(data_input, timepoint, "Motor")
  
  # Dot plot for non-motor covariates
  dot_cov_nonmotor <- generate_dotplot_covariates(data_input, timepoint, "Non-Motor")
  
  # Dot plot for top pathways
  dot_pathways <- generate_dotplot_pathways(data_input, timepoint, 20)
  
  # Save plots
  ggsave(file.path(save_path, paste0("dotplot_cov_all_", timepoint, ".png")), 
         dot_cov_all$plot, width = 10, height = 8, dpi = 300)
  ggsave(file.path(save_path, paste0("dotplot_cov_motor_", timepoint, ".png")), 
         dot_cov_motor$plot, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300)
  ggsave(file.path(save_path, paste0("dotplot_cov_nonmotor_", timepoint, ".png")), 
         dot_cov_nonmotor$plot, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300)
  ggsave(file.path(save_path, paste0("dotplot_pathways_", timepoint, ".png")), 
         dot_pathways$plot, width = 10, height = 8, dpi = 300)
  
  # Save summary tables
  write.csv(dot_cov_all$data, file.path(save_path, paste0("covariate_summary_all_", timepoint, ".csv")), row.names = FALSE)
  write.csv(dot_pathways$data, file.path(save_path, paste0("pathway_summary_", timepoint, ".csv")), row.names = FALSE)
  
  return(list(
    cov_all = dot_cov_all,
    cov_motor = dot_cov_motor,
    cov_nonmotor = dot_cov_nonmotor,
    pathways = dot_pathways
  ))
}

# Run for all timepoints
results_M0  <- generate_figures_for_paper(Sev_score_detail_M0,  "M0")
results_M12 <- generate_figures_for_paper(Sev_score_detail_M12, "M12")
results_M24 <- generate_figures_for_paper(Sev_score_detail_M24, "M24")
results_M36 <- generate_figures_for_paper(Sev_score_detail_M36, "M36")



```

Resultados unificados todas las visitas

```{r}

# --- Function to prepare consolidated data across all visits ---
prepare_consolidated_data <- function() {
  # Add timepoint column to each dataset
  Sev_score_detail_M0$Timepoint  <- "M0"
  Sev_score_detail_M12$Timepoint <- "M12" 
  Sev_score_detail_M24$Timepoint <- "M24"
  Sev_score_detail_M36$Timepoint <- "M36"
  
  # Merge all timepoints into a single dataset
  consolidated_data <- bind_rows(
    Sev_score_detail_M0,
    Sev_score_detail_M12,
    Sev_score_detail_M24,
    Sev_score_detail_M36
  ) %>%
    mutate(Timepoint = factor(Timepoint, levels = c("M0", "M12", "M24", "M36")))
  
  return(consolidated_data)
}

# --- Function for consolidated covariate dotplot ---
generate_dotplot_covariates_consolidated <- function(consolidated_data, type = "All") {
  
  # Filter by type if needed
  if (type == "Motor") {
    data <- consolidated_data %>% filter(!Covariate %in% Non_Motor)
  } else if (type == "Non-Motor") {
    data <- consolidated_data %>% filter(!Covariate %in% Motor)
  } else {
    data <- consolidated_data
  }
  
  # Compute summary metrics per covariate (across all timepoints)
  covariate_summary <- data %>%
    group_by(Covariate) %>%
    summarise(
      total_signals = n(),
      unique_pathways = n_distinct(Pathway),
      affected_patients = n_distinct(sample_id),
      active_timepoints = n_distinct(Timepoint),
      mean_cluster_size = mean(n, na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    mutate(
      # Ensure range 1–4
      active_timepoints = pmin(pmax(active_timepoints, 1), 4)
    ) %>%
    arrange(desc(total_signals))
  
  # Create dotplot
  p <- ggplot(covariate_summary, aes(x = total_signals, y = reorder(Covariate, total_signals))) +
    geom_point(aes(size = unique_pathways, color = active_timepoints), alpha = 0.7) +
    scale_color_gradientn(
      colors = c("blue", "green", "red"),
      limits = c(1, 4),
      breaks = 1:4,
      name = "Active\ntimepoints"
    ) +
    scale_size_continuous(range = c(2, 8), name = "Unique\npathways") +
    labs(
      title = "Consolidated Summary of Signals per Covariate",
      subtitle = ifelse(type != "All", paste("Type:", type), "All timepoints (M0–M36)"),
      x = "Total number of signals",
      y = "Covariate",
      caption = "Size: Unique pathways | Color: Number of active timepoints (1–4)"
    ) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10),
      axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8),
      panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "grey90"),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
    )
  
  return(list(plot = p, data = covariate_summary))
}

# --- Function for consolidated pathway dotplot ---
generate_dotplot_pathways_consolidated <- function(consolidated_data, top_n = 25) {
  
  pathway_summary <- consolidated_data %>%
    group_by(Pathway) %>%
    summarise(
      total_signals = n(),
      unique_covariates = n_distinct(Covariate),
      affected_patients = n_distinct(sample_id),
      active_timepoints = n_distinct(Timepoint),
      mean_cluster_size = mean(n, na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    mutate(
      # Ensure fixed range 1–4
      active_timepoints = pmin(pmax(active_timepoints, 1), 4)
    ) %>%
    arrange(desc(total_signals)) %>%
    head(top_n)
  
  pathway_summary <- pathway_summary %>%
    mutate(Pathway_short = str_replace(Pathway, "CP:REACTOME:", "R:") %>%
             str_replace("CP:WIKIPATHWAYS:", "W:") %>%
             str_replace("CP:KEGG:", "K:") %>%
             str_trunc(35))
  
  p <- ggplot(pathway_summary, aes(x = total_signals, y = reorder(Pathway_short, total_signals))) +
    geom_point(aes(size = unique_covariates, color = active_timepoints), alpha = 0.7) +
    scale_color_gradientn(
      colors = c("lightblue", "blue", "darkblue"),
      limits = c(1, 4),
      breaks = 1:4,
      name = "Active\ntimepoints"
    ) +
    scale_size_continuous(range = c(3, 10), name = "Unique\ncovariates") +
    labs(
      title = paste("Top", top_n, "Most Active Pathways - Consolidated"),
      subtitle = "All timepoints (M0–M36)",
      x = "Total number of signals",
      y = "Pathway",
      caption = "Size: Unique covariates | Color: Number of active timepoints (1–4)"
    ) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10),
      axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9),
      panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "grey90"),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
    )
  
  return(list(plot = p, data = pathway_summary))
}

# --- Function to generate a heatmap of temporal evolution per covariate ---
generate_heatmap_covariate_evolution <- function(consolidated_data, type = "All") {
  
  # Filter by covariate type
  if (type == "Motor") {
    data_filtered <- consolidated_data %>% filter(!Covariate %in% Non_Motor)
  } else if (type == "Non-Motor") {
    data_filtered <- consolidated_data %>% filter(!Covariate %in% Motor)
  } else {
    data_filtered <- consolidated_data
  }
  
  # Count number of signals per covariate and timepoint
  heatmap_data <- data_filtered %>%
    group_by(Covariate, Timepoint) %>%
    summarise(n_signals = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
    pivot_wider(names_from = Timepoint, values_from = n_signals, values_fill = 0) %>%
    column_to_rownames("Covariate") %>%
    as.matrix()
  
  # Order by total activity
  heatmap_data <- heatmap_data[order(rowSums(heatmap_data), decreasing = TRUE), ]
  
  # Create heatmap
  p <- pheatmap::pheatmap(
    heatmap_data,
    main = paste("Temporal Evolution of Covariate-Associated Signals -", type),
    color = colorRampPalette(c("white", "lightgreen", "darkgreen"))(50),
    cluster_rows = FALSE,
    cluster_cols = FALSE,
    display_numbers = TRUE,
    number_format = "%d",
    number_color = "black",
    fontsize_number = 7,
    fontsize_row = 8,
    fontsize_col = 9,
    angle_col = 0,
    silent = TRUE
  )
  
  return(p)
}

# --- Main function to generate all consolidated figures ---
generate_consolidated_figures <- function(save_path = ".") {
  
  # Create output directory if it does not exist
  if (!dir.exists(save_path)) {
    dir.create(save_path)
  }
  
  # Prepare consolidated data
  cat("Preparing consolidated data...\n")
  consolidated_data <- prepare_consolidated_data()
  
  # Generate consolidated dot plots
  cat("Generating covariate dot plots...\n")
  dot_cov_all <- generate_dotplot_covariates_consolidated(consolidated_data, "All")
  dot_cov_motor <- generate_dotplot_covariates_consolidated(consolidated_data, "Motor")
  dot_cov_nonmotor <- generate_dotplot_covariates_consolidated(consolidated_data, "Non-Motor")
  
  cat("Generating pathway dot plots...\n")
  dot_pathways <- generate_dotplot_pathways_consolidated(consolidated_data, 25)
  
  # Generate heatmaps of temporal evolution
  cat("Generating temporal evolution heatmaps...\n")
  heatmap_all <- generate_heatmap_covariate_evolution(consolidated_data, "All")
  heatmap_motor <- generate_heatmap_covariate_evolution(consolidated_data, "Motor")
  heatmap_nonmotor <- generate_heatmap_covariate_evolution(consolidated_data, "Non-Motor")
  
  # Save plots
  cat("Saving figures...\n")
  ggsave(file.path(save_path, "dotplot_cov_all_consolidated.png"), 
         dot_cov_all$plot, width = 12, height = 10, dpi = 300)
  ggsave(file.path(save_path, "dotplot_cov_motor_consolidated.png"), 
         dot_cov_motor$plot, width = 10, height = 8, dpi = 300)
  ggsave(file.path(save_path, "dotplot_cov_nonmotor_consolidated.png"), 
         dot_cov_nonmotor$plot, width = 10, height = 8, dpi = 300)
  ggsave(file.path(save_path, "dotplot_pathways_consolidated.png"), 
         dot_pathways$plot, width = 12, height = 10, dpi = 300)
  
  # Save heatmaps
  png(file.path(save_path, "heatmap_evolution_all.png"), width = 1000, height = 1200, res = 150)
  grid::grid.draw(heatmap_all$gtable)
  dev.off()
  
  png(file.path(save_path, "heatmap_evolution_motor.png"), width = 800, height = 1000, res = 150)
  grid::grid.draw(heatmap_motor$gtable)
  dev.off()
  
  png(file.path(save_path, "heatmap_evolution_nonmotor.png"), width = 800, height = 1000, res = 150)
  grid::grid.draw(heatmap_nonmotor$gtable)
  dev.off()
  
  # Save summary tables
  write.csv(dot_cov_all$data, file.path(save_path, "covariate_summary_all_consolidated.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
  write.csv(dot_pathways$data, file.path(save_path, "pathway_summary_consolidated.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
  
  # Global summary statistics
  global_summary <- consolidated_data %>%
    summarise(
      total_signals = n(),
      unique_covariates = n_distinct(Covariate),
      unique_pathways = n_distinct(Pathway),
      unique_patients = n_distinct(sample_id),
      analyzed_timepoints = n_distinct(Timepoint)
    )
  
  write.csv(global_summary, file.path(save_path, "global_summary_consolidated.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
  
  cat("Consolidated analysis completed. Figures saved in:", save_path, "\n")
  
  return(list(
    data = consolidated_data,
    cov_all = dot_cov_all,
    cov_motor = dot_cov_motor,
    cov_nonmotor = dot_cov_nonmotor,
    pathways = dot_pathways,
    heatmaps = list(
      all = heatmap_all,
      motor = heatmap_motor,
      nonmotor = heatmap_nonmotor
    )
  ))
}

# --- Execute the consolidated analysis ---
consolidated_results <- generate_consolidated_figures()




```

### Se relacionan las mismas pathways con las mismas covariables clínicas en distintas visitas?

```{r eval=FALSE}

check_multiple_combinations <- function(df_list, combinations) {
  results <- data.frame(
    Pathway = combinations$Pathway,
    Covariate = combinations$Covariate,
    M0 = FALSE,
    M12 = FALSE,
    M24 = FALSE,
    M36 = FALSE,
    Total_TRUE = 0,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  
  for (i in 1:nrow(combinations)) {
    pathway <- combinations$Pathway[i]
    covariate <- combinations$Covariate[i]
    
    # Verificar presencia en cada dataframe
    results$M0[i] <- any(df_list$M0$Pathway == pathway & df_list$M0$Covariate == covariate)
    results$M12[i] <- any(df_list$M12$Pathway == pathway & df_list$M12$Covariate == covariate)
    results$M24[i] <- any(df_list$M24$Pathway == pathway & df_list$M24$Covariate == covariate)
    results$M36[i] <- any(df_list$M36$Pathway == pathway & df_list$M36$Covariate == covariate)
    
    # Contar el número de TRUE
    results$Total_TRUE[i] <- sum(results[i, c("M0", "M12", "M24", "M36")])
  }
  
  return(results)
}

# Lista de dataframes nombrados
df_list_named <- list(
  M0 = Sev_score_detail_M0,
  M12 = Sev_score_detail_M12,
  M24 = Sev_score_detail_M24,
  M36 = Sev_score_detail_M36
)

# Obtener todas las combinaciones únicas
all_combinations <- bind_rows(df_list_named) %>%
  distinct(Pathway, Covariate)

# Verificar presencia
results <- check_multiple_combinations(df_list_named, all_combinations)

# Filtrar las combinaciones con más TRUEs (orden descendente)
top_combinations <- results %>%
  filter(Total_TRUE > 1) %>%
  arrange(desc(Total_TRUE))

# Mostrar las top 10
datatable(top_combinations)

  
  
```

# ***Validando el severity score como proxy para la severidad clínica de la enfermedad.

## Score ~ Cov

```{r eval=FALSE}


library(dplyr)
library(pheatmap)
library(grid)

analisis_covariables_lm <- function(Covariates_score, 
                                    score_col = "Motor_Score_mod_Cumulative", 
                                    visitas = c("M0", "M12", "M24", "M36"),
                                    vars_covariables = NULL,
                                    output_file = "Heatmap_R2.pdf",
                                    output_width = 8,
                                    output_height = 10,
                                    alpha = 0.05) {
  
  if (is.null(vars_covariables)) {
    vars_covariables <- names(Covariates_score) %>%
      intersect(names(Covariates_score)[sapply(Covariates_score, is.numeric)]) %>%
      setdiff(c(score_col, "visit", "visit_name", "participant_id", "Order"))
  }
  
  # Empty matrices
  R2_matrix <- matrix(NA, nrow = length(vars_covariables), ncol = length(visitas),
                      dimnames = list(vars_covariables, visitas))
  stars_matrix <- matrix("", nrow = length(vars_covariables), ncol = length(visitas),
                         dimnames = list(vars_covariables, visitas))
  
  # Collect results
  results <- list()
  
  for (v in visitas) {
    df_visit <- Covariates_score %>% filter(visit_name == v)
    
    for (var_name in vars_covariables) {
      x <- df_visit[[var_name]]
      y <- df_visit[[score_col]]
      
      if (all(is.na(x)) || var(x, na.rm = TRUE) == 0) next
      
      df_model <- data.frame(x = x, y = y) %>% na.omit()
      if (nrow(df_model) < 5) next
      
      modelo <- lm(y ~ x, data = df_model)
      summ <- summary(modelo)
      r2 <- summ$r.squared
      pval <- coef(summ)[2, 4]
      beta <- coef(summ)[2, 1]
      
      R2_matrix[var_name, v] <- r2
      if (!is.na(pval) && pval < alpha) {
        stars_matrix[var_name, v] <- "*"
      }
      
      results[[length(results) + 1]] <- data.frame(
        covariate = var_name,
        visit = v,
        R2 = r2,
        beta = beta,
        pvalue = pval,
        stringsAsFactors = FALSE
      )
    }
  }
  
  results_df <- bind_rows(results)
  
  # Save PDF with legend note
  pdf(output_file, width = output_width, height = output_height, useDingbats = FALSE)
  pheatmap(R2_matrix,
           cluster_rows = TRUE, 
           cluster_cols = TRUE,
           display_numbers = stars_matrix,
           number_color = "black",
           color = colorRampPalette(c("white", "yellow", "red"))(50),
           main = paste("R² of covariates predicting", score_col, "per visit"),
           fontsize = 10,
           fontsize_row = 8,
           fontsize_col = 10)
  
  # Add legend note
  grid::grid.text("* p < 0.05", x = 0.9, y = 0.02, gp = gpar(fontsize = 10))
  dev.off()
  
  message("Heatmap saved to: ", output_file)
  
  return(list(R2_matrix = R2_matrix, Results = results_df))
}



## NO ABRIR EL OBJETO ENTERO!!!

R2_results_Motor <- analisis_covariables_lm(Covariates_score, 
                                      score_col = "Motor_Score_raw",
                                      vars_covariables = c(Motor, Non_Motor),
                                      output_file = "Heatmap_R2_Motor_Cov.pdf"
                                      )


R2_results_Non_Motor <- analisis_covariables_lm(Covariates_score, 
                                      score_col = "Non_Motor_Score_raw",
                                      vars_covariables = c(Motor, Non_Motor),
                                      output_file = "Heatmap_R2_Non_Motor_Cov.pdf"
                                      )





```



## Score ~ Pathways

```{r eval=FALSE}

pathways_M0 <- Subsets_pathways[unique(Sev_score_detail_M0$Pathway)] # 3791 genes
pathways_M12 <- Subsets_pathways[unique(Sev_score_detail_M12$Pathway)] # 1779 genes
pathways_M24 <- Subsets_pathways[unique(Sev_score_detail_M24$Pathway)] # 3002 genes
pathways_M36 <- Subsets_pathways[unique(Sev_score_detail_M36$Pathway)] # 5914 genes

pathways_all <- c(pathways_M0, pathways_M12, pathways_M24, pathways_M36)


analisis_pathways_multivariante <- function(df_transcript, 
                                           Covariates_score,
                                           pathways_list,
                                           score_col = "Motor_Score_raw", 
                                           visitas = c("M0", "M12", "M24", "M36"),
                                           min_pathway_size = 5,
                                           max_pathway_size = 50,  # Más conservador para regresión múltiple
                                           min_samples_per_gene = 5,  # Mínimo de muestras por gen
                                           output_file = "Heatmap_R2_Pathways_Multivariate.pdf",
                                           output_width = 20,
                                           output_height = 75,
                                           alpha = 0.05) {
  
  # Cargar librerías necesarias
  if (!requireNamespace("pheatmap", quietly = TRUE)) {
    install.packages("pheatmap")
  }
  if (!requireNamespace("dplyr", quietly = TRUE)) {
    install.packages("dplyr")
  }
  library(pheatmap)
  library(dplyr)
  
  # Filtrar pathways por tamaño
  pathway_sizes <- sapply(pathways_list, length)
  valid_pathways <- pathways_list[pathway_sizes >= min_pathway_size & pathway_sizes <= max_pathway_size]
  
  min_pathway_size <- min(sapply(valid_pathways, length))
  
  message("Analizando ", length(valid_pathways), " pathways (tamaño: ", min_pathway_size, "-", max_pathway_size, " genes)")
  message("Enfoque: Regresión lineal múltiple con todos los genes")
  
  # Empty matrices
  R2_matrix <- matrix(NA, nrow = length(valid_pathways), ncol = length(visitas),
                      dimnames = list(names(valid_pathways), visitas))
  stars_matrix <- matrix("", nrow = length(valid_pathways), ncol = length(visitas),
                         dimnames = list(names(valid_pathways), visitas))
  
  # Collect results
  results <- list()
  
  for (v in visitas) {
    message("Procesando visita: ", v)
    
    # Filtrar datos para la visita actual
    df_visit <- Covariates_score %>% filter(visit_name == v)
    sample_ids <- df_visit$sample_id
    df_transcript_visit <- df_transcript[rownames(df_transcript) %in% sample_ids, ]
    
    for (pathway_name in names(valid_pathways)) {
      valid_genes <- valid_pathways[[pathway_name]][valid_pathways[[pathway_name]] %in% colnames(df_transcript_visit)]
      
      if (length(valid_genes) < min_pathway_size) {
        next
      }
      
      # Obtener datos del pathway
      df_pathway <- df_transcript_visit[, valid_genes, drop = FALSE]
      
      # Obtener severity scores
      severity_scores <- df_visit[[score_col]][match(rownames(df_pathway), df_visit$sample_id)]
      
      # Preparar datos para el modelo
      df_model <- data.frame(y = severity_scores)
      df_model <- cbind(df_model, df_pathway)
      df_model <- na.omit(df_model)
      
      # Construir fórmula para regresión múltiple
      formula_str <- paste("y ~", paste(valid_genes, collapse = " + "))
      
      # Ajustar modelo lineal múltiple
      modelo <- tryCatch({
        lm(as.formula(formula_str), data = df_model)
      }, error = function(e) {
        message("  Error en regresión para pathway '", pathway_name, "': ", e$message)
        return(NULL)
      })
      
      if (is.null(modelo)) next
      
      # Obtener R² y p-value global (usando test F)
      summ <- summary(modelo)
      r2 <- summ$r.squared
      r2_adj <- summ$adj.r.squared
      
      # Test F global
      f_test <- tryCatch({
        anova(modelo)
      }, error = function(e) {
        return(NULL)
      })
      
      if (is.null(f_test)) {
        pval_global <- NA
      } else {
        pval_global <- pf(f_test$F[1], f_test$Df[1], f_test$Df[2], lower.tail = FALSE)
      }
      
      R2_matrix[pathway_name, v] <- r2
      
      if (!is.na(pval_global) && pval_global < alpha) {
        stars_matrix[pathway_name, v] <- "*"
      }
      
      # Guardamos resultados: resumen global + coeficientes
      results[[length(results) + 1]] <- list(
        summary = summ,  # aquí va todo el detalle del lm
        coef_table = broom::tidy(modelo), # tabla de coeficientes limpia
        pathway = pathway_name,
        visit = v,
        R2 = r2,
        R2_adj = r2_adj,
        pvalue_FTest = pval_global,
        n_genes = length(valid_genes),
        n_samples = nrow(df_model)
      )
    }
  }
  
  # Convertir a dataframe resumido
  results_df <- bind_rows(lapply(results, function(x) {
    data.frame(
      pathway = x$pathway,
      visit = x$visit,
      R2 = x$R2,
      R2_adj = x$R2_adj,
      pvalue_FTest = x$pvalue_FTest,
      n_genes = x$n_genes,
      n_samples = x$n_samples,
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
  }))
  
  # Filtrar pathways con al menos un valor
  valid_rows <- rowSums(!is.na(R2_matrix)) > 0
  R2_matrix_filtered <- R2_matrix[valid_rows, , drop = FALSE]
  stars_matrix_filtered <- stars_matrix[valid_rows, , drop = FALSE]
  
  if (nrow(R2_matrix_filtered) == 0) {
    warning("No pathways with valid results found.")
    return(list(R2_matrix = R2_matrix, Results = results_df, Detailed = results))
  }
  
  # Guardar heatmap
  pdf(output_file, width = output_width, height = output_height, useDingbats = FALSE)
  
  pheatmap(R2_matrix_filtered,
           cluster_rows = TRUE, 
           cluster_cols = TRUE,
           display_numbers = stars_matrix_filtered,
           number_color = "black",
           color = colorRampPalette(c("white", "yellow", "red"))(50),
           main = paste("R² pathways predicting", score_col, "per visit"),
           fontsize = 10,
           fontsize_row = 6,
           fontsize_col = 10,
           angle_col = 45)
  
  grid::grid.text("* p < 0.05 (test F global)", x = 0.85, y = 0.02, gp = grid::gpar(fontsize = 8))
  dev.off()
  
  message("Heatmap saved to: ", output_file)
  
  return(list(R2_matrix = R2_matrix, Results = results_df, Detailed = results))
}


sum(sapply(pathways_all, length) <= 200)
```

## Score ~ All Covs
 
Modelos por separado por visitas


```{r}

analisis_multivariable_lm <- function(Covariates_score, 
                                     score_col = "Motor_Score_mod_Cumulative", 
                                     visitas = c("M0", "M12", "M24", "M36"),
                                     vars_covariables = NULL,
                                     alpha = 0.05,
                                     cohort_name = "training") {
  
  # Cargar librerías necesarias
  if (!require(dplyr)) install.packages("dplyr")
  if (!require(broom)) install.packages("broom")
  library(dplyr)
  library(broom)
  
  if (is.null(vars_covariables)) {
    vars_covariables <- names(Covariates_score) %>%
      intersect(names(Covariates_score)[sapply(Covariates_score, is.numeric)]) %>%
      setdiff(c(score_col, "visit", "visit_name", "participant_id", "Order"))
  }
  
  # Lista para almacenar resultados
  results <- list()
  models <- list()
  model_data <- list()
  training_predictions <- list()
  
  for (v in visitas) {
    # Filtrar datos para la visita actual
    df_visit <- Covariates_score %>% 
      filter(visit_name == v) %>%
      dplyr::select(all_of(c(score_col, "participant_id", vars_covariables))) %>%
      na.omit()
    
    # Verificar que hay suficientes datos
    if (nrow(df_visit) < length(vars_covariables) + 10) {
      message(paste("No hay suficientes datos para la visita", v, "- omitiendo"))
      next
    }
    
    # Crear fórmula con todas las covariables
    formula_str <- paste(score_col, "~", paste(vars_covariables, collapse = " + "))
    formula_obj <- as.formula(formula_str)
    
    # Entrenar modelo lineal múltiple
    modelo <- lm(formula_obj, data = df_visit)
    
    # Predecir sobre los datos de entrenamiento
    train_pred <- predict(modelo, newdata = df_visit, se.fit = TRUE)
    
    # Almacenar predicciones de entrenamiento
    training_predictions[[v]] <- data.frame(
      participant_id = df_visit$participant_id,
      visit = v,
      cohort = cohort_name,
      actual_score = df_visit[[score_col]],
      predicted_score = train_pred$fit,
      se_fit = train_pred$se.fit,
      df = train_pred$df,
      residual_scale = train_pred$residual.scale,
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
    
    # Resumen del modelo
    summ <- summary(modelo)
    
    # Extraer coeficientes y estadísticas
    coef_df <- broom::tidy(modelo) %>%
      filter(term != "(Intercept)") %>%
      mutate(
        visit = v,
        significant = p.value < alpha,
        term = gsub("`", "", term)
      )
    
    # Almacenar resultados
    results[[v]] <- list(
      visit = v,
      n_observations = nrow(df_visit),
      r_squared = summ$r.squared,
      adj_r_squared = summ$adj.r.squared,
      f_statistic = summ$fstatistic[1],
      f_pvalue = pf(summ$fstatistic[1], summ$fstatistic[2], summ$fstatistic[3], lower.tail = FALSE),
      coefficients = coef_df
    )
    
    models[[v]] <- modelo
    model_data[[v]] <- df_visit
  }
  
  # Combinar todos los coeficientes en un solo dataframe
  all_coefficients <- bind_rows(lapply(results, function(x) x$coefficients))
  all_training_predictions <- bind_rows(training_predictions)
  
  # Crear resumen por visita
  summary_df <- data.frame(
    visit = sapply(results, function(x) x$visit),
    n_obs = sapply(results, function(x) x$n_observations),
    r_squared = sapply(results, function(x) x$r_squared),
    adj_r_squared = sapply(results, function(x) x$adj_r_squared),
    f_statistic = sapply(results, function(x) x$f_statistic),
    f_pvalue = sapply(results, function(x) x$f_pvalue),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  
  return(list(
    models = models,               # Modelos completos por visita
    model_data = model_data,       # Datos usados para entrenar cada modelo
    training_predictions = all_training_predictions, # Predicciones en cohorte de entrenamiento
    summary = summary_df,          # Resumen estadístico
    coefficients = all_coefficients, # Coeficientes detallados
    detailed_results = results,    # Resultados completos por visita
    formula = formula_str,         # Fórmula utilizada
    variables = vars_covariables,  # Variables predictoras
    target = score_col,            # Variable objetivo
    cohort_name = cohort_name      # Nombre de la cohorte de entrenamiento
  ))
}



# Función para graficar coeficientes
plot_coefficients <- function(resultados) {
  if (!require(ggplot2)) install.packages("ggplot2")
  if (!require(dplyr)) install.packages("dplyr")
  library(ggplot2)
  library(dplyr)
  
  coef_data <- resultados$coefficients
  
  ggplot(coef_data, aes(x = estimate, y = term, color = significant)) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", alpha = 0.5) +
    geom_point(size = 2) +
    facet_grid(visit ~ ., scales = "free_y") +
    labs(title = "Linear model coefficients per visit",
         x = "Value",
         y = "Clinical covariates",
         color = "P-Value < 0.05") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      legend.position = "bottom",
      axis.text.y = element_text(size = 5),  # Tamaño reducido para variables del eje Y
      axis.text.x = element_text(size = 9),  # Tamaño para eje X
      strip.text = element_text(size = 10)   # Tamaño para títulos de facetas
    )
}


plot_coefficients_heatmap <- function(resultados) {
  if (!require(ggplot2)) install.packages("ggplot2")
  if (!require(dplyr)) install.packages("dplyr")
  library(ggplot2)
  library(dplyr)
  
  coef_data <- resultados$coefficients
  
  ggplot(coef_data, aes(x = visit, y = term, fill = estimate)) +
    geom_tile(color = "white") +
    scale_fill_gradient2(low = "blue", high = "red", mid = "white", 
                         midpoint = 0, name = "Coeficiente") +
    geom_text(aes(label = ifelse(significant, 
                                 sprintf("%.3f*", estimate), 
                                 sprintf("%.3f", estimate))), 
              size = 3) +
    labs(title = "Coefficients per visit",
         x = "Visit", y = "Clinical covariates") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
}

plot_coefficients_heatmap <- function(resultados) {
  if (!require(ggplot2)) install.packages("ggplot2")
  if (!require(dplyr)) install.packages("dplyr")
  library(ggplot2)
  library(dplyr)
  
  coef_data <- resultados$coefficients %>%
    mutate(
      # Añadir asteriscos según nivel de significancia
      significance_stars = case_when(
        p.value < 0.001 ~ "***",
        p.value < 0.01 ~ "**",
        p.value < 0.05 ~ "*",
        p.value < 0.1 ~ ".",
        TRUE ~ ""
      ),
      # Crear etiqueta con coeficiente y asteriscos
      label = sprintf("%.3f%s", estimate, significance_stars)
    )
  
  ggplot(coef_data, aes(x = visit, y = term, fill = estimate)) +
    geom_tile(color = "white") +
    scale_fill_gradient2(low = "blue", high = "red", mid = "white", 
                         midpoint = 0, name = "Coefficients") +
    geom_text(aes(label = label), size = 3) +
    labs(title = "Coefficients per visit",
         x = NULL, y = "Clinical covariates") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
      plot.caption = element_text(size = 9, hjust = 0, face = "italic")
    )
}
```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(ggpubr)

# ==============================
# Diccionario de nombres de covariables
# ==============================
covariate_labels <- c(
  # --- Motor ---
  "code_upd2hy_hoehn_and_yahr_stage" = "Hoehn & Yahr Stage",
  "mds_updrs_part_ii_summary_score" = "MDS-UPDRS-II",
  "mds_updrs_part_iii_summary_score" = "MDS-UPDRS-III",
  "Tremor_Right_UM" = "Right Tremor",
  "Tremor_Left_UM" = "Left Tremor",
  "Tremor_all_Um" = "Overall Tremor",
  "PIGD_UM" = "PIGD",
  "mod_schwab_england_pct_adl_score" = "Schwab & England ADL",

  # --- Non-Motor ---
  "code_upd2106_dopamine_dysregulation_syndrome_features" = "DDS",
  "mds_updrs_part_i_summary_score" = "MDS-UPDRS-I",
  "code_upd2102_hallucinations_and_psychosis" = "Hallucinations & Psychosis",
  "code_upd2101_cognitive_impairment" = "Cognitive Impairment",
  "code_upd2103_depressed_mood" = "Depressed Mood",
  "code_upd2104_anxious_mood" = "Anxious Mood",
  "code_upd2105_apathy" = "Apathy",
  "rbd_summary_score" = "RBD",
  "ess_summary_score" = "ESS",
  "moca_total_score" = "MoCA Score"
)

# ==============================
# 1. Extraer heatmaps
# ==============================
heatmap_motor <- plot_coefficients_heatmap(resultados_modelo)
heatmap_non_motor <- plot_coefficients_heatmap(resultados_modelo_Non_Motor)

# ==============================
# 2. Renombrar eje Y según diccionario
# ==============================
heatmap_motor$data$term <- covariate_labels[heatmap_motor$data$term]
heatmap_non_motor$data$term <- covariate_labels[heatmap_non_motor$data$term]

# ==============================
# 3. Eliminar asteriscos de significancia
# ==============================
heatmap_motor$data$significance_stars <- ""
heatmap_non_motor$data$significance_stars <- ""

# ==============================
# 4. Ajustar títulos y ejes
# ==============================
heatmap_motor <- heatmap_motor +
  labs(title = "Motor") +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 0, hjust = 0.5),
    axis.title.y = element_blank(),          # Eliminar título eje Y
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0)
  )

heatmap_non_motor <- heatmap_non_motor +
  labs(title = "Non-Motor") +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 0, hjust = 0.5),
    axis.title.y = element_text(face = "bold"),  # Solo título eje Y aquí
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0)
  )

# ==============================
# 5. Guardar p-values por separado
# ==============================
Motor_significance <- heatmap_motor$data$p.value
NonMotor_significance <- heatmap_non_motor$data$p.value

# ==============================
# 6. Combinar heatmaps
# ==============================
Figure_R3 <- ggarrange(
  heatmap_non_motor, heatmap_motor, 
  ncol = 2, nrow = 1,
  common.legend = FALSE,
  legend = "right",
  labels = NULL
)

# ==============================
# 7. Agregar título de eje X compartido
# ==============================
Figure_R3 <- annotate_figure(Figure_R3,
                             bottom = text_grob("Visit", face = "bold", size = 14))

# ==============================
# 8. Guardar PNG
# ==============================
ggsave("Figure_R3.png", Figure_R3, width = 12, height = 6, dpi = 300)

# ==============================
# 9. Guardar objetos para portar
# ==============================
save(heatmap_motor, heatmap_non_motor,
     Motor_significance, NonMotor_significance,
     file = "Figure_R3_objects_R1.RData")


```

```{r}
# Cohorte genética y HC
Covariates_p <- as.data.frame(readRDS(file.path(base_dir, "metadata_reduced", "PPMI_covariates_extra")))
rownames(Covariates_p) <- Covariates$sample_id


# Visit to include in the analysis
Visit <- c("M0","M12","M24","M36") # "M0","M12","M24","M36", "M6"

cohort <- c(  "Healthy Control", "GBA - Aff", "LRRK2 - Aff", "LRRK2 - Unaff", "SNCA - Aff", "SNCA - Unaff", "GBA - Unaff") #  "Healthy Control", "Sporadic", "GBA - Aff", "LRRK2 - Aff", "LRRK2 - Unaff", "SNCA - Aff", "SNCA - Unaff", "GBA - Unaff"

Diagnosis <- c("Case", "Control") # "Case", "Control"


Covariates_p %>%
  filter(!is.na(mutation)) %>%        # solo filas con valor en mutation
  group_by(mutation) %>%
  summarise(visitas = paste(unique(visit_name), collapse = ", "),
            n = n()) %>%
  arrange(mutation)


Covariates_p <- Covariates_p %>%
  group_by(participant_id) %>%
  mutate(mutation = ifelse(all(is.na(mutation)), NA, first(na.omit(mutation)))) %>%
  ungroup()


# Filtrar los datos según las condiciones adicionales
Covariates_p <- Covariates_p %>%
  filter(case_control_other_latest %in% Diagnosis &
         mutation %in% cohort)


table(Covariates_p$visit_name)


# --- Definir cohortes de interés y cohorte de referencia para los tests ---
cohortes_a_predecir <- c("Healthy Control", "GBA - Aff", "LRRK2 - Aff", "SNCA - Aff")
cohorte_referencia <- "PPMI - Sporadic"   # cohorte contra la que quieres comparar

# Si en tus datos la cohorte "Sporadic" está sin el prefijo, la normalizamos:
Covariates_p <- Covariates_p %>%
  mutate(mutation = case_when(
    mutation == "Sporadic" ~ cohorte_referencia,
    TRUE ~ mutation
  ))

# Filtrar los datos (mantener solo cohortes que interesan o la referencia)
Covariates_p <- Covariates_p %>%
  filter(case_control_other_latest %in% Diagnosis &
         mutation %in% c(cohortes_a_predecir, cohorte_referencia))




variables_modelo <- c(Motor, Non_Motor)
variables_existentes <- intersect(variables_modelo, names(Covariates_p))



predecir_sobre_cohorte <- function(modelo_resultado, nueva_cohorte, 
                                  cohort_name = "test", visitas = NULL,
                                  max_na_percent = 10) {
  
  if (is.null(visitas)) {
    visitas <- names(modelo_resultado$models)
  }
  
  predictions <- list()
  
  # Función para calcular porcentaje de NAs
  percent_na <- function(df) {
    colMeans(is.na(df)) * 100
  }
  
  # Función para calcular la moda
  calculate_mode <- function(x) {
    ux <- unique(na.omit(x))
    if (length(ux) == 0) return(NA)
    ux[which.max(tabulate(match(x, ux)))]
  }
  
  for (v in visitas) {
    # Verificar si existe modelo para esta visita
    if (!v %in% names(modelo_resultado$models)) {
      message(paste("No hay modelo para la visita", v, "- omitiendo"))
      next
    }
    
    modelo_original <- modelo_resultado$models[[v]]
    
    # Filtrar datos de la nueva cohorte para esta visita
    df_visit_nueva <- nueva_cohorte %>%
      filter(visit_name == v) %>%
      dplyr::select(all_of(c("participant_id", modelo_resultado$variables))) 
    
    # 1. Identificar variables con muchos NAs en los nuevos datos
    na_percent <- percent_na(df_visit_nueva)
    vars_con_muchos_nas <- names(na_percent[na_percent > max_na_percent])
    
    # Variables disponibles para predecir (excluyendo las problemáticas)
    variables_disponibles <- setdiff(modelo_resultado$variables, vars_con_muchos_nas)
    
    if (length(vars_con_muchos_nas) > 0) {
      message(paste("Visita", v, ": Variables excluidas por >", max_na_percent, "% NAs:", 
                   paste(vars_con_muchos_nas, collapse = ", ")))
      message(paste("Visita", v, ": Variables disponibles:", length(variables_disponibles), "de", 
                   length(modelo_resultado$variables)))
    }
    
    # Verificar que tenemos al menos algunas variables para predecir
    if (length(variables_disponibles) == 0) {
      warning(paste("Visita", v, ": No hay variables disponibles después de excluir las con muchos NAs"))
      next
    }
    
    # 2. Reentrenar modelo solo con variables disponibles
    # Obtener datos de entrenamiento originales
    datos_entrenamiento <- modelo_resultado$model_data[[v]]
    
    # Filtrar solo variables disponibles
    datos_entrenamiento_adaptado <- datos_entrenamiento %>%
      dplyr::select(all_of(c(modelo_resultado$target, variables_disponibles))) %>%
      na.omit()
    
    # Reentrenar modelo
    formula_adaptada <- as.formula(paste(modelo_resultado$target, "~", 
                                        paste(variables_disponibles, collapse = " + ")))
    
    modelo_adaptado <- lm(formula_adaptada, data = datos_entrenamiento_adaptado)
    
    # 3. Preparar datos para predicción (imputar NAs restantes)
    df_visit_para_prediccion <- df_visit_nueva %>%
      dplyr::select(all_of(c("participant_id", variables_disponibles)))
    
    # Imputar valores faltantes en variables restantes
    for (col_name in variables_disponibles) {
      if (any(is.na(df_visit_para_prediccion[[col_name]]))) {
        if (is.numeric(df_visit_para_prediccion[[col_name]])) {
          # Imputar numéricos con mediana
          median_val <- median(df_visit_para_prediccion[[col_name]], na.rm = TRUE)
          df_visit_para_prediccion[[col_name]][is.na(df_visit_para_prediccion[[col_name]])] <- median_val
        } else {
          # Imputar no numéricos con moda
          mode_val <- calculate_mode(df_visit_para_prediccion[[col_name]])
          if (!is.na(mode_val)) {
            df_visit_para_prediccion[[col_name]][is.na(df_visit_para_prediccion[[col_name]])] <- mode_val
          }
        }
      }
    }
    
    # 4. Predecir con modelo adaptado
    tryCatch({
      predicciones <- predict(modelo_adaptado, newdata = df_visit_para_prediccion, se.fit = TRUE)
      
      predictions[[v]] <- data.frame(
        participant_id = df_visit_para_prediccion$participant_id,
        visit = v,
        cohort = cohort_name,
        predicted_score = predicciones$fit,
        se_fit = predicciones$se.fit,
        df = predicciones$df,
        residual_scale = predicciones$residual.scale,
        n_variables_utilizadas = length(variables_disponibles),
        variables_utilizadas = paste(variables_disponibles, collapse = ", "),
        variables_excluidas = if (length(vars_con_muchos_nas) > 0) {
          paste(vars_con_muchos_nas, collapse = ", ")
        } else {
          "Ninguna"
        },
        stringsAsFactors = FALSE
      )
      
      message(paste("✓ Visita", v, ": Predicciones exitosas usando", length(variables_disponibles), 
                   "variables para", nrow(df_visit_para_prediccion), "participantes"))
      
    }, error = function(e) {
      message(paste("✗ Error en la visita", v, ":", e$message))
    })
  }
  
  # Combinar todas las predicciones
  if (length(predictions) > 0) {
    todas_predicciones <- bind_rows(predictions)
    return(todas_predicciones)
  } else {
    warning("No se pudieron hacer predicciones para ninguna visita")
    return(NULL)
  }
}



# --- Función para predecir SOLO las cohortes seleccionadas ---
predecir_todas_cohortes <- function(modelo_resultado, datos, visitas = c("M0","M12","M24","M36"),
                                   max_na_percent = 10, cohortes_objetivo = cohortes_a_predecir) {
  
  # Tomar solo cohortes objetivo que estén presentes en los datos
  cohortes_presentes <- intersect(unique(datos$mutation), c(cohortes_objetivo, cohorte_referencia))
  
  predicciones_por_cohorte <- map(cohortes_presentes, function(c) {
    # Solo predecir para las cohortes objetivo (excluye la referencia si no está en cohortes_objetivo)
    if (!(c %in% cohortes_objetivo) && c != cohorte_referencia) return(NULL)
    
    datos_filtrados <- datos %>% filter(mutation == c, visit_name %in% visitas)
    if (nrow(datos_filtrados) == 0) return(NULL)
    
    predecir_sobre_cohorte(
      modelo_resultado = modelo_resultado,
      nueva_cohorte = datos_filtrados,
      cohort_name = c,
      visitas = visitas,
      max_na_percent = max_na_percent
    )
  })
  
  bind_rows(predicciones_por_cohorte)
}

# --- Ejecutar predicciones para las cohortes objetivo ---
predicciones_todas <- predecir_todas_cohortes(
  modelo_resultado = resultados_modelo,
  datos = Covariates_p,
  visitas = c("M0", "M12", "M24", "M36"),
  max_na_percent = 10,
  cohortes_objetivo = cohortes_a_predecir
)

# --- Preparar all_preds combinando entrenamiento (train_preds) y predicciones externas ---
train_preds <- resultados_modelo$training_predictions %>%
  dplyr::select(participant_id, visit, cohort, predicted_score, actual_score) %>%
  # Aseguramos que la etiqueta de la cohorte training coincida con la referencia si procede
  mutate(cohort = ifelse(cohort == "Sporadic", cohorte_referencia, cohort))



visitas_plot <- c("M0", "M12", "M24", "M36")



all_preds <- bind_rows(
  train_preds,
  predicciones_todas %>% mutate(actual_score = NA)
) %>%
  filter(visit %in% visitas_plot)

# --- Plot: caja general por cohort (sin cambios) ---
ggplot(all_preds, aes(x = interaction(cohort, visit), y = predicted_score, fill = cohort)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.6) +
  labs(
    title = "Motor severity score prediction from clinical covariates",
    x = "Cohort and Visit",
    y = "Predicted Motor Score"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

# --- Plot comparativo predicted vs actual por cohort (sin cambios) ---
all_preds_long <- all_preds %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(predicted_score, actual_score),
               names_to = "type",
               values_to = "score")

ggplot(all_preds_long, aes(x = cohort, y = score, fill = type)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.6, position = position_dodge(width = 0.8)) +
  labs(
    title = "Motor severity score: predicted vs actual",
    x = "Cohort",
    y = "Score",
    fill = "Score type"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

# --- Plots por visita: aplicar tests solo entre cada cohorte objetivo y la cohorte de referencia ---
visitas_unicas <- unique(all_preds$visit)

for (v in visitas_unicas) {
  datos_visita <- all_preds %>% filter(visit == v)
  
  # Cohortes en esta visita (filtramos para asegurar orden consistente)
  cohortes_en_visita <- intersect(unique(datos_visita$cohort), c(cohortes_a_predecir, cohorte_referencia))
  
  # Construir lista de comparaciones: para cada cohorte objetivo vs cohorte_referencia
  comparisons_visita <- lapply(setdiff(cohortes_en_visita, cohorte_referencia), function(c) {
    c(c, cohorte_referencia)
  })
  # Si no hay comparaciones (ej. no está la referencia o no hay cohortes objetivo), omitir
  if (length(comparisons_visita) == 0) {
    message(paste("No hay comparaciones para la visita", v, "- omitiendo test."))
    next
  }
  
  # Crear plot individual con comparaciones específicas
  p_individual <- ggplot(datos_visita %>% filter(cohort %in% c(cohortes_a_predecir, cohorte_referencia)),
                         aes(x = cohort, y = predicted_score, fill = cohort)) +
    geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7) +
    labs(
      title = paste("Motor severity score prediction -", v),
      subtitle = paste("Comparaciones: cada cohorte vs", cohorte_referencia),
      x = "Cohort",
      y = "Predicted Motor Score",
      caption = "Significancia: *** p < 0.001; ** p < 0.01; * p < 0.05; ns = no significativo"
    ) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10, color = "gray40"),
      plot.caption = element_text(size = 9, color = "gray40")
    ) +
    stat_compare_means(
      comparisons = comparisons_visita,
      method = "wilcox.test",
      label = "p.signif"
    )
  
  # Guardar plot individual
  ggsave(
    filename = paste0("cohort_predict_comparison_Motor_", v, ".png"),
    plot = p_individual,
    width = 12,
    height = 8,
    dpi = 300
  )
  message(paste("Plot guardado para visita:", v))
}

# --- Scatter train: predicted vs actual (sin cambios) ---
ggplot(train_preds, aes(x = actual_score, y = predicted_score)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  labs(
    title = paste0("Severity Score Predicted VS Actual Severity Scores in ", cohorte_referencia),
    x = "Motor_Score_raw (real)",
    y = "Predicted Motor Score"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()



# Ejemplo: predecir para todas las cohortes presentes en Covariates_p
predicciones_todas <- predecir_todas_cohortes(
  modelo_resultado = resultados_modelo,
  datos = Covariates_p,
  visitas = c("M0", "M12", "M24", "M36"),
  max_na_percent = 10
)

train_preds <- resultados_modelo$training_predictions %>%
  dplyr::select(participant_id, visit, cohort, predicted_score, actual_score)


all_preds <- bind_rows(
  train_preds,
  predicciones_todas %>% mutate(actual_score = NA)
) %>%
  filter(visit %in% visitas_plot)






# Separando predicciones por visitas
ggplot(all_preds, aes(x = interaction(cohort, visit), y = predicted_score, fill = cohort)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.6) +
  labs(
    title = "Motor severity score prediction from clinical covariates",
    x = "Cohort and Visit",
    y = "Predicted Motor Score"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))



library(reshape2)

# Transformar a formato largo para ggplot
all_preds_long <- all_preds %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(predicted_score, actual_score),
               names_to = "type",
               values_to = "score")

ggplot(all_preds_long, aes(x = cohort, y = score, fill = type)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.6, position = position_dodge(width = 0.8)) +
  labs(
    title = "Motor severity score: predicted vs actual",
    x = "Cohort",
    y = "Score",
    fill = "Score type"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

# Crear un plot separado para cada visita
visitas_unicas <- unique(all_preds$visit)

for (v in visitas_unicas) {
  # Filtrar datos para esta visita
  datos_visita <- all_preds %>% filter(visit == v)
  
  # Crear comparaciones solo para esta visita
  comparisons_visita <- combn(unique(datos_visita$cohort), 2, simplify = FALSE)
  
  # Crear plot individual
  p_individual <- ggplot(datos_visita, aes(x = cohort, y = predicted_score, fill = cohort)) +
    geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7) +
    labs(
      title = paste("Motor severity score prediction -", v),
      subtitle = "Test estadístico: Wilcoxon rank-sum test",
      x = "Cohort",
      y = "Predicted Motor Score",
      caption = "Significancia: *** p < 0.001; ** p < 0.01; * p < 0.05; ns = no significativo"
    ) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10, color = "gray40"),
      plot.caption = element_text(size = 9, color = "gray40")
    ) +
    stat_compare_means(
      comparisons = comparisons_visita,
      method = "wilcox.test",
      label = "p.signif"
    )
  
  # Guardar plot individual
  ggsave(
    filename = paste0("cohort_predict_comparison_Motor_", v, ".png"),
    plot = p_individual,
    width = 12,
    height = 8,
    dpi = 300
  )
  
  message(paste("Plot guardado para visita:", v))
}

ggplot(train_preds, aes(x = actual_score, y = predicted_score)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7, color = "steelblue") +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  labs(

  ) +
```

```{r}

# ==============================================
# Figure R5 — Comparación de predicciones por cohorte
# ==============================================

# Librerías necesarias
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(ggpubr)

# ==============================================
# 1. Configuración inicial
# ==============================================

cohort_order <- c("Healthy Control", "SNCA - Aff", "GBA - Aff", "LRRK2 - Aff", "PPMI - Sporadic")
cohort_colors <- c(
  "Healthy Control" = "#1b9e77",
  "SNCA - Aff"      = "#d95f02",
  "GBA - Aff"       = "#7570b3",
  "LRRK2 - Aff"     = "#e7298a",
  "PPMI - Sporadic" = "#66a61e"
)
cohorte_referencia <- "PPMI - Sporadic"
visitas_plot <- c("M0", "M12", "M24", "M36")

# ==============================================
# 2. Función auxiliar: plot solo con comparaciones significativas
# ==============================================

plot_predicciones_significativas <- function(datos_visita, cohorte_ref, colores, orden, ylab = "") {
  
  # Factorizar cohort
  datos_visita <- datos_visita %>% mutate(cohort = factor(cohort, levels = orden))
  
  # Comparaciones: cada cohort vs referencia
  cohortes_a_comparar <- setdiff(levels(datos_visita$cohort), cohorte_ref)
  
  # Calcular p-values y solo mantener significativos
  signif_df <- lapply(cohortes_a_comparar, function(c) {
    g1 <- datos_visita$predicted_score[datos_visita$cohort == c]
    g2 <- datos_visita$predicted_score[datos_visita$cohort == cohorte_ref]
    if (length(g1) > 1 && length(g2) > 1) {
      p <- wilcox.test(g1, g2)$p.value
      if (!is.na(p) && p < 0.05) {
        y_pos <- max(g1, na.rm = TRUE) + 0.05 * (max(datos_visita$predicted_score, na.rm = TRUE) -
                                                 min(datos_visita$predicted_score, na.rm = TRUE))
        label <- ifelse(p < 0.001, "***",
                        ifelse(p < 0.01, "**", "*"))
        return(data.frame(cohort = c, ypos = y_pos, label = label))
      }
    }
    return(NULL)
  })
  
  signif_df <- do.call(rbind, signif_df)
  
  # Crear plot base
  p <- ggplot(datos_visita, aes(x = cohort, y = predicted_score, fill = cohort)) +
    geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.85, width = 0.65, color = "black",
                 outlier.shape = 16, outlier.size = 1.5) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = colores, drop = FALSE) +
    scale_x_discrete(labels = c("HC", "SNCA", "GBA", "LRRK2", "sPD"), drop = FALSE) +    
    labs(x = NULL, y = ylab, title = unique(datos_visita$visit)) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_text(size = 18),
      axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
      legend.position = "none",
      panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      axis.line = element_line(color = "black", linewidth = 0.6),
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
    )
  
  # Agregar etiquetas de significancia directamente sobre cada box (tamaño del asterisco incluído)
  if (!is.null(signif_df) && nrow(signif_df) > 0) {
    p <- p + geom_text(data = signif_df, aes(x = cohort, y = ypos, label = label),
                       inherit.aes = FALSE, size = 7.5)
  }
  
  return(p)
}



# ==============================================
# 3. Generar plots por visita usando predicciones
# ==============================================

plots_motor <- lapply(visitas_plot, function(v) {
  datos_visita <- all_preds_motor %>% filter(visit == v)
  plot_predicciones_significativas(datos_visita, cohorte_referencia, cohort_colors, cohort_order)
})

plots_nonmotor <- lapply(visitas_plot, function(v) {
  datos_visita <- all_preds_nonmotor %>% filter(visit == v)
  plot_predicciones_significativas(datos_visita, cohorte_referencia, cohort_colors, cohort_order)
})

# ==============================================
# 4. Combinar plots por columna manteniendo el orden original
# ==============================================

# Columna izquierda: Non-Motor
col_nonmotor <- ggarrange(
  plotlist = plots_nonmotor,
  ncol = 1,
  nrow = length(visitas_plot)
)

# Columna derecha: Motor
col_motor <- ggarrange(
  plotlist = plots_motor,
  ncol = 1,
  nrow = length(visitas_plot)
)

# Combinar ambas columnas lado a lado
combined_plot <- ggarrange(
  col_nonmotor,
  col_motor,
  ncol = 2,
  nrow = 1,
  widths = c(1, 1)
)


# ==============================================
# 5. Crear leyenda expandida
# ==============================================
# 
# create_wide_legend <- function() {
#   dummy_data <- data.frame(cohort = factor(cohort_order, levels = cohort_order))
#   
#   dummy_plot <- ggplot(dummy_data, aes(x = cohort, y = 1, fill = cohort)) +
#     geom_tile() +
#     scale_fill_manual(values = cohort_colors, name = "", drop = FALSE) +
#     theme_void() +
#     theme(
#       legend.position = "bottom",
#       legend.direction = "horizontal",
#       legend.justification = "center",
#       legend.text = element_text(size = 16, margin = ggplot2::margin(0, 20, 0, 0)),
#       legend.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
#       legend.key = element_blank(),
#       legend.key.width = grid::unit(1.5, "cm"),
#       legend.key.height = grid::unit(1, "cm"),
#       legend.spacing.x = grid::unit(1.5, "cm")
#     )
#   
#   ggpubr::get_legend(dummy_plot)
# }
# 
# wide_legend <- create_wide_legend()
# 
# legend_title <- ggpubr::text_grob("Cohort", size = 18, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
# legend_block <- ggpubr::ggarrange(legend_title, wide_legend, ncol = 1, heights = c(0.2, 1))

# ==============================================
# 6. Crear títulos de columnas
# ==============================================

title_nonmotor <- ggpubr::text_grob("Predicted Non-Motor Score", size = 20, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
title_motor <- ggpubr::text_grob("Predicted Motor Score", size = 20, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
titles_row <- ggpubr::ggarrange(title_nonmotor, title_motor, 
                                ncol = 2, 
                                widths = c(1, 1), 
                                labels = c("A", "B"),
                                label.x = 0.05,
                                label.y = 0.75,
                                hjust = 0,
                                align = "hv")

# ==============================================
# 7. Combinar todo en la figura final
# ==============================================

final_plot <- ggarrange(
  titles_row,
  combined_plot,
  # legend_block,
  nrow = 2,
  heights = c(0.05, 0.8) #, 0.15)
)

# ==============================================
# 8. Guardar la figura
# ==============================================

ggsave(
  filename = "Figure_R5.png",
  plot = final_plot,
  width = 16,
  height = 16,
  dpi = 300
)


# ==============================================
# 9. Guardar objetos para reproducibilidad
# ==============================================

save(
  plots_motor,           # lista de plots de motor
  plots_nonmotor,        # lista de plots de non-motor
  combined_plot,         # plots combinados en columnas
  final_plot,            # figura final lista para guardar
  plot_predicciones_significativas, # función para plots
  cohort_order,          # orden de cohortes
  cohort_colors,         # colores de cohortes
  cohorte_referencia,    # cohorte de referencia
  visitas_plot,          # visitas a plotear
  file = "Figure_R5_objects.RData"
)



```

```{r}
    stat_compare_means(

    width = 12,
    height = 8,

  )

}
```

### Score ~ transcriptomics

```{r eval=FALSE}

# This dataset contains the samples resulting from previous data selection (2832 samples × 58,780 genes)
PPMI_exp_p <- readRDS(file.path(base_dir, "1aFase_ReducirGenes", "PPMI_exp"))[, colnames(PPMI_exp)]

# Case samples (sample_id = "Case") are selected. A merge is then performed with the Covariates dataset, which has been previously filtered.
PPMI_exp_p$sample_id <- rownames(PPMI_exp_p)

# PPMI_exp_p <- vst(as.matrix(t(PPMI_exp_p)))



# Uso de medicación 
Covariates_medication <- Covariates_score %>%
  left_join(Medication %>% 
             select(sample_id, 
                    use_of_pd_medication,
                    pd_medication_recent_use_months_after_baseline,
                    on_levodopa,
                    on_dopamine_agonist,
                    on_other_pd_medications),
           by = "sample_id")  


# Calcular el número de datos disponibles por visita
disponibilidad_medicacion <- Covariates_medication %>%
  group_by(visit_name) %>%
  summarise(
    n_total = n(),
    use_of_pd_medication_disponible = sum(!is.na(use_of_pd_medication)),
    pd_medication_recent_use_disponible = sum(!is.na(pd_medication_recent_use_months_after_baseline)),
    on_levodopa_disponible = sum(!is.na(on_levodopa)),
    on_dopamine_agonist_disponible = sum(!is.na(on_dopamine_agonist)),
    on_other_pd_medications_disponible = sum(!is.na(on_other_pd_medications)),
    .groups = 'drop'
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    use_of_pd_medication_pct = round(use_of_pd_medication_disponible / n_total * 100, 1),
    pd_medication_recent_use_pct = round(pd_medication_recent_use_disponible / n_total * 100, 1),
    on_levodopa_pct = round(on_levodopa_disponible / n_total * 100, 1),
    on_dopamine_agonist_pct = round(on_dopamine_agonist_disponible / n_total * 100, 1),
    on_other_pd_medications_pct = round(on_other_pd_medications_disponible / n_total * 100, 1)
  )

# Mostrar el resultado
print(disponibilidad_medicacion)


# Genetic and Healthy COntrol Cohorts

  left_join(Medication %>% 
             select(sample_id, 
                    use_of_pd_medication,
                    pd_medication_recent_use_months_after_baseline,
                    on_levodopa,
                    on_dopamine_agonist,
                    on_other_pd_medications),
           by = "sample_id")

  group_by(visit_name) %>%
  summarise(
    n_total = n(),
    use_of_pd_medication_disponible = sum(!is.na(use_of_pd_medication)),
    pd_medication_recent_use_disponible = sum(!is.na(pd_medication_recent_use_months_after_baseline)),
    on_levodopa_disponible = sum(!is.na(on_levodopa)),
    on_dopamine_agonist_disponible = sum(!is.na(on_dopamine_agonist)),
    on_other_pd_medications_disponible = sum(!is.na(on_other_pd_medications)),
    .groups = 'drop'
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    use_of_pd_medication_pct = round(use_of_pd_medication_disponible / n_total * 100, 1),
    pd_medication_recent_use_pct = round(pd_medication_recent_use_disponible / n_total * 100, 1),
    on_levodopa_pct = round(on_levodopa_disponible / n_total * 100, 1),
    on_dopamine_agonist_pct = round(on_dopamine_agonist_disponible / n_total * 100, 1),
    on_other_pd_medications_pct = round(on_other_pd_medications_disponible / n_total * 100, 1)
  )

# Mostrar el resultado
```

#### VS Genetic Cohort and Healthy Control

##### Motor RF

```{r}

# Función para análisis multivariable con Random Forest para datos de transcriptómica
analisis_multivariable_rf <- function(
    expression_data, 
    clinical_data,
    score_col = "Motor_Score_raw", 
    visitas = c("M0", "M12", "M24", "M36"),
    alpha = 0.05,
    cohort_name = "training",
    n_trees = 500,
    n_cores = 4,
    preprocess = TRUE,
    max_features = 500,
    importance_type = "permutation",
    test_fraction = 0.3,   # proporción test
    n_bootstrap = 10       # número de repeticiones bootstrap
) {
  
  required_packages <- c("dplyr", "broom", "ranger", "caret", "doParallel", "foreach", "tidyr")
  for (pkg in required_packages) {
    if (!require(pkg, character.only = TRUE)) {
      install.packages(pkg)
      library(pkg, character.only = TRUE)
    }
  }
  
  registerDoParallel(cores = n_cores)
  
  expression_processed <- expression_data %>%
    mutate(sample_id = rownames(.))
  
  combined_data <- clinical_data %>%
    inner_join(expression_processed, by = "sample_id") %>%
    filter(visit_name %in% visitas)
  
  results <- list()
  models <- list()
  model_data <- list()
  test_predictions <- list()
  feature_importance <- list()
  
  for (v in visitas) {
    df_visit <- combined_data %>% 
      filter(visit_name == v) %>%
      dplyr::select(all_of(c(score_col, "participant_id", "sample_id", setdiff(colnames(expression_processed), "participant_id")))) %>%
      na.omit()
    
    if (nrow(df_visit) < 20) {
      message(paste("No hay suficientes datos para la visita", v, "- omitiendo"))
      next
    }
    
    X <- as.matrix(df_visit %>% dplyr::select(-all_of(c(score_col, "participant_id", "sample_id"))))
    y <- df_visit[[score_col]]
    
    if (preprocess) {
      # Eliminar predictores de varianza casi cero
      nzv <- nearZeroVar(X)
      if (length(nzv) > 0 && length(nzv) < ncol(X)) {
        X <- X[, -nzv]
      }
      # Eliminar predictores altamente correlacionados
      if (ncol(X) > 2) {
        high_corr <- findCorrelation(cor(X), cutoff = 0.95)
        if (length(high_corr) > 0 && length(high_corr) < ncol(X)) {
          X <- X[, -high_corr]
        }
      }
    }
    
    
    # ---- Split train/test ----
    set.seed(123)
    train_idx <- sample(seq_len(nrow(X)), size = floor((1 - test_fraction) * nrow(X)))
    test_idx <- setdiff(seq_len(nrow(X)), train_idx)
    
    X_train <- X[train_idx, , drop = FALSE]
    y_train <- y[train_idx]
    X_test  <- X[test_idx, , drop = FALSE]
    y_test  <- y[test_idx]
    
    feature_names <- colnames(X_train)
    
    # ---- Random Forest ajustado ----
    final_model <- ranger(
      x = X_train,
      y = y_train,
      num.trees = n_trees,
      mtry = min(50, ncol(X_train)),   # limitar predictores por split
      min.node.size = 5,               # tamaño mínimo de nodo
      sample.fraction = 0.7,           # fracción de datos por árbol
      importance = importance_type,
      verbose = FALSE,
      num.threads = n_cores
    )
    
    # Predicciones sobre test
    test_pred <- predict(final_model, data = as.data.frame(X_test))$predictions
    
    test_predictions[[v]] <- data.frame(
      participant_id = df_visit$participant_id[test_idx],
      sample_id = df_visit$sample_id[test_idx],
      visit = v,
      cohort = cohort_name,
      actual_score = y_test,
      predicted_score = as.numeric(test_pred),
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
    
    actual <- y_test
    predicted <- as.numeric(test_pred)
    
    rmse_final <- sqrt(mean((actual - predicted)^2))
    mae_final <- mean(abs(actual - predicted))
    r2_final <- cor(actual, predicted)^2
    
    # Importancia de features
    if (!is.null(final_model$variable.importance)) {
      imp <- final_model$variable.importance
      imp_df <- data.frame(
        feature = names(imp),
        importance = imp,
        stringsAsFactors = FALSE
      ) %>%
        arrange(desc(importance))
      feature_importance[[v]] <- imp_df
    }
    
    # ---- Validación bootstrap sobre test ----
    bootstrap_results <- foreach(i = 1:n_bootstrap, .combine = rbind, .packages = c("ranger")) %dopar% {
      set.seed(123 + i)
      train_idx <- sample(seq_len(nrow(X)), size = floor((1 - test_fraction) * nrow(X)))
      test_idx  <- setdiff(seq_len(nrow(X)), train_idx)
      
      X_train <- X[train_idx, , drop = FALSE]
      y_train <- y[train_idx]
      X_test  <- X[test_idx, , drop = FALSE]
      y_test  <- y[test_idx]
      
      rf_fit <- ranger(
        x = X_train,
        y = y_train,
        num.trees = n_trees,
        mtry = min(50, ncol(X_train)),
        min.node.size = 5,
        sample.fraction = 0.7,
        importance = "none",
        verbose = FALSE
      )
      
      pred <- predict(rf_fit, data = as.data.frame(X_test))$predictions
      
      rmse <- sqrt(mean((y_test - pred)^2))
      mae <- mean(abs(y_test - pred))
      r2 <- cor(y_test, pred)^2
      
      data.frame(
        bootstrap = i,
        rmse = rmse,
        mae = mae,
        r2 = r2,
        n_train = length(train_idx),
        n_test = length(test_idx)
      )
    }
    
    results[[v]] <- list(
      visit = v,
      n_train = length(train_idx),
      n_test = length(test_idx),
      n_features = ncol(X),
      rmse = rmse_final,
      mae = mae_final,
      r2 = r2_final,
      bootstrap_results = bootstrap_results
    )
    
    models[[v]] <- final_model
    model_data[[v]] <- list(
      X_train = X_train, y_train = y_train,
      X_test = X_test, y_test = y_test,
      participant_ids = df_visit$participant_id,
      feature_names = feature_names
    )
  }
  
  all_test_predictions <- bind_rows(test_predictions)
  
  summary_df <- data.frame(
    visit = sapply(results, function(x) x$visit),
    n_train = sapply(results, function(x) x$n_train),
    n_test = sapply(results, function(x) x$n_test),
    n_features = sapply(results, function(x) x$n_features),
    rmse = sapply(results, function(x) x$rmse),
    mae = sapply(results, function(x) x$mae),
    r2 = sapply(results, function(x) x$r2),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  
  return(list(
    models = models,
    model_data = model_data,
    test_predictions = all_test_predictions,
    summary = summary_df,
    detailed_results = results,
    feature_importance = feature_importance,
    target = score_col,
    cohort_name = cohort_name
  ))
}





# Entrenar el modelo con la cohorte principal 
resultados_rf <- analisis_multivariable_rf(
  expression_data = PPMI_exp_p,  
  clinical_data = Covariates_score, 
  score_col = "Motor_Score_raw",
  visitas = c("M0", "M12", "M24", "M36"),
  cohort_name = "PPMI - Sporadic",
  n_trees = 500,
  test_fraction = 0.2,
  n_cores = 8
)



ggplot(resultados_rf$test_predictions,
       aes(x = actual_score, y = predicted_score, color = visit)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, color = "black") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Predicted vs Actual Motor Score (Test Set)",
    x = "Actual score",
    y = "Predicted score"
  )


boot_all <- lapply(resultados_rf$detailed_results, function(x) {
  x$bootstrap_results %>% mutate(visit = x$visit)
}) %>% bind_rows()

ggplot(boot_all, aes(x = visit, y = r2, fill = visit)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Bootstrap R² distribution per visit",
    y = "R²",
    x = "Visit"
  )



```

```{r eval=FALSE}
         absolute_error = abs(residual))

# Función para calcular correlaciones por visita
analyze_error_relationship <- function(predictions_df) {
  correlations <- predictions_df %>%
    group_by(visit) %>%
    summarise(
      cor_residual_actual = cor(actual_score, residual, method = "pearson"),
      cor_abs_error_actual = cor(actual_score, absolute_error, method = "pearson"),
      p_value_residual = cor.test(actual_score, residual)$p.value,
      p_value_abs_error = cor.test(actual_score, absolute_error)$p.value,
      n = n()
    ) %>%
    mutate(
      sig_residual = ifelse(p_value_residual < 0.05, "*", ""),
      sig_abs_error = ifelse(p_value_abs_error < 0.05, "*", "")
    )
  
  return(correlations)
}

# Aplicar el análisis
error_correlations <- analyze_error_relationship(resultados_rf$test_predictions)
print(error_correlations)

  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "plasma") +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "red", se = TRUE) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Joint Distribution: Actual Score vs Absolute Error",

    y = "Absolute Error",
    fill = "Count"
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ visit, scales = "free")

quantile_analysis <- resultados_rf$test_predictions %>%
  group_by(visit) %>%
  mutate(score_quantile = cut(actual_score, 
                             breaks = quantile(actual_score, probs = seq(0, 1, 0.25)),
                             labels = c("Q1", "Q2", "Q3", "Q4"),
                             include.lowest = TRUE)) %>%
  group_by(visit, score_quantile) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_actual = mean(actual_score),
    mean_absolute_error = mean(absolute_error),
    mean_squared_error = mean(residual^2),
    n = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Visualizar error por cuantil

    x = "Score Quantile",
```
