## Percentile of severity score variation 

```{r}

# Función para calcular y visualizar cambios en percentiles
visualizar_cambios_percentiles <- function(severity_scores) {
  # Calcular percentiles para cada visita
  severity_percentiles <- severity_scores %>%
    group_by(Visit) %>%
    mutate(
      Percentil_raw = rank(Motor_Score_raw) / n(),
      Percentil_mod = rank(Motor_Score_mod) / n()
    ) %>%
    ungroup()
  
  # Seleccionar un subconjunto aleatorio de pacientes para visualización
  pacientes_muestra <- sample(unique(severity_percentiles$participant_id), 2)
  
  # Gráfico de evolución de percentiles (raw)
  p_raw <- severity_percentiles %>%
    filter(participant_id %in% pacientes_muestra) %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = Visit, y = Percentil_raw, group = participant_id)) +
    geom_line(alpha = 0.6) +
    geom_point(size = 2) +
    labs(title = "Evolución de Percentiles (Score Raw)",
         y = "Percentil", x = "Visita (meses)") +
    theme_minimal() +
    scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0.5, linetype = "dashed", color = "red")
  
  # Gráfico de evolución de percentiles (mod)
  p_mod <- severity_percentiles %>%
    filter(participant_id %in% pacientes_muestra) %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = Visit, y = Percentil_mod, group = participant_id)) +
    geom_line(alpha = 0.6) +
    geom_point(size = 2) +
    labs(title = "Evolución de Percentiles (Score Modificado)",
         y = "Percentil", x = "Visita (meses)") +
    theme_minimal() +
    scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0.5, linetype = "dashed", color = "red")
  
  # Calcular cambios entre visitas consecutivas
  cambios <- severity_percentiles %>%
    arrange(participant_id, Visit) %>%
    group_by(participant_id) %>%
    mutate(
      Cambio_raw = Percentil_raw - lag(Percentil_raw),
      Cambio_mod = Percentil_mod - lag(Percentil_mod),
      Intervalo = Visit - lag(Visit)
    ) %>%
    filter(!is.na(Cambio_raw)) %>%
    ungroup()
  
  # Gráfico de distribución de cambios
  p_dist <- ggplot(cambios, aes(x = Cambio_mod)) +
    geom_histogram(bins = 30, fill = "steelblue", alpha = 0.7) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
    labs(title = "Distribución de Cambios en Percentiles",
         x = "Cambio en percentil", y = "Frecuencia") +
    theme_minimal() +
    scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::percent)
  
  # Resumen estadístico de los cambios
  resumen_cambios <- cambios %>%
    group_by(Intervalo) %>%
    summarise(
      Media_raw = mean(Cambio_raw),
      Mediana_raw = median(Cambio_raw),
      SD_raw = sd(Cambio_raw),
      Media_mod = mean(Cambio_mod),
      Mediana_mod = median(Cambio_mod),
      SD_mod = sd(Cambio_mod)
    )
  
  # Mostrar gráficos y resumen
  print(ggarrange(p_raw, p_mod, ncol = 2))
  print(p_dist)
  print(resumen_cambios)
  
  return(list(
    datos_percentiles = severity_percentiles,
    cambios = cambios,
    resumen = resumen_cambios
  ))
}

# Ejecutar el análisis
resultados_visualizacion <- visualizar_cambios_percentiles(combined_severity_score_all_visits)


```

### Lasso


#### Predict percentile variation between visits (train visit genes only)

```{r eval=FALSE}

predict_motor_percentile_change <- function(expression_data, severity_scores, 
                                           train_visits = 0, predict_visits = NULL, 
                                           test_size = 0.2, n_bootstrap = 100) {
  
  if (is.null(predict_visits)) {
    predict_visits <- train_visits
  }
  
  # Calculate percentile ranks for each visit
  severity_scores <- severity_scores %>%
    group_by(Visit) %>%
    mutate(
      Motor_Score_raw_percentile = rank(Motor_Score_raw) / n(),
      Motor_percentile_mod = rank(Motor_Score_mod) / n()
    ) %>%
    ungroup()
  
  combinations <- expand.grid(
    train_visit = train_visits,
    predict_visit = predict_visits,
    score_type = c("raw", "mod"),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  ) %>%
    filter(predict_visit > train_visit)  # Only predict future visits
  
  # Train and evaluate function remains the same
  train_and_evaluate <- function(df_model, target_var, test_size, best_lambda = NULL) {
    
    df_model <- df_model %>% filter(!is.na(.data[[target_var]]))
    
    set.seed(123)
    train_index <- createDataPartition(df_model[[target_var]], p = 1 - test_size, list = FALSE)
    train_data <- df_model[train_index, ]
    test_data <- df_model[-train_index, ]
    
    x_train <- as.matrix(train_data %>% dplyr::select(starts_with("ENSG")))
    y_train <- train_data[[target_var]]
    x_test <- as.matrix(test_data %>% dplyr::select(starts_with("ENSG")))
    y_test <- test_data[[target_var]]
    
    grid <- 10^seq(3, -3, length = 1000)
    lasso_model <- cv.glmnet(x_train, y_train, alpha = 1, lambda = grid)
    best_lambda <- lasso_model$lambda.min
    
    predictions <- predict(lasso_model, newx = x_test, s = best_lambda)
    mse <- mean((y_test - predictions)^2)
    varianza_y <- var(y_test)
    rmse <- sqrt(mse)
    desviacion_y <- sd(y_test)
    r_squared <- 1 - (mse / varianza_y)
    
    registerDoParallel(cores = 10)
    bootstrap_r2 <- function(x_train, y_train, x_test, y_test, lambda, n_bootstrap = 1000) {
      r2_values <- foreach(i = 1:n_bootstrap, .combine = 'c', .packages = "glmnet") %dopar% {
        idx <- sample(1:nrow(x_train), nrow(x_train), replace = TRUE)
        model_boot <- glmnet(x_train[idx, ], y_train[idx], alpha = 1, lambda = lambda)
        pred_test <- predict(model_boot, newx = x_test)
        var_y <- var(y_test)
        ifelse(var_y > .Machine$double.eps, 1 - (mean((y_test - pred_test)^2) / var_y), NA)
      }
      list(
        r2_mean = mean(r2_values, na.rm = TRUE),
        r2_median = median(r2_values, na.rm = TRUE),
        r2_ci = quantile(r2_values, probs = c(0.025, 0.975), na.rm = TRUE)
      )
    }
    
    bootstrap_results <- bootstrap_r2(x_train, y_train, x_test, y_test, best_lambda, n_bootstrap)
    
    list(
      MSE = mse,
      Variance = varianza_y,
      RMSE = rmse,
      SD = desviacion_y,
      Rsquared_original = r_squared,
      Rsquared_bootstrap_mean = bootstrap_results$r2_mean,
      Rsquared_bootstrap_median = bootstrap_results$r2_median,
      Rsquared_lower_ci = bootstrap_results$r2_ci[1],
      Rsquared_upper_ci = bootstrap_results$r2_ci[2],
      best_lambda = best_lambda,
      n_predictors = ncol(x_train)
    )
  }
  
  # Modified processing function to predict percentile change
  process_combination <- function(train_visit, predict_visit, score_type) {
    
    # Choose pathways based on train_visit
    pathways_list <- get(paste0("pathways_M", train_visit))
    pathways_select_unique <- pathways_list[!duplicated(names(pathways_list))]
    gene_names <- unique(unlist(pathways_select_unique))
    
    # Select and preprocess expression data
    PPMI_exp_selected <- expression_data %>%
      dplyr::select(any_of(gene_names)) %>%
      rownames_to_column(var = "sample_id") %>%
      dplyr::filter(sample_id %in% severity_scores$sample_id) #%>%
      # mutate_at(vars(-sample_id), ~ log2(. + 0.1))
    
    # Remove highly correlated genes
    correlation_matrix <- cor(PPMI_exp_selected %>% dplyr::select(-sample_id))
    correlation_long <- as.data.frame(as.table(correlation_matrix))
    highly_correlated <- correlation_long %>% filter(Freq > 0.95, Var1 != Var2)
    correlated_genes <- unique(c(highly_correlated$Var1, highly_correlated$Var2))
    remaining_genes <- setdiff(gene_names, correlated_genes)
    
    PPMI_exp_selected <- PPMI_exp_selected %>% 
      dplyr::select(sample_id, any_of(remaining_genes))
    
    # Merge with severity scores
    genes_predict_severity <- merge(
      severity_scores[, c("Motor_Score_raw_percentile", "Motor_percentile_mod", 
                          "sample_id", "participant_id", "Visit")], 
      PPMI_exp_selected, 
      by = "sample_id"
    )
    
    # Select the appropriate score type
    percentile_col <- ifelse(score_type == "raw", "Motor_Score_raw_percentile", "Motor_percentile_mod")
    
    # Prepare data for modeling
    df_model <- genes_predict_severity %>%
      filter(Visit %in% c(train_visit, predict_visit)) %>%
      arrange(participant_id, Visit) %>%
      group_by(participant_id) %>%
      filter(n() == 2) %>%  # Only keep participants with both visits
      summarize(
        baseline_percentile = .data[[percentile_col]][Visit == train_visit],
        followup_percentile = .data[[percentile_col]][Visit == predict_visit],
        percentile_change = followup_percentile - baseline_percentile,
        across(starts_with("ENSG"), ~ .[Visit == train_visit])
      ) %>%
      ungroup()
    
    # Train and evaluate the model
    res_change <- train_and_evaluate(df_model, "percentile_change", test_size)
    
    data.frame(train_visit, predict_visit, score_type,
               target = "percentile_change", res_change, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  }

  all_results <- pmap_dfr(combinations, process_combination)
  
  return(all_results)
}


# Example usage
results_percentile_change <- predict_motor_percentile_change(
  expression_data = PPMI_exp,
  severity_scores = combined_severity_score_all_visits,
  train_visits = c(0, 12, 24), 
  predict_visits = c(12, 24, 36),
  n_bootstrap = 100
)

saveRDS(results_percentile_change, "results_percentile_change_nofilter_group_factor_vsg")
```



#### Predict percentile variation between visits (all pathways)

```{r eval=FALSE}

predict_motor_percentile_change_all_pathways <- function(expression_data, severity_scores, 
                                           train_visits = 0, predict_visits = NULL, 
                                           test_size = 0.2, pathways_to_use = pathways_all, n_bootstrap = 100) {
  
  if (is.null(predict_visits)) {
    predict_visits <- train_visits
  }
  
  # Calculate percentile ranks for each visit
  severity_scores <- severity_scores %>%
    group_by(Visit) %>%
    mutate(
      Motor_Score_raw_percentile = rank(Motor_Score_raw) / n(),
      Motor_percentile_mod = rank(Motor_Score_mod) / n()
    ) %>%
    ungroup()
  
  combinations <- expand.grid(
    train_visit = train_visits,
    predict_visit = predict_visits,
    score_type = c("raw", "mod"),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  ) %>%
    filter(predict_visit > train_visit)  # Only predict future visits
  
  # Train and evaluate function remains the same
  train_and_evaluate <- function(df_model, target_var, test_size, best_lambda = NULL) {
    
    df_model <- df_model %>% filter(!is.na(.data[[target_var]]))
    
    set.seed(123)
    train_index <- createDataPartition(df_model[[target_var]], p = 1 - test_size, list = FALSE)
    train_data <- df_model[train_index, ]
    test_data <- df_model[-train_index, ]
    
    x_train <- as.matrix(train_data %>% dplyr::select(starts_with("ENSG")))
    y_train <- train_data[[target_var]]
    x_test <- as.matrix(test_data %>% dplyr::select(starts_with("ENSG")))
    y_test <- test_data[[target_var]]
    
    grid <- 10^seq(3, -3, length = 1000)
    lasso_model <- cv.glmnet(x_train, y_train, alpha = 1, lambda = grid)
    best_lambda <- lasso_model$lambda.min
    
    predictions <- predict(lasso_model, newx = x_test, s = best_lambda)
    mse <- mean((y_test - predictions)^2)
    varianza_y <- var(y_test)
    rmse <- sqrt(mse)
    desviacion_y <- sd(y_test)
    r_squared <- 1 - (mse / varianza_y)
    
    registerDoParallel(cores = 10)
    bootstrap_r2 <- function(x_train, y_train, x_test, y_test, lambda, n_bootstrap = 1000) {
      r2_values <- foreach(i = 1:n_bootstrap, .combine = 'c', .packages = "glmnet") %dopar% {
        idx <- sample(1:nrow(x_train), nrow(x_train), replace = TRUE)
        model_boot <- glmnet(x_train[idx, ], y_train[idx], alpha = 1, lambda = lambda)
        pred_test <- predict(model_boot, newx = x_test)
        var_y <- var(y_test)
        ifelse(var_y > .Machine$double.eps, 1 - (mean((y_test - pred_test)^2) / var_y), NA)
      }
      list(
        r2_mean = mean(r2_values, na.rm = TRUE),
        r2_median = median(r2_values, na.rm = TRUE),
        r2_ci = quantile(r2_values, probs = c(0.025, 0.975), na.rm = TRUE)
      )
    }
    
    bootstrap_results <- bootstrap_r2(x_train, y_train, x_test, y_test, best_lambda, n_bootstrap)
    
    list(
      MSE = mse,
      Variance = varianza_y,
      RMSE = rmse,
      SD = desviacion_y,
      Rsquared_original = r_squared,
      Rsquared_bootstrap_mean = bootstrap_results$r2_mean,
      Rsquared_bootstrap_median = bootstrap_results$r2_median,
      Rsquared_lower_ci = bootstrap_results$r2_ci[1],
      Rsquared_upper_ci = bootstrap_results$r2_ci[2],
      best_lambda = best_lambda,
      n_predictors = ncol(x_train)
    )
  }
  
  # Modified processing function to predict percentile change
  process_combination <- function(train_visit, predict_visit, score_type) {
    
    # Choose pathways based on train_visit
    pathways_list <- pathways_to_use
    pathways_select_unique <- pathways_list[!duplicated(names(pathways_list))]
    gene_names <- unique(unlist(pathways_select_unique))
    
    # Select and preprocess expression data
    PPMI_exp_selected <- expression_data %>%
      dplyr::select(any_of(gene_names)) %>%
      rownames_to_column(var = "sample_id") %>%
      dplyr::filter(sample_id %in% severity_scores$sample_id) #%>%
      # mutate_at(vars(-sample_id), ~ log2(. + 0.1))
    
    # Remove highly correlated genes
    correlation_matrix <- cor(PPMI_exp_selected %>% dplyr::select(-sample_id))
    correlation_long <- as.data.frame(as.table(correlation_matrix))
    highly_correlated <- correlation_long %>% filter(Freq > 0.95, Var1 != Var2)
    correlated_genes <- unique(c(highly_correlated$Var1, highly_correlated$Var2))
    remaining_genes <- setdiff(gene_names, correlated_genes)
    
    PPMI_exp_selected <- PPMI_exp_selected %>% 
      dplyr::select(sample_id, any_of(remaining_genes))
    
    # Merge with severity scores
    genes_predict_severity <- merge(
      severity_scores[, c("Motor_Score_raw_percentile", "Motor_percentile_mod", 
                          "sample_id", "participant_id", "Visit")], 
      PPMI_exp_selected, 
      by = "sample_id"
    )
    
    # Select the appropriate score type
    percentile_col <- ifelse(score_type == "raw", "Motor_Score_raw_percentile", "Motor_percentile_mod")
    
    # Prepare data for modeling
    df_model <- genes_predict_severity %>%
      filter(Visit %in% c(train_visit, predict_visit)) %>%
      arrange(participant_id, Visit) %>%
      group_by(participant_id) %>%
      filter(n() == 2) %>%  # Only keep participants with both visits
      summarize(
        baseline_percentile = .data[[percentile_col]][Visit == train_visit],
        followup_percentile = .data[[percentile_col]][Visit == predict_visit],
        percentile_change = followup_percentile - baseline_percentile,
        across(starts_with("ENSG"), ~ .[Visit == train_visit])
      ) %>%
      ungroup()
    
    # Train and evaluate the model
    res_change <- train_and_evaluate(df_model, "percentile_change", test_size)
    
    data.frame(train_visit, predict_visit, score_type,
               target = "percentile_change", res_change, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  }

  all_results <- pmap_dfr(combinations, process_combination)
  
  return(all_results)
}


# Example usage
results_percentile_change_all_pathways <- predict_motor_percentile_change_all_pathways(
  expression_data = PPMI_exp,
  severity_scores = combined_severity_score_all_visits,
  train_visits = c(0, 12, 24), 
  predict_visits = c(12, 24, 36),
  pathways_to_use = pathways_all,  
  n_bootstrap = 100
)

saveRDS(results_percentile_change_all_pathways, "results_percentile_change_nofilter_group_factor_all_pathways_vsg")



```

```{r}
plot_percentile <- generate_r2_plot(results_percentile_change_all_pathways, "Lasso para predicción percentile change (all pathways)")
```

### Random Forest

#### MOTOR - Predict percentile variation between visits (all pathways)

```{r eval=FALSE}

predict_motor_percentile_change_rf <- function(expression_data, severity_scores, 
                                               train_visits = 0, predict_visits = NULL, 
                                               test_size = 0.2, pathways_to_use = pathways_all, 
                                               n_bootstrap = 100) {
  
  if (is.null(predict_visits)) {
    predict_visits <- train_visits
  }
  
  # Calcular percentiles por visita
  severity_scores <- severity_scores %>%
    group_by(Visit) %>%
    mutate(
      Motor_Score_raw_percentile = rank(Motor_Score_raw) / n(),
      Motor_percentile_mod = rank(Motor_Score_mod) / n()
    ) %>%
    ungroup()
  
  combinations <- expand.grid(
    train_visit = train_visits,
    predict_visit = predict_visits,
    score_type = c("raw", "mod"),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  ) %>%
    filter(predict_visit > train_visit)
  
  # Nueva versión con randomForest
  train_and_evaluate <- function(df_model, target_var, test_size) {
    
    df_model <- df_model %>% filter(!is.na(.data[[target_var]]))
    
    set.seed(123)
    train_index <- createDataPartition(df_model[[target_var]], p = 1 - test_size, list = FALSE)
    train_data <- df_model[train_index, ]
    test_data <- df_model[-train_index, ]
    
    x_train <- train_data %>% dplyr::select(starts_with("ENSG"))
    y_train <- train_data[[target_var]]
    x_test <- test_data %>% dplyr::select(starts_with("ENSG"))
    y_test <- test_data[[target_var]]
    
    rf_model <- randomForest(x = x_train, y = y_train)
    predictions <- predict(rf_model, newdata = x_test)
    
    mse <- mean((y_test - predictions)^2)
    varianza_y <- var(y_test)
    rmse <- sqrt(mse)
    desviacion_y <- sd(y_test)
    r_squared <- 1 - (mse / varianza_y)
    
    registerDoParallel(cores = 10)
    bootstrap_r2 <- function(x_train, y_train, x_test, y_test, n_bootstrap = 100) {
      r2_values <- foreach(i = 1:n_bootstrap, .combine = 'c', .packages = "randomForest") %dopar% {
        idx <- sample(1:nrow(x_train), nrow(x_train), replace = TRUE)
        rf_boot <- randomForest(x = x_train[idx, ], y = y_train[idx])
        pred_test <- predict(rf_boot, newdata = x_test)
        var_y <- var(y_test)
        ifelse(var_y > .Machine$double.eps, 1 - (mean((y_test - pred_test)^2) / var_y), NA)
      }
      list(
        r2_mean = mean(r2_values, na.rm = TRUE),
        r2_median = median(r2_values, na.rm = TRUE),
        r2_ci = quantile(r2_values, probs = c(0.025, 0.975), na.rm = TRUE)
      )
    }
    
    bootstrap_results <- bootstrap_r2(x_train, y_train, x_test, y_test, n_bootstrap)
    
    list(
      MSE = mse,
      Variance = varianza_y,
      RMSE = rmse,
      SD = desviacion_y,
      Rsquared_original = r_squared,
      Rsquared_bootstrap_mean = bootstrap_results$r2_mean,
      Rsquared_bootstrap_median = bootstrap_results$r2_median,
      Rsquared_lower_ci = bootstrap_results$r2_ci[1],
      Rsquared_upper_ci = bootstrap_results$r2_ci[2],
      best_lambda = NA,
      n_predictors = ncol(x_train)
    )
  }
  
  process_combination <- function(train_visit, predict_visit, score_type) {
    pathways_list <- pathways_to_use
    pathways_select_unique <- pathways_list[!duplicated(names(pathways_list))]
    gene_names <- unique(unlist(pathways_select_unique))
    
    PPMI_exp_selected <- expression_data %>%
      dplyr::select(any_of(gene_names)) %>%
      rownames_to_column(var = "sample_id") %>%
      dplyr::filter(sample_id %in% severity_scores$sample_id) #%>%
      # mutate_at(vars(-sample_id), ~ log2(. + 0.1))
    
    correlation_matrix <- cor(PPMI_exp_selected %>% dplyr::select(-sample_id))
    correlation_long <- as.data.frame(as.table(correlation_matrix))
    highly_correlated <- correlation_long %>% filter(Freq > 0.95, Var1 != Var2)
    correlated_genes <- unique(c(highly_correlated$Var1, highly_correlated$Var2))
    remaining_genes <- setdiff(gene_names, correlated_genes)
    
    PPMI_exp_selected <- PPMI_exp_selected %>% 
      dplyr::select(sample_id, any_of(remaining_genes))
    
    genes_predict_severity <- merge(
      severity_scores[, c("Motor_Score_raw_percentile", "Motor_percentile_mod", 
                          "sample_id", "participant_id", "Visit")], 
      PPMI_exp_selected, 
      by = "sample_id"
    )
    
    percentile_col <- ifelse(score_type == "raw", "Motor_Score_raw_percentile", "Motor_percentile_mod")
    
    df_model <- genes_predict_severity %>%
      filter(Visit %in% c(train_visit, predict_visit)) %>%
      arrange(participant_id, Visit) %>%
      group_by(participant_id) %>%
      filter(n() == 2) %>% 
      summarize(
        baseline_percentile = .data[[percentile_col]][Visit == train_visit],
        followup_percentile = .data[[percentile_col]][Visit == predict_visit],
        percentile_change = followup_percentile - baseline_percentile,
        across(starts_with("ENSG"), ~ .[Visit == train_visit])
      ) %>%
      ungroup()
    
    res_change <- train_and_evaluate(df_model, "percentile_change", test_size)
    
    data.frame(train_visit, predict_visit, score_type,
               target = "percentile_change", res_change, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  }
  
  all_results <- pmap_dfr(combinations, process_combination)
  
  return(all_results)
}



# Example usage
results_rf_percentile_change_all_pathways <- predict_motor_percentile_change_rf(
  expression_data = PPMI_exp,
  severity_scores = combined_severity_score_all_visits,
  train_visits = c(0, 12, 24), 
  predict_visits = c(12, 24, 36),
  pathways_to_use = pathways_all,  
  n_bootstrap = 25
)

saveRDS(results_rf_percentile_change_all_pathways, "results_RF_percentile_change_nofilter_group_factor_all_pathways_vsg")
```

#### NON MOTOR - Predict percentile variation between visits (all pathways)

```{r eval=FALSE}

predict_non_motor_percentile_change_rf <- function(expression_data, severity_scores, 
                                               train_visits = 0, predict_visits = NULL, 
                                               test_size = 0.2, pathways_to_use = pathways_all, 
                                               n_bootstrap = 100) {
  
  if (is.null(predict_visits)) {
    predict_visits <- train_visits
  }
  
  # Calcular percentiles por visita
  severity_scores <- severity_scores %>%
    group_by(Visit) %>%
    mutate(
      Non_Motor_Score_raw_percentile = rank(Non_Motor_Score_raw) / n(),
      Non_Motor_percentile_mod = rank(Non_Motor_Score_mod) / n()
    ) %>%
    ungroup()
  
  combinations <- expand.grid(
    train_visit = train_visits,
    predict_visit = predict_visits,
    score_type = c("raw", "mod"),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  ) %>%
    filter(predict_visit > train_visit)
  
  # Nueva versión con randomForest
  train_and_evaluate <- function(df_model, target_var, test_size) {
    
    df_model <- df_model %>% filter(!is.na(.data[[target_var]]))
    
    set.seed(123)
    train_index <- createDataPartition(df_model[[target_var]], p = 1 - test_size, list = FALSE)
    train_data <- df_model[train_index, ]
    test_data <- df_model[-train_index, ]
    
    x_train <- train_data %>% dplyr::select(starts_with("ENSG"))
    y_train <- train_data[[target_var]]
    x_test <- test_data %>% dplyr::select(starts_with("ENSG"))
    y_test <- test_data[[target_var]]
    
    rf_model <- randomForest(x = x_train, y = y_train)
    predictions <- predict(rf_model, newdata = x_test)
    
    mse <- mean((y_test - predictions)^2)
    varianza_y <- var(y_test)
    rmse <- sqrt(mse)
    desviacion_y <- sd(y_test)
    r_squared <- 1 - (mse / varianza_y)
    
    registerDoParallel(cores = 10)
    bootstrap_r2 <- function(x_train, y_train, x_test, y_test, n_bootstrap = 100) {
      r2_values <- foreach(i = 1:n_bootstrap, .combine = 'c', .packages = "randomForest") %dopar% {
        idx <- sample(1:nrow(x_train), nrow(x_train), replace = TRUE)
        rf_boot <- randomForest(x = x_train[idx, ], y = y_train[idx])
        pred_test <- predict(rf_boot, newdata = x_test)
        var_y <- var(y_test)
        ifelse(var_y > .Machine$double.eps, 1 - (mean((y_test - pred_test)^2) / var_y), NA)
      }
      list(
        r2_mean = mean(r2_values, na.rm = TRUE),
        r2_median = median(r2_values, na.rm = TRUE),
        r2_ci = quantile(r2_values, probs = c(0.025, 0.975), na.rm = TRUE)
      )
    }
    
    bootstrap_results <- bootstrap_r2(x_train, y_train, x_test, y_test, n_bootstrap)
    
    list(
      MSE = mse,
      Variance = varianza_y,
      RMSE = rmse,
      SD = desviacion_y,
      Rsquared_original = r_squared,
      Rsquared_bootstrap_mean = bootstrap_results$r2_mean,
      Rsquared_bootstrap_median = bootstrap_results$r2_median,
      Rsquared_lower_ci = bootstrap_results$r2_ci[1],
      Rsquared_upper_ci = bootstrap_results$r2_ci[2],
      best_lambda = NA,
      n_predictors = ncol(x_train)
    )
  }
  
  process_combination <- function(train_visit, predict_visit, score_type) {
    pathways_list <- pathways_to_use
    pathways_select_unique <- pathways_list[!duplicated(names(pathways_list))]
    gene_names <- unique(unlist(pathways_select_unique))
    
    PPMI_exp_selected <- expression_data %>%
      dplyr::select(any_of(gene_names)) %>%
      rownames_to_column(var = "sample_id") %>%
      dplyr::filter(sample_id %in% severity_scores$sample_id) #%>%
      # mutate_at(vars(-sample_id), ~ log2(. + 0.1))
    
    correlation_matrix <- cor(PPMI_exp_selected %>% dplyr::select(-sample_id))
    correlation_long <- as.data.frame(as.table(correlation_matrix))
    highly_correlated <- correlation_long %>% filter(Freq > 0.95, Var1 != Var2)
    correlated_genes <- unique(c(highly_correlated$Var1, highly_correlated$Var2))
    remaining_genes <- setdiff(gene_names, correlated_genes)
    
    PPMI_exp_selected <- PPMI_exp_selected %>% 
      dplyr::select(sample_id, any_of(remaining_genes))
    
    genes_predict_severity <- merge(
      severity_scores[, c("Non_Motor_Score_raw_percentile", "Non_Motor_percentile_mod", 
                          "sample_id", "participant_id", "Visit")], 
      PPMI_exp_selected, 
      by = "sample_id"
    )
    
    percentile_col <- ifelse(score_type == "raw", "Non_Motor_Score_raw_percentile", "Non_Motor_percentile_mod")
    
    df_model <- genes_predict_severity %>%
      filter(Visit %in% c(train_visit, predict_visit)) %>%
      arrange(participant_id, Visit) %>%
      group_by(participant_id) %>%
      filter(n() == 2) %>% 
      summarize(
        baseline_percentile = .data[[percentile_col]][Visit == train_visit],
        followup_percentile = .data[[percentile_col]][Visit == predict_visit],
        percentile_change = followup_percentile - baseline_percentile,
        across(starts_with("ENSG"), ~ .[Visit == train_visit])
      ) %>%
      ungroup()
    
    res_change <- train_and_evaluate(df_model, "percentile_change", test_size)
    
    data.frame(train_visit, predict_visit, score_type,
               target = "percentile_change", res_change, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  }
  
  all_results <- pmap_dfr(combinations, process_combination)
  
  return(all_results)
}

  expression_data = PPMI_exp,
  severity_scores = combined_severity_score_all_visits,

  predict_visits = c(12, 24, 36),
```

# *Selection of the Optimal k for Progression Clustering (Motor & Non-Motor)

```{r}


severity_scores = combined_severity_score_all_visits


# Primero identificar las columnas
cols_to_use <- names(severity_scores)[grepl("Score", names(severity_scores))]
print(cols_to_use)

# Luego calcular percentiles
severity_scores <- severity_scores %>%
  group_by(Visit) %>%
  mutate(across(all_of(cols_to_use),
                ~ rank(.) / n(),
                .names = "{.col}_percentile")) %>%
  ungroup()
# 
# # Identificar columnas de interés (las que terminan en _mod o _raw)
# cols_percentiles <- c("Motor_Score_mod", "Motor_Score_raw",
#                       "Non_Motor_Score_mod", "Non_Motor_Score_raw",
#                       "Cognitive_Score_mod", "Cognitive_Score_raw",
#                       "Neuro_Score_mod", "Neuro_Score_raw",
#                       "Neuro_Cogn_Score_mod", "Neuro_Cogn_Score_raw",
#                       "Sleep_Score_mod", "Sleep_Score_raw",
#                       "Non_Motor_Overall_Score_mod", "Non_Motor_Overall_Score_raw")

# Calcular percentiles por visita
# severity_scores <- severity_scores %>%
#   group_by(Visit) %>%
#   mutate(across(all_of(cols_percentiles),
#                 ~ rank(.) / n(),
#                 .names = "{.col}_percentile")) %>%
#   ungroup()


# saveRDS(severity_scores, file.path(base_dir, "Pipeline", "severity_scores_for_pred_K"))



evaluate_clustering_progression <- function(Covariates, Covariates_score, 
                                            evaluation_covariates, 
                                            progression_column = "Motor_Score_raw_Cumulative",
                                            k_range = 2:20) {
  results_distances <- data.frame()
  
  for (k in k_range) {
    Covariates_scores_all <- Covariates_score
    
    # --- 1. Long to wide for progression column ---
    df_long <- Covariates_scores_all[, c("participant_id", "Visit", progression_column)]
    colnames(df_long)[3] <- "progression_score"
    df_long_cluster <- df_long[order(df_long$participant_id, df_long$Visit), ]
    df_wide <- tidyr::pivot_wider(df_long_cluster, names_from = Visit, values_from = progression_score, names_prefix = "Visit_")
    
    # --- 2. Compute progression differences ---
    df_diff <- df_wide %>%
      mutate(
        S0 = Visit_0,
        diff_0_12 = Visit_12 - Visit_0,
        diff_12_24 = Visit_24 - Visit_12,
        diff_24_36 = Visit_36 - Visit_24
      ) %>%
      dplyr::select(participant_id, S0, diff_0_12, diff_12_24, diff_24_36)
    
    # --- 3. Filtrar columnas constantes y escalar ---
    data_to_scale <- df_diff[, -1]
    data_filtered <- data_to_scale[, apply(data_to_scale, 2, sd, na.rm = TRUE) != 0]
    data_scaled <- scale(data_filtered)
    
    # --- 4. Clustering k-means ---
    km_cluster <- kmeans(data_scaled, centers = k, nstart = 25)
    df_cluster <- df_diff["participant_id"] %>% dplyr::mutate(cluster_motor = km_cluster$cluster)
    
    # --- 5. Verificar que haya al menos 2 individuos por cluster ---
    cluster_labels <- df_cluster$cluster_motor
    cluster_sizes <- table(cluster_labels)
    if (any(cluster_sizes < 2)) {
      message(paste("Saltando k =", k, "porque al menos un cluster tiene menos de 2 individuos."))
      next
    }
    
    # --- 6. Calcular métricas sobre distancias internas del clustering ---
    dist_kmeans <- dist(data_scaled, method = "euclidean")
    stats_kmeans <- fpc::cluster.stats(dist_kmeans, cluster_labels)
    db_index_kmeans <- clusterSim::index.DB(data_scaled, cluster_labels, d = dist_kmeans)$DB
    
    results_distances <- rbind(results_distances, data.frame(
      k = k,
      average_within = stats_kmeans$average.within,
      average_between = stats_kmeans$average.between,
      within_between_ratio = stats_kmeans$wb.ratio,
      average_silhouette = stats_kmeans$avg.silwidth,
      davies_bouldin = db_index_kmeans,
      method = "cluster_distances"
    ))
  }
  
  # --- 7. Devolver solo las métricas de distancia ---
  return(results_distances)
}



# Identificar todas las columnas que terminan en _percentile
percentile_cols <- names(severity_scores)[grepl("_percentile$", names(severity_scores))]

# Aplicar la función a cada columna percentile
results_list <- map(percentile_cols, ~ {
  evaluate_clustering_progression(
    Covariates = Covariates,
    Covariates_score = severity_scores,
    evaluation_covariates = NULL,
    progression_column = .x,
    k_range = 2:20
  )
})

# Asignar nombres significativos a los resultados
names(results_list) <- percentile_cols




### Motor ###

results_motor <- evaluate_clustering_progression(
  Covariates = Covariates,                   # aunque no se usa ahora, se requiere
  Covariates_score = severity_scores,       
  evaluation_covariates = NULL,               # no se usa, puede ser NULL o vacío
  progression_column = "Motor_Score_raw_percentile",
  k_range = 2:20                              # rango de clusters a evaluar
)


### Non Motor ###

results_Non_Motor <- evaluate_clustering_progression(
  Covariates = Covariates,                   # aunque no se usa ahora, se requiere
  Covariates_score = severity_scores,       
  evaluation_covariates = NULL,               # no se usa, puede ser NULL o vacío
  progression_column = "Non_Motor_Score_raw_percentile",
  k_range = 2:20                              # rango de clusters a evaluar
)




### Cognitive (raw) ###
results_Cognitive <- evaluate_clustering_progression(
  Covariates = Covariates,
  Covariates_score = severity_scores,
  evaluation_covariates = NULL,
  progression_column = "Cognitive_Score_raw_percentile",
  k_range = 2:20
)

### Neuro (raw) ###
results_Neuro <- evaluate_clustering_progression(
  Covariates = Covariates,
  Covariates_score = severity_scores,
  evaluation_covariates = NULL,
  progression_column = "Neuro_Score_raw_percentile",
  k_range = 2:20
)

### Neuro-Cogn (raw) ###
results_Neuro_Cogn <- evaluate_clustering_progression(
  Covariates = Covariates,
  Covariates_score = severity_scores,
  evaluation_covariates = NULL,
  progression_column = "Neuro_Cogn_Score_raw_percentile",
  k_range = 2:20
)

### Sleep (raw) ###
results_Sleep <- evaluate_clustering_progression(
  Covariates = Covariates,
  Covariates_score = severity_scores,
  evaluation_covariates = NULL,
  progression_column = "Sleep_Score_raw_percentile",
  k_range = 2:20
)

### Non-Motor Overall (raw) ###
results_Non_Motor_Overall <- evaluate_clustering_progression(
  Covariates = Covariates,
  Covariates_score = severity_scores,
  evaluation_covariates = NULL,
  progression_column = "Non_Motor_Overall_Score_raw_percentile",
  k_range = 2:20
)




# Lista con resultados y etiquetas
results_list <- list(
  Motor = results_motor,
  Non_Motor = results_Non_Motor,
  Cognitive = results_Cognitive,
  Neuro = results_Neuro,
  Neuro_Cogn = results_Neuro_Cogn,
  Sleep = results_Sleep,
  Non_Motor_Overall = results_Non_Motor_Overall
)

# Función para graficar las 4 métricas
plot_clustering_metrics <- function(df, label) {
  plots <- list()
  
  # 1. Average Within / Average Between
  p1 <- ggplot(df, aes(x = k)) +
    geom_line(aes(y = average_within, color = "Average Within"), size = 1) +
    geom_point(aes(y = average_within, color = "Average Within"), size = 2) +
    geom_line(aes(y = average_between, color = "Average Between"), size = 1) +
    geom_point(aes(y = average_between, color = "Average Between"), size = 2) +
    scale_color_manual(values = c("Average Within" = "blue", "Average Between" = "red")) +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = unique(df$k)) +
    labs(title = paste("Evolución de Within/Between vs k -", label),
         x = "Número de Clusters (k)", y = "Distancia Promedio", color = "Métrica") +
    theme_minimal()
  
  # 2. Ratio Within/Between
  p2 <- ggplot(df, aes(x = k, y = within_between_ratio)) +
    geom_line() +
    geom_point() +
    ggtitle(paste("Ratio Cohesión/Separación vs k -", label)) +
    xlab("Número de Clusters (k)") + ylab("Within/Between Ratio") +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = unique(df$k)) +
    theme_minimal()
  
  # 3. Davies-Bouldin
  p3 <- ggplot(df, aes(x = k, y = davies_bouldin)) +
    geom_line() +
    geom_point() +
    ggtitle(paste("Davies-Bouldin Index vs k -", label)) +
    xlab("Número de Clusters (k)") + ylab("Davies-Bouldin Index") +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = unique(df$k)) +
    theme_minimal()
  
  # 4. Silhouette
  p4 <- ggplot(df, aes(x = k, y = average_silhouette)) +
    geom_line() +
    geom_point() +
    ggtitle(paste("Average Silhouette vs k -", label)) +
    xlab("Número de Clusters (k)") + ylab("Average Silhouette") +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = unique(df$k)) +
    theme_minimal()
  
  plots <- list(p1, p2, p3, p4)
  return(plots)
}

# Generar todas las gráficas
all_plots <- imap(results_list, ~ plot_clustering_metrics(.x, .y))


all_plots







# A partir de la Opción 1 - results_list ya contiene todos los resultados
# results_list tiene nombres como: "Motor_Score_raw_percentile", "Motor_Score_mod_percentile", etc.

# Función para extraer el nombre del dominio de forma más legible
get_domain_name <- function(full_name) {
  # Remover "_percentile" y extraer la parte antes del primer "_mod" o "_raw"
  clean_name <- gsub("_percentile$", "", full_name)
  domain <- gsub("_(mod|raw)$", "", clean_name)
  return(domain)
}

# Crear lista organizada por dominios (sin duplicados)
domains <- unique(sapply(names(results_list), get_domain_name))

# Crear lista de resultados por dominio (usamos el primer resultado por dominio)
results_by_domain <- list()

for (domain in domains) {
  # Encontrar la primera columna que pertenece a este dominio
  domain_cols <- names(results_list)[grepl(paste0("^", domain), names(results_list))]
  if (length(domain_cols) > 0) {
    # Usar el primer resultado disponible para este dominio
    results_by_domain[[domain]] <- results_list[[domain_cols[1]]]
  }
}

# Función para graficar las 4 métricas (la que ya tenías)
plot_clustering_metrics <- function(df, label) {
  plots <- list()
  
  # 1. Average Within / Average Between
  p1 <- ggplot(df, aes(x = k)) +
    geom_line(aes(y = average_within, color = "Average Within"), size = 1) +
    geom_point(aes(y = average_within, color = "Average Within"), size = 2) +
    geom_line(aes(y = average_between, color = "Average Between"), size = 1) +
    geom_point(aes(y = average_between, color = "Average Between"), size = 2) +
    scale_color_manual(values = c("Average Within" = "blue", "Average Between" = "red")) +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = unique(df$k)) +
    labs(title = paste("Evolución de Within/Between vs k -", label),
         x = "Número de Clusters (k)", y = "Distancia Promedio", color = "Métrica") +
    theme_minimal()
  
  # 2. Ratio Within/Between
  p2 <- ggplot(df, aes(x = k, y = within_between_ratio)) +
    geom_line() +
    geom_point() +
    ggtitle(paste("Ratio Cohesión/Separación vs k -", label)) +
    xlab("Número de Clusters (k)") + ylab("Within/Between Ratio") +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = unique(df$k)) +
    theme_minimal()
  
  # 3. Davies-Bouldin
  p3 <- ggplot(df, aes(x = k, y = davies_bouldin)) +
    geom_line() +
    geom_point() +
    ggtitle(paste("Davies-Bouldin Index vs k -", label)) +
    xlab("Número de Clusters (k)") + ylab("Davies-Bouldin Index") +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = unique(df$k)) +
    theme_minimal()
  
  # 4. Silhouette
  p4 <- ggplot(df, aes(x = k, y = average_silhouette)) +
    geom_line() +
    geom_point() +
    ggtitle(paste("Average Silhouette vs k -", label)) +
    xlab("Número de Clusters (k)") + ylab("Average Silhouette") +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = unique(df$k)) +
    theme_minimal()
  
  plots <- list(p1, p2, p3, p4)
  return(plots)
}

# Generar todas las gráficas automáticamente
all_plots <- imap(results_by_domain, ~ plot_clustering_metrics(.x, .y))

# Ver todas las gráficas
all_plots

```

## ***All Optimal

```{r}

analyze_progression_cluster <- function(severity_scores, score_col, label, k = 2, save_plot = TRUE) {
  library(dplyr)
  library(tidyr)
  library(ggplot2)
  library(factoextra)
  library(ggpubr)
  
  # --- 1. Selección de columnas ---
  df_long <- severity_scores[, c("participant_id", "Visit", score_col)]
  colnames(df_long)[3] <- "score"
  df_long <- df_long[order(df_long$participant_id, df_long$Visit), ]
  
  # --- 2. Wide format ---
  df_wide <- pivot_wider(df_long, names_from = Visit, values_from = score, names_prefix = "Visit_")
  
  # --- 3. Calcular S0 y diferencias entre visitas ---
  df_diff <- df_wide %>%
    mutate(
      S0 = Visit_0,
      diff_0_12 = Visit_12 - Visit_0,
      diff_12_24 = Visit_24 - Visit_12,
      diff_24_36 = Visit_36 - Visit_24
    ) %>%
    dplyr::select(participant_id, S0, diff_0_12, diff_12_24, diff_24_36)
  
  # --- 4. Filtrado y escalado ---
  data_to_scale <- df_diff[, -1]
  data_filtered <- data_to_scale[, apply(data_to_scale, 2, sd, na.rm = TRUE) != 0]
  data_scaled <- scale(data_filtered)
  
  # --- 5. K-means clustering ---
  km_cluster <- kmeans(data_scaled, centers = k, nstart = 25)
  df_diff$cluster <- factor(km_cluster$cluster)
  
  # --- 6. Long format para trayectorias ---
  df_long_diff <- df_diff %>%
    pivot_longer(cols = c("S0", "diff_0_12", "diff_12_24", "diff_24_36"),
                 names_to = "interval", values_to = "delta") %>%
    mutate(interval = factor(interval, levels = c("S0","diff_0_12","diff_12_24","diff_24_36")))
  
  # --- 7. Calcular media y SD por cluster ---
  summary_stats <- df_long_diff %>%
    group_by(cluster, interval) %>%
    summarise(
      media = mean(delta, na.rm = TRUE),
      desviacion = sd(delta, na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups = "drop"
    )
  
  # --- 8. Gráfico de trayectorias (media ± SD) ---
  trajectories <- ggplot(summary_stats, aes(x = interval, y = media, group = cluster, color = cluster)) +
    geom_line(size = 1) +
    geom_point(size = 2) +
    geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = media - desviacion, ymax = media + desviacion, fill = cluster), 
                alpha = 0.2, color = NA) +
    labs(title = paste("Trajectory of", label, "Percentile Change by Cluster"),
         x = "Visit Interval", y = "Change in Percentile (Mean ± SD)") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
      axis.title = element_text(size = 14),
      axis.text = element_text(size = 12),
      legend.title = element_text(size = 13, face = "bold"),
      legend.text = element_text(size = 12),
      legend.position = "bottom"
    )
  
  if (save_plot) {
    ggsave(paste0("trajectory_", label, "_k", k, ".png"), trajectories, 
           width = 10, height = 8, dpi = 300, bg = "white")
  }
  
  # --- 9. Añadir cluster al dataset original ---
  df_cluster <- df_diff[, c("participant_id", "cluster")]
  Covariates_cluster <- merge(df_cluster, severity_scores, by = "participant_id")
  

  return(list(trajectories = trajectories,
              clusters = Covariates_cluster
              ))
}

results_all_raw <- list(
  Motor = analyze_progression_cluster(severity_scores, "Motor_Score_raw_percentile", "Motor", k = 2),
  Non_Motor = analyze_progression_cluster(severity_scores, "Non_Motor_Score_raw_percentile", "Non-Motor", k = 3),
  Cognitive = analyze_progression_cluster(severity_scores, "Cognitive_Score_raw_percentile", "Cognitive", k = 5),
  Neuro = analyze_progression_cluster(severity_scores, "Neuro_Score_raw_percentile", "Neuro"),
  Neuro_Cogn = analyze_progression_cluster(severity_scores, "Neuro_Cogn_Score_raw_percentile", "Neuro-Cogn"),
  Sleep = analyze_progression_cluster(severity_scores, "Sleep_Score_raw_percentile", "Sleep"),
  Non_Motor_Overall = analyze_progression_cluster(severity_scores, "Non_Motor_Overall_Score_raw_percentile", "Non-Motor Overall")
)


results_all_raw
```

```{r}
k_non_motor <- 3
```

## Motor optimal
```{r}


# Mostramos las columnas que nos van a interesar en este apartado:
datatable(severity_scores[, c("participant_id", "Visit", "Motor_Score_raw_percentile")])


# Proceso de clustering
k <- 2

# Selección de las citadas columnas 
df_long <- severity_scores[, c("participant_id", "Visit", "Motor_Score_raw_percentile")]

# Nos aseguramos que el dataframe está ordenado por paciente y visita
df_long_cluster <- df_long[order(df_long$participant_id, df_long$Visit), ]

head(df_long_cluster)


# Transformar a formato wide, en el que cada paciente ocupa una fila y las columnas pasan a ser los valores de Motor_Score_Cumulative en cada visita
df_wide <- pivot_wider(df_long_cluster, names_from = Visit, values_from = Motor_Score_raw_percentile, names_prefix = "Visit_")

head(df_wide)


# Se genera el dataframe para el clustering a partir del anterior. 
# Contendrá el valor de la columna Visit_0 (score del paciente en visita M0) y los deltas entre visitas.
df_diff <- df_wide %>%
  mutate(
    S0 = Visit_0,
    diff_0_12 = (Visit_12 - Visit_0),
    diff_12_24 = (Visit_24 - Visit_12),
    diff_24_36 = (Visit_36 - Visit_24)
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(participant_id, S0, diff_0_12, diff_12_24, diff_24_36)

  # 1. Selecciona columnas numéricas (sin participant_id)
data_to_scale <- df_diff[, -1]

removed_vars <- names(data_to_scale)[apply(data_to_scale, 2, sd, na.rm = TRUE) == 0]
cat("Not considered due to no variation:", removed_vars)

# 2. Elimina columnas con desviación estándar cero
data_filtered <- data_to_scale[, apply(data_to_scale, 2, sd, na.rm = TRUE) != 0]

# 3. Escala solo las columnas válidas
data_scaled <- scale(data_filtered)

# 4. Aplica kmeans con la matriz escalada
km_cluster_motor <- kmeans(data_scaled, k, nstart = 25)

cluster_sizes <- table(km_cluster_motor$cluster)
cluster_labels <- paste0("Cluster ", names(cluster_sizes), ": n=", as.vector(cluster_sizes))
title_text <- paste(cluster_labels, collapse = " | ")

print(fviz_cluster(
  km_cluster_motor, 
  data = data_scaled, 
  main = title_text
))



df_diff$cluster <- factor(km_cluster_motor$cluster)

df_long_diff <- df_diff %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c("S0", "diff_0_12", "diff_12_24", "diff_24_36"),
    names_to = "interval",
    values_to = "delta"
  ) %>%
  # Convertir 'interval' en factor ordenado
  mutate(interval = factor(interval, levels = c("S0", "diff_0_12", "diff_12_24", "diff_24_36")))

# Si 'cluster' también necesita ordenarse, puedes hacer:
# df_long_diff <- df_long_diff %>%
#   mutate(cluster = factor(cluster, levels = c("Cluster1", "Cluster2", ...)))

summary_stats <- df_long_diff %>%
  group_by(cluster, interval) %>%
  summarise(
    mediana = median(delta, na.rm = TRUE),
    Q1 = quantile(delta, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
    Q3 = quantile(delta, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Generar el gráfico (el resto del código sigue igual)
trajectories <- ggplot(summary_stats, aes(x = interval, y = mediana, group = cluster, color = cluster)) +
  geom_line(size = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Q1, ymax = Q3, fill = cluster), alpha = 0.2, color = NA) +
  labs(
    title = "Trajectory of Motor Percentile Change by Cluster",
    x = "Visit Interval",
    y = "Change in Percentile (Median and IQR)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 14),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 12),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 13, face = "bold"),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 12),
    legend.position = "bottom"
  )

print(trajectories)



# Si necesitas calcular las estadísticas
summary_stats <- df_long_diff %>%
  group_by(cluster, interval) %>%
  summarise(
    media = mean(delta, na.rm = TRUE),
    desviacion = sd(delta, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = 'drop'
  )


# Generar el gráfico con desviación estándar en lugar de IQR
trajectories_sd <- ggplot(summary_stats, aes(x = interval, y = media, group = cluster, color = cluster)) +
  geom_line(size = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = media - desviacion, ymax = media + desviacion, fill = cluster), 
              alpha = 0.2, color = NA) +
  labs(
    title = "Trajectory of Motor Percentile Change by Cluster",
    x = "Visit Interval",
    y = "Change in Percentile (Mean ± SD)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 14),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 12),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 13, face = "bold"),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 12),
    legend.position = "bottom"
  )

print(trajectories)

print(trajectories_sd)




# Asegúrate de que el gráfico está creado y se llama `volcano_plot`
ggsave(
  filename = "trajectory_plot_k2.png",  # Nombre del archivo
  plot = trajectories,                       # Objeto ggplot
  device = "png",                            # Formato (también puede ser "pdf", "tiff", etc.)
  width = 10,                                # Ancho (en pulgadas)
  height = 8,                                # Alto (en pulgadas)
  units = "in",                              # Unidades ("in", "cm", "mm")
  dpi = 300,                                 # Resolución (300 dpi para alta calidad)
  bg = "white"                               # Fondo blanco (útil para PNG)
)




# Generamos el dataframe con las variables clínicas y las etiquetas de cluster motor
df_cluster_motor <- df_diff[, "participant_id"] %>% mutate(cluster_motor = km_cluster_motor$cluster)
  
Covariates_clusterMotor <- merge(df_cluster_motor, severity_scores, by = "participant_id") 

datatable(Covariates_clusterMotor, options = list(
  pageLength = 10,
  searching = TRUE,
  scrollX = TRUE))





# Calcular la diferencia M36 - M0 para :

Covariates_clusterMotor_diff <- Covariates_clusterMotor %>%
  group_by(participant_id) %>%
  summarise(
    cluster_motor = dplyr::first(cluster_motor),
    Motor_Score_Diff = Motor_Score_raw[visit_name == "M36"] - Motor_Score_raw[visit_name == "M0"]
  )

# Obtener el tamaño de cada cluster
cluster_sizes <- Covariates_clusterMotor_diff %>%
  dplyr::count(cluster_motor) %>%
  dplyr::rename(size = n)



# Convertir cluster_motor a factor y agregar el tamaño en las etiquetas
Covariates_clusterMotor_diff <- Covariates_clusterMotor_diff %>%
  mutate(cluster_motor = factor(cluster_motor, 
                                labels = paste0("M", levels(factor(cluster_motor)), 
                                                " (n=", cluster_sizes$size, ")")))


ggplot(Covariates_clusterMotor_diff, aes(x = cluster_motor, y = Motor_Score_Diff)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  stat_compare_means(
    method = "kruskal.test", 
    label.y = max(Covariates_clusterMotor_diff$Motor_Score_Diff) * 2,
    label.x = 1,  # Ajusta este valor según la ubicación deseada
    hjust = 0,    # Alinea el texto a la izquierda
    size = 3
  ) +
  stat_compare_means(
    method = "wilcox.test",
    comparisons = combn(levels(Covariates_clusterMotor_diff$cluster_motor), 2, simplify = FALSE),
    size = 3
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Diferencia en Motor Score entre M36 y M0 por cluster",
    x = "Cluster Motor (tamaño de muestra)",
    y = "Motor Score (M36 - M0)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 10),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 8)
  )




library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

# Transformamos a formato long para plot
df_long_percentile <- Covariates_clusterMotor %>%
  select(participant_id, cluster_motor, Visit, Motor_Score_raw_percentile) %>%
  mutate(cluster_motor = factor(cluster_motor))  # Por si acaso

# Calcular estadísticas resumen por cluster y visita usando media y SD
summary_percentile_sd <- df_long_percentile %>%
  group_by(cluster_motor, Visit) %>%
  summarise(
    media = mean(Motor_Score_raw_percentile, na.rm = TRUE),
    desviacion = sd(Motor_Score_raw_percentile, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Plot de percentiles absolutos con ±1 SD
percentile_sd_plot <- ggplot(summary_percentile_sd, aes(x = Visit, y = media, group = cluster_motor, color = cluster_motor)) +
  geom_line(size = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = media - desviacion, ymax = media + desviacion, fill = cluster_motor),
              alpha = 0.2, color = NA) +
  labs(
    title = "Motor Score Percentile by Cluster Across Visits",
    x = "Visit",
    y = "Motor Score Percentile (Mean ± SD)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 14),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 12),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 13, face = "bold"),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 12),
    legend.position = "bottom"
  )

print(percentile_sd_plot)

  distinct(participant_id, cluster_motor) %>%   # un paciente cuenta solo una vez
  count(cluster_motor, name = "n_patients")
```

###Diferencias entre clusters motores en clínica

```{r}

df_cluster_motor_PP <- df_cluster_motor %>% mutate(participant_id = paste("PP-", participant_id, sep = ""))
Covariates_score_cluster <- merge(df_cluster_motor_PP, Covariates_score, by = "participant_id") 


Covs_stad <- c(Motor, Non_Motor)


# Asumiendo que Covs_stad contiene los nombres de las covariables numéricas a analizar
# y que Covariates_score_cluster es tu dataframe

# Filtrar solo los datos de la visita M0
df_m0 <- Covariates_score_cluster[Covariates_score_cluster$visit_name == "M0", ]

# Obtener los clusters únicos
clusters <- unique(df_m0$cluster_motor)
clusters <- sort(clusters[!is.na(clusters)])
n_clust <- length(clusters)

# Inicializar dataframe para almacenar resultados
results_df <- data.frame(
  variable = character(),
  test = character(),
  p_value = numeric(),
  p_value_adj = numeric(),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# Función para agregar estadísticas descriptivas por cluster
add_cluster_stats <- function(results_df, variable, clusters, df_m0) {
  for (cluster in clusters) {
    cluster_data <- df_m0[df_m0$cluster_motor == cluster, variable]
    
    # Calcular estadísticas descriptivas
    mean_val <- mean(cluster_data, na.rm = TRUE)
    median_val <- median(cluster_data, na.rm = TRUE)
    sd_val <- sd(cluster_data, na.rm = TRUE)
    n <- sum(!is.na(cluster_data))
    
    # Agregar columnas para cada estadística por cluster
    results_df[[paste0("cluster_", cluster, "_mean")]] <- numeric()
    results_df[[paste0("cluster_", cluster, "_median")]] <- numeric()
    results_df[[paste0("cluster_", cluster, "_sd")]] <- numeric()
    results_df[[paste0("cluster_", cluster, "_n")]] <- numeric()
    
    # Asignar valores a la última fila
    if (nrow(results_df) > 0) {
      results_df[nrow(results_df), paste0("cluster_", cluster, "_mean")] <- round(mean_val, 3)
      results_df[nrow(results_df), paste0("cluster_", cluster, "_median")] <- round(median_val, 3)
      results_df[nrow(results_df), paste0("cluster_", cluster, "_sd")] <- round(sd_val, 3)
      results_df[nrow(results_df), paste0("cluster_", cluster, "_n")] <- n
    }
  }
  return(results_df)
}

# Inicializar las columnas de estadísticas descriptivas
for (cluster in clusters) {
  results_df[[paste0("cluster_", cluster, "_mean")]] <- numeric()
  results_df[[paste0("cluster_", cluster, "_median")]] <- numeric()
  results_df[[paste0("cluster_", cluster, "_sd")]] <- numeric()
  results_df[[paste0("cluster_", cluster, "_n")]] <- numeric()
}

# Realizar test para cada variable en Covs_stad
for (variable in Covs_stad) {
  
  if (is.numeric(df_m0[[variable]])) {
    
    # Verificar que hay al menos 2 clusters con datos
    valid_clusters <- sapply(clusters, function(clust) {
      sum(!is.na(df_m0[df_m0$cluster_motor == clust, variable])) > 0
    })
    
    if (sum(valid_clusters) >= 2) {
      
      # Realizar test de Kruskal-Wallis para múltiples grupos
      kruskal_test <- kruskal.test(df_m0[[variable]] ~ df_m0$cluster_motor)
      
      # Ajustar p-value usando Bonferroni
      p_value_adj <- p.adjust(kruskal_test$p.value, method = "bonferroni", 
                             n = n_clust * length(Covs_stad))
      
      # Agregar resultado principal
      new_row <- data.frame(
        variable = variable,
        test = "Kruskal-Wallis",
        p_value = round(kruskal_test$p.value, 6),
        p_value_adj = round(p_value_adj, 6),
        stringsAsFactors = FALSE
      )
      
      # Agregar estadísticas descriptivas
      for (cluster in clusters) {
        cluster_data <- df_m0[df_m0$cluster_motor == cluster, variable]
        
        if (sum(!is.na(cluster_data)) > 0) {
          mean_val <- mean(cluster_data, na.rm = TRUE)
          median_val <- median(cluster_data, na.rm = TRUE)
          sd_val <- sd(cluster_data, na.rm = TRUE)
          n <- sum(!is.na(cluster_data))
          
          new_row[[paste0("cluster_", cluster, "_mean")]] <- round(mean_val, 3)
          new_row[[paste0("cluster_", cluster, "_median")]] <- round(median_val, 3)
          new_row[[paste0("cluster_", cluster, "_sd")]] <- round(sd_val, 3)
          new_row[[paste0("cluster_", cluster, "_n")]] <- n
        } else {
          new_row[[paste0("cluster_", cluster, "_mean")]] <- NA
          new_row[[paste0("cluster_", cluster, "_median")]] <- NA
          new_row[[paste0("cluster_", cluster, "_sd")]] <- NA
          new_row[[paste0("cluster_", cluster, "_n")]] <- 0
        }
      }
      
      results_df <- rbind(results_df, new_row)
      
      # Si hay exactamente 2 clusters, realizar también Mann-Whitney
      if (length(clusters) == 2) {
        cluster_i_data <- df_m0[df_m0$cluster_motor == clusters[1], variable]
        cluster_j_data <- df_m0[df_m0$cluster_motor == clusters[2], variable]
        
        # Mann-Whitney test
        mw_test <- wilcox.test(cluster_i_data, cluster_j_data, 
                              exact = min(length(cluster_i_data), length(cluster_j_data)) < 20)
        
        # Ajustar p-value
        mw_p_value_adj <- p.adjust(mw_test$p.value, method = "bonferroni", 
                                  n = n_clust * length(Covs_stad))
        
        # Agregar resultado de Mann-Whitney
        mw_row <- data.frame(
          variable = variable,
          test = "Mann-Whitney",
          p_value = round(mw_test$p.value, 6),
          p_value_adj = round(mw_p_value_adj, 6),
          stringsAsFactors = FALSE
        )
        
        # Agregar estadísticas descriptivas (iguales que para Kruskal-Wallis)
        for (cluster in clusters) {
          cluster_data <- df_m0[df_m0$cluster_motor == cluster, variable]
          
          if (sum(!is.na(cluster_data)) > 0) {
            mean_val <- mean(cluster_data, na.rm = TRUE)
            median_val <- median(cluster_data, na.rm = TRUE)
            sd_val <- sd(cluster_data, na.rm = TRUE)
            n <- sum(!is.na(cluster_data))
            
            mw_row[[paste0("cluster_", cluster, "_mean")]] <- round(mean_val, 3)
            mw_row[[paste0("cluster_", cluster, "_median")]] <- round(median_val, 3)
            mw_row[[paste0("cluster_", cluster, "_sd")]] <- round(sd_val, 3)
            mw_row[[paste0("cluster_", cluster, "_n")]] <- n
          } else {
            mw_row[[paste0("cluster_", cluster, "_mean")]] <- NA
            mw_row[[paste0("cluster_", cluster, "_median")]] <- NA
            mw_row[[paste0("cluster_", cluster, "_sd")]] <- NA
            mw_row[[paste0("cluster_", cluster, "_n")]] <- 0
          }
        }
        
        results_df <- rbind(results_df, mw_row)
      }
    }
  }
}

# Ordenar resultados por variable y test
results_df <- results_df[order(results_df$variable, results_df$test), ]

# Mostrar resultados
print(results_df)




library(vegan)

# Seleccionar columnas clínicas
df_clin <- df_m0[, Covs_stad]

# Quitar filas con cualquier NA
df_complete <- df_clin[complete.cases(df_clin), ]

# Filtrar df_m0 para que coincida
df_m0_complete <- df_m0[complete.cases(df_clin), ]

# Estandarizar
df_complete <- scale(df_complete)

# Ejecutar PERMANOVA
adonis2(df_complete ~ cluster_motor, data = df_m0_complete, method = "euclidean", permutations = 999)

# Comparar cada variable clínica entre clusters
res_list <- lapply(names(df_m0_complete[, Covs_stad]), function(var) {
  formula <- as.formula(paste(var, "~ cluster_motor"))
  fit <- aov(formula, data = df_m0_complete)
  data.frame(
    variable = var,
    F = summary(fit)[[1]]$`F value`[1],
    p = summary(fit)[[1]]$`Pr(>F)`[1]
  )
})

res_df <- do.call(rbind, res_list)
res_df$p_adj <- p.adjust(res_df$p, method = "BH")  # corrección FDR
res_df[order(res_df$p_adj), ]

aggregate(df_m0_complete[, c("code_upd2hy_hoehn_and_yahr_stage", "moca_total_score")],
          by = list(cluster = df_m0_complete$cluster_motor),
          FUN = mean, na.rm = TRUE)



df_m0_complete$cluster_motor <- factor(df_m0_complete$cluster_motor)



ggarrange(
  # Hoehn & Yahr
  ggboxplot(df_m0_complete, x = "cluster_motor", y = "code_upd2hy_hoehn_and_yahr_stage",
            color = "cluster_motor", palette = "jco", add = "jitter") +
    stat_compare_means(method = "wilcox.test", label = "p.signif",
                       label.x = 0.5, label.y = max(df_m0_complete$code_upd2hy_hoehn_and_yahr_stage, na.rm = TRUE) + 0.2) +
    labs(y = "Hoehn & Yahr stage") +
    theme_minimal(),

  # MoCA
  ggboxplot(df_m0_complete, x = "cluster_motor", y = "moca_total_score",
            color = "cluster_motor", palette = "jco", add = "jitter") +
    stat_compare_means(method = "wilcox.test", label = "p.signif",
                       label.x = 0.5, label.y = max(df_m0_complete$moca_total_score, na.rm = TRUE) + 0.5) +
    labs(y = "MoCA total score") +
    theme_minimal(),

  ncol = 2, nrow = 1, common.legend = TRUE, legend = "right"
)
```

```{r}
library(ggpubr)
library(dplyr)

# Seleccionamos las variables de interés (ejemplo con H&Y y MoCA)
vars_to_plot <- c(Motor, Non_Motor, colnames(Covariates_score[,28:60]), "Abeta", "Tau", "p-Tau", "Motor_Score_raw", "Non_Motor_Score_raw")

# Nos aseguramos de que las visitas estén ordenadas correctamente
Covariates_score_cluster$visit_name <- factor(
  Covariates_score_cluster$visit_name,
  levels = c("M0", "M12", "M24", "M36")
)

# Convertimos cluster_motor en factor (por si acaso)
Covariates_score_cluster$cluster_motor <- as.factor(Covariates_score_cluster$cluster_motor)

# Función para crear un boxplot + trayectoria de medianas por variable
plot_by_visit <- function(df, variable, palette = "jco") {
  # Filtrar solo la variable que nos interesa
  df_var <- df %>% select(participant_id, visit_name, cluster_motor, !!sym(variable))
  
  # Renombrar para facilidad
  names(df_var)[4] <- "value"
  
  # Calcular las medianas por cluster y visita
  df_medians <- df_var %>%
    group_by(cluster_motor, visit_name) %>%
    summarise(mediana = median(value, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")
  
  # Crear el boxplot con comparación estadística dentro de cada visita
  p <- ggboxplot(df_var, 
                 x = "cluster_motor", 
                 y = "value",
                 color = "cluster_motor",
                 palette = palette,
                 add = "jitter") +
    facet_wrap(~ visit_name, ncol = 4) +  # una columna por visita
    stat_compare_means(
      aes(label = paste0("p = ", ..p.format..)),
      method = "wilcox.test", 
      label.y = max(df_var$value, na.rm = TRUE) * 1.05,
      size = 3
    ) +
    # Añadimos las trayectorias entre medianas
    geom_line(
      data = df_medians,
      aes(x = as.numeric(cluster_motor), y = mediana, group = cluster_motor, color = cluster_motor),
      size = 1.2
    ) +
    geom_point(
      data = df_medians,
      aes(x = as.numeric(cluster_motor), y = mediana, color = cluster_motor),
      size = 2
    ) +
    labs(
      x = "Cluster (Motor)",
      y = variable,
      title = paste("Evolution of", variable, "by cluster across visits")
    ) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
      strip.text = element_text(size = 12),
      axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
      axis.text = element_text(size = 10)
    )
  
  return(p)
}

# Generar los gráficos para cada variable
plots <- lapply(vars_to_plot, function(v) plot_by_visit(Covariates_score_cluster, v))

plots

```


### Modelo predictivo basado en clínica

```{r}

library(caret)
library(ranger)
library(doParallel)
library(dplyr)
library(foreach)

evaluate_cluster_with_clinical_covs <- function(
  cluster_labels,            # vector factor con cluster de cada paciente
  covariates_data,           # dataframe solo con Covs_stad (18 vars)
  n_bootstrap = 25,
  test_size = 0.2,
  n_cores = 2,
  mtry_select = c(3, 4, 5, 6),
  ntrees_select = 500,
  min.node.size_select = 1
) {
  library(caret)
  library(ranger)
  library(doParallel)
  library(dplyr)
  library(foreach)
  
  registerDoParallel(cores = n_cores)
  
  # Factor con nombres válidos
  cluster_labels <- factor(cluster_labels)
  levels(cluster_labels) <- make.names(levels(cluster_labels))
  
  # Preparar dataframe de modelado
  df_model <- covariates_data
  df_model$cluster <- cluster_labels
  
  # Split train/test
  set.seed(123)
  train_idx <- createDataPartition(df_model$cluster, p = 1 - test_size, list = FALSE)
  train_data <- df_model[train_idx, ]
  test_data <- df_model[-train_idx, ]
  
  # Control de entrenamiento
  ctrl <- trainControl(
    method = "repeatedcv",
    number = 3,
    repeats = 1,
    classProbs = TRUE,
    summaryFunction = multiClassSummary,
    allowParallel = TRUE
  )
  
  tune_grid <- expand.grid(
    mtry = mtry_select,
    splitrule = "gini",
    min.node.size = min.node.size_select
  )
  
  # Entrenamiento Random Forest
  set.seed(123)
  rf_model <- train(
    x = train_data %>% select(-cluster),
    y = train_data$cluster,
    method = "ranger",
    metric = "Accuracy",
    tuneGrid = tune_grid,
    trControl = ctrl,
    num.trees = ntrees_select,
    importance = "permutation"
  )
  
  # Predicciones test
  test_pred <- predict(rf_model, newdata = test_data)
  conf_matrix <- confusionMatrix(test_pred, test_data$cluster)
  
  balanced_accuracy_test <- if (is.matrix(conf_matrix$byClass)) {
    mean(conf_matrix$byClass[,"Sensitivity"], na.rm = TRUE)
  } else {
    conf_matrix$byClass["Balanced Accuracy"]
  }
  
  # Bootstrap para estabilidad
  bootstrap_importance <- foreach(i = 1:n_bootstrap, .combine = rbind, .packages = c("ranger")) %dopar% {
    idx <- sample(1:nrow(train_data), replace = TRUE)
    rf_boot <- ranger(
      formula = cluster ~ .,
      data = train_data[idx, ],
      num.trees = ntrees_select,
      importance = "permutation"
    )
    rf_boot$variable.importance
  }
  
  mean_importance <- colMeans(bootstrap_importance, na.rm = TRUE)
  
  # Resultado
  list(
    Model = rf_model,
    ConfusionMatrix = conf_matrix,
    BalancedAccuracyTest = balanced_accuracy_test,
    BootstrapVariableImportance = bootstrap_importance,
    MeanVariableImportance = mean_importance
  )
}



results_clinical <- evaluate_cluster_with_clinical_covs(
  cluster_labels = factor(make.names(df_diff$cluster)),   # cluster motor como factor con nombres válidos
  covariates_data = Covariates_score_cluster[Covs_stad],
  n_bootstrap = 25,
  n_cores = 4
)
```

```{r}

# --- PCA de covariables motoras por cluster de progresión ---

library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(ggrepel)
library(factoextra)

# 1. Seleccionar solo las covariables motoras
Motor_vars <- c(Motor)

# 2. Subconjunto de datos de la visita M0
df_motor_m0 <- df_m0 %>%
  dplyr::select(participant_id, cluster_motor, all_of(Motor_vars)) %>%
  drop_na()

# 3. Escalar las variables (muy importante para PCA)
motor_scaled <- scale(df_motor_m0[, Motor_vars])

# 4. PCA
pca_motor <- prcomp(motor_scaled, center = TRUE, scale. = TRUE)

# 5. Resumen de varianza explicada
summary(pca_motor)

# 6. Preparar data.frame con coordenadas PCA + cluster
df_pca_plot <- as.data.frame(pca_motor$x) %>%
  mutate(cluster_motor = factor(df_motor_m0$cluster_motor),
         participant_id = df_motor_m0$participant_id)

# 7. Porcentaje de varianza explicada
var_explained <- round(100 * summary(pca_motor)$importance[2, 1:2], 1)

# 8. Plot PCA con elipses por cluster
ggplot(df_pca_plot, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, color = cluster_motor)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.8) +
  stat_ellipse(level = 0.68, linetype = 2, linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_text_repel(aes(label = participant_id), size = 3, max.overlaps = 10, show.legend = FALSE) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#1f77b4", "#ff7f0e")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  labs(
    title = "PCA of Motor Covariates at Baseline (M0)",
    x = paste0("PC1 (", var_explained[1], "%)"),
    y = paste0("PC2 (", var_explained[2], "%)"),
    color = "Motor progression cluster"
  ) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "gray90"),
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold")
  )

# 9. Visualizar las contribuciones de las variables al PCA
fviz_pca_var(pca_motor,
             col.var = "contrib",
             gradient.cols = c("#00AFBB", "#E7B800", "#FC4E07"),
             repel = TRUE,
             title = "Contribution of Motor Covariates to PCA")






df_m12 <- Covariates_score_cluster[Covariates_score_cluster$visit_name == "M12", ]
df_m24 <- Covariates_score_cluster[Covariates_score_cluster$visit_name == "M24", ]
df_m36 <- Covariates_score_cluster[Covariates_score_cluster$visit_name == "M36", ]

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

# --- Definir variables motoras ---
Motor_vars <- Motor

# --- Unir todas las visitas ---
df_all_visits <- bind_rows(
  df_m0  %>% mutate(Visit = "M0"),
  df_m12 %>% mutate(Visit = "M12"),
  df_m24 %>% mutate(Visit = "M24"),
  df_m36 %>% mutate(Visit = "M36")
) %>%
  dplyr::select(participant_id, cluster_motor, Visit, all_of(Motor_vars)) %>%
  drop_na()

# --- PCA sobre M0 ---
df_m0_scaled <- scale(df_m0[, Motor_vars])
pca_motor <- prcomp(df_m0_scaled, center = TRUE, scale. = TRUE)

# --- Proyectar todas las visitas en el PCA de M0 ---
scaled_all <- scale(df_all_visits[, Motor_vars],
                    center = attr(df_m0_scaled, "scaled:center"),
                    scale = attr(df_m0_scaled, "scaled:scale"))

proj_all <- as.data.frame(predict(pca_motor, newdata = scaled_all))
proj_all <- cbind(df_all_visits[, c("participant_id", "cluster_motor", "Visit")], proj_all)

# --- Calcular centroides (PC1 y PC2 promedio por cluster y visita) ---
centroids <- proj_all %>%
  group_by(cluster_motor, Visit) %>%
  summarise(
    PC1 = mean(PC1),
    PC2 = mean(PC2),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# --- Ordenar las visitas para que la trayectoria salga en orden ---
centroids$Visit <- factor(centroids$Visit, levels = c("M0", "M12", "M24", "M36"))

# --- Graficar trayectorias de centroides ---
ggplot(centroids, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, color = as.factor(cluster_motor), group = cluster_motor)) +
  geom_path(linewidth = 1.2, alpha = 0.8) +            # trayectoria del cluster
  geom_point(aes(shape = Visit), size = 3, fill = "white") + # centroides
  geom_text(aes(label = Visit), vjust = -1, size = 3) +      # etiquetas de visita
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Dark2") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  labs(
    title = "Cluster trajectories across visits in motor PCA space",
    x = "PC1", y = "PC2", color = "Cluster", shape = "Visit"
  ) +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank())





```

```{r}

# Código para Non Motor - EJECUTAR PRIMERO
k_non_motor <- 3

# Procesamiento para Non Motor
df_long_non_motor <- severity_scores[, c("participant_id", "Visit", "Non_Motor_Score_raw_percentile")]
df_long_cluster_non_motor <- df_long_non_motor[order(df_long_non_motor$participant_id, df_long_non_motor$Visit), ]

df_wide_non_motor <- pivot_wider(df_long_cluster_non_motor, 
                                names_from = Visit, 
                                values_from = Non_Motor_Score_raw_percentile, 
                                names_prefix = "Visit_")

df_diff_non_motor <- df_wide_non_motor %>%
  mutate(
    S0 = Visit_0,
    diff_0_12 = (Visit_12 - Visit_0),
    diff_12_24 = (Visit_24 - Visit_12),
    diff_24_36 = (Visit_36 - Visit_24)
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(participant_id, S0, diff_0_12, diff_12_24, diff_24_36)

# Clustering Non Motor
data_to_scale_non_motor <- df_diff_non_motor[, -1]
data_filtered_non_motor <- data_to_scale_non_motor[, apply(data_to_scale_non_motor, 2, sd, na.rm = TRUE) != 0]
data_scaled_non_motor <- scale(data_filtered_non_motor)

km_cluster_non_motor <- kmeans(data_scaled_non_motor, k_non_motor, nstart = 25)

# Guardar objetos de Non Motor
save(k_non_motor, df_diff_non_motor, km_cluster_non_motor, data_scaled_non_motor,
     file = "saved_objects/non_motor_objects.RData")

# Crear plot para Non Motor con paleta magma
df_diff_non_motor$cluster <- factor(km_cluster_non_motor$cluster)

# Calcular tamaños de clusters para las etiquetas
cluster_sizes_non_motor <- table(km_cluster_non_motor$cluster)
cluster_labels_non_motor <- paste0("Cluster ", names(cluster_sizes_non_motor), 
                                  " (n=", as.vector(cluster_sizes_non_motor), ")")

df_long_diff_non_motor <- df_diff_non_motor %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c("S0", "diff_0_12", "diff_12_24", "diff_24_36"),
    names_to = "interval",
    values_to = "delta"
  ) %>%
  mutate(interval = factor(interval, levels = c("S0", "diff_0_12", "diff_12_24", "diff_24_36")))

summary_stats_non_motor <- df_long_diff_non_motor %>%
  group_by(cluster, interval) %>%
  summarise(
    media = mean(delta, na.rm = TRUE),
    desviacion = sd(delta, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = 'drop'
  )

# Plot Non Motor con paleta magma
trajectories_non_motor_sd <- ggplot(summary_stats_non_motor, 
                                   aes(x = interval, y = media, group = cluster, color = cluster)) +
  geom_line(size = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = media - desviacion, ymax = media + desviacion, fill = cluster), 
              alpha = 0.2, color = NA) +
  scale_color_manual(values = magma(length(cluster_sizes_non_motor)),
                     labels = cluster_labels_non_motor) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = magma(length(cluster_sizes_non_motor)),
                    labels = cluster_labels_non_motor) +
  labs(
    title = "Non-Motor Score Trajectories by Cluster",
    x = "Visit Interval",
    y = "Percentile Change (Mean ± SD)",
    color = "Cluster",
    fill = "Cluster"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 14),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 12),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 13, face = "bold"),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 12),
    legend.position = "bottom"
  )

print(trajectories_non_motor_sd)




# Generamos el dataframe con las variables clínicas y las etiquetas de cluster motor
df_cluster_non_motor <- df_diff_non_motor[, "participant_id"] %>% mutate(cluster_non_motor = km_cluster_non_motor$cluster)
  
Covariates_clusterNonMotor <- merge(df_cluster_non_motor, severity_scores, by = "participant_id") 

datatable(Covariates_clusterNonMotor, options = list(
  pageLength = 10,
  searching = TRUE,
  scrollX = TRUE))
```
