### Figura R4

```{r}

library(ggplot2)
library(ggrepel)
library(UpSetR)
library(tidyverse)
library(patchwork)
library(grid)
library(gridExtra)

# =============================================================================
# INITIAL CONFIGURATION AND COLORS
# =============================================================================

# Define distinctive colors for RF and DEG
color_rf <- "#FF6B00"  # Orange for Random Forest
color_deg <- "#00A8FF" # Blue for DEG

# Colors for clusters in volcano plots
color_cluster1 <- "#E41A1C"  # Red
color_cluster2 <- "#377EB8"  # Blue  
color_cluster3 <- "#4DAF4A"  # Green
color_none <- "gray70"

# =============================================================================
# CALCULATE COMMON Y-AXIS LIMITS FOR VOLCANO PLOTS
# =============================================================================

# Calculate Y-axis limits from both datasets
y_limits_motor <- range(-log10(volcano_motor_data$padj), na.rm = TRUE)
y_limits_non_motor <- range(-log10(volcano_non_motor_data$padj), na.rm = TRUE)

# Find common Y-axis limits
common_y_limits <- range(c(y_limits_motor, y_limits_non_motor), na.rm = TRUE)

# Extend the upper limit slightly for better visualization
common_y_limits[2] <- common_y_limits[2] * 1.05

cat("Common Y-axis limits for volcano plots:", common_y_limits, "\n")


x_limits_motor <- range(volcano_motor_data$log2FoldChange, na.rm = TRUE)
x_limits_non_motor <- range(volcano_non_motor_data$log2FoldChange, na.rm = TRUE)

# Find common X-axis limits
common_x_limits <- range(c(x_limits_motor, x_limits_non_motor), na.rm = TRUE)

# Extend the upper limit slightly for better visualization
common_x_limits[2] <- common_x_limits[2] * 1.05

cat("Common X-axis limits for volcano plots:", common_x_limits, "\n")



# =============================================================================
# VOLCANO PLOT - MOTOR
# =============================================================================
volcano_motor <- ggplot(volcano_motor_data, aes(x = log2FoldChange, y = -log10(padj))) +
  geom_point(aes(color = significance, alpha = significance), size = 1.8) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c(
      "Cluster 1" = color_cluster1,
      "Cluster 2" = color_cluster2,
      "Not significant" = color_none
    ),
    name = "Overexpressed in",
    drop = FALSE
  ) +
  scale_alpha_manual(
    values = c("Cluster 1" = 0.8, "Cluster 2" = 0.8, "Not significant" = 0.3),
    guide = "none"
  ) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(0.05), linetype = "dashed", color = "darkgray", linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = c(-0.5, 0.5), linetype = "dashed", color = "darkgray", linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_text_repel(
    data = top_deg_labels_symbol %>% filter(overexpressed_in %in% c("Cluster 1", "Cluster 2")),
    aes(label = gene),
    color = "black",
    size = 5,              # 🔹 Aumentado: etiquetas de genes (antes 2)
    box.padding = 0.4,
    max.overlaps = 15,
    segment.color = "grey50",
    segment.size = 0.3
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = common_y_limits) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = common_x_limits) +
  labs(
    x = "Log2 Fold Change",
    y = "-Log10(Adjusted p-value)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "top",
    legend.text = element_text(size = 12),   # 🔹 Aumentado (antes 8)
    legend.title = element_text(size = 12),  # 🔹 Aumentado (antes 9)
    axis.text = element_text(size = 9),      # 🔹 Aumentado (nuevo, texto ejes)
    axis.title = element_text(size = 11),    # 🔹 Aumentado (antes 10)
    panel.grid = element_blank()  # eliminar líneas de fondo
  )


# =============================================================================
# 2. VOLCANO PLOT - NON MOTOR (with all comparisons and common Y-scale)
# =============================================================================

# Prepare data for non-motor volcano plot - ensure all comparisons are represented
volcano_non_motor_data <- res_all %>%
  mutate(
    method = "DEG",
    # Create a factor that includes all comparisons, even if no DEGs
    significance = factor(
      case_when(
        significant & comparison == "1 vs 2+3" ~ "Cluster 1",
        significant & comparison == "2 vs 1+3" ~ "Cluster 2", 
        significant & comparison == "3 vs 1+2" ~ "Cluster 3",
        TRUE ~ "Not significant"
      ),
      levels = c("Cluster 1", "Cluster 2", "Cluster 3", "Not significant")
    )
  )

# Get top 10 genes by absolute log2FoldChange for each comparison, regardless of significance
top_10_non_motor <- res_all %>%
  group_by(comparison) %>%
  slice_max(order_by = abs(log2FoldChange), n = 10) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  # Add significance information for coloring
  mutate(
    significance = factor(
      case_when(
        significant & comparison == "1 vs 2+3" ~ "Cluster 1",
        significant & comparison == "2 vs 1+3" ~ "Cluster 2", 
        significant & comparison == "3 vs 1+2" ~ "Cluster 3",
        TRUE ~ "Not significant"
      ),
      levels = c("Cluster 1", "Cluster 2", "Cluster 3", "Not significant")
    )
  )


volcano_non_motor <- ggplot(volcano_non_motor_data, aes(x = log2FoldChange, y = -log10(padj))) +
  geom_point(aes(color = significance, alpha = significance), size = 1.8) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c(
      "Cluster 1" = color_cluster1,
      "Cluster 2" = color_cluster2,
      "Cluster 3" = color_cluster3,
      "Not significant" = color_none
    ),
    name = "Overexpressed in",
    drop = FALSE
  ) +
  scale_alpha_manual(
    values = c(
      "Cluster 1" = 0.8,
      "Cluster 2" = 0.8,
      "Cluster 3" = 0.8,
      "Not significant" = 0.3
    ),
    guide = "none"
  ) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(0.05), linetype = "dashed", color = "darkgray", linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = c(-0.5, 0.5), linetype = "dashed", color = "darkgray", linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_text_repel(
    data = top_10_non_motor %>% filter(significance != "Not significant"),
    aes(label = gene, color = significance),
    size = 5,               # 🔹 Aumentado (antes 2)
    box.padding = 0.4,
    max.overlaps = 30,
    segment.color = "grey50",
    segment.size = 0.3,
    show.legend = FALSE
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = common_y_limits) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = common_x_limits) +
  labs(
    x = "Log2 Fold Change",
    y = "-Log10(Adjusted p-value)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "top",
    legend.text = element_text(size = 12),   # 🔹 Aumentado
    legend.title = element_text(size = 12),  # 🔹 Aumentado
    axis.text = element_text(size = 9),      # 🔹 Aumentado
    axis.title = element_text(size = 11),    # 🔹 Aumentado
    panel.grid = element_blank(),  # eliminar líneas de fondo
  )

# =============================================================================
# HEATMAP - OVERLAPPING GENES
# =============================================================================
heatmap_plot <- ggplot(heatmap_ggplot_data, aes(x = Analysis, y = reorder(Gene, desc(Gene)))) +
  geom_tile(aes(fill = as.factor(Presence)), color = "white", linewidth = 0.3) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("0" = "white", "1" = "#2E86AB"), labels = c("0" = "Absent", "1" = "Present")) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c(
    "RF_Motor" = "RF\nMotor", 
    "DEG_Motor" = "DEG\nMotor", 
    "RF_NonMotor" = "RF\nNon-Motor", 
    "DEG_NonMotor" = "DEG\nNon-Motor"
  )) +
  labs(
    x = "Analysis",
    y = "Genes",
    fill = "Presence"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 0, hjust = 0.5, size = 12, face = "bold"),  # 🔹 Aumentado (antes 8)
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12),                                         # 🔹 Aumentado (antes 5)
    axis.title = element_text(size = 11),                                         # 🔹 Aumentado (antes 10)
    legend.position = "right",
    legend.text = element_text(size = 9),                                         # 🔹 Aumentado (antes 8)
    legend.title = element_text(size = 10),                                       # 🔹 Aumentado (antes 9)
    panel.grid = element_blank()
  )

# =============================================================================
# 4. CREATE UPSET PLOT AND SAVE AS IMAGE FOR INTEGRATION
# =============================================================================

# Cambiar los nombres de los conjuntos antes del upset
names(gene_sets_list) <- c("RF Motor", "DEG Motor", "RF Non-Motor", "DEG Non-Motor")

# Luego generas el plot como siempre
upset_plot <- upset(
  fromList(gene_sets_list),
  nsets = 4,
  nintersects = 12,
  order.by = "freq",
  mainbar.y.label = "Number of Genes",
  sets.x.label = "Genes per Method",
  sets.bar.color = c(color_rf, color_deg, color_rf, color_deg),
  matrix.color = "black",
  main.bar.color = "steelblue",
  matrix.dot.alpha = 0.7,
  text.scale = c(1.8, 1.4, 1.8, 1.2, 2.4, 2.4)
)


# Save upset plot temporarily
png("temp_upset.png", width = 7, height = 5, units = "in", res = 300)
print(upset_plot)
dev.off()

# Create a ggplot object for the upset plot image
upset_plot_gg <- ggplot() +
  annotation_custom(
    grid::rasterGrob(png::readPNG("temp_upset.png"), 
                     width = unit(1, "npc"), 
                     height = unit(1, "npc")),
    xmin = -Inf, xmax = Inf, ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf
  ) +
  theme_void()

# =============================================================================
# 5. COMBINE ALL PLOTS INTO SINGLE FIGURE
# =============================================================================

combined_figure <- 
  (volcano_non_motor | volcano_motor) /
  ((heatmap_plot | upset_plot_gg) + plot_layout(widths = c(0.8, 1.2))) +   
  plot_layout(heights = c(1, 1.2)) +
  plot_annotation(tag_levels = list(c('A', 'B', 'C', 'D'))) &
  theme(
    plot.tag = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 13, face = "bold")
  )

# Save the combined figure
ggsave(
  filename = "Combined_Figure_Publication.png",
  plot = combined_figure,
  device = "png",
  width = 16,
  height = 14,
  units = "in",
  dpi = 300,
  bg = "white"
)

# Clean up temporary file
if (file.exists("temp_upset.png")) {
  file.remove("temp_upset.png")
}

# =============================================================================
# 6. SUMMARY STATISTICS
# =============================================================================

# Create gene summary
gene_summary <- data.frame(
  Analysis = names(gene_sets_list),
  Number_of_Genes = sapply(gene_sets_list, length)
)

print(gene_summary)

# Calculate overlap between methods
cat("\nGene overlap between analyses:\n")
for(i in 1:(length(gene_sets_list)-1)) {
  for(j in (i+1):length(gene_sets_list)) {
    overlap <- length(intersect(gene_sets_list[[i]], gene_sets_list[[j]]))
    overlap_pct <- round(overlap / length(gene_sets_list[[i]]) * 100, 1)
    cat(paste0(names(gene_sets_list)[i], " vs ", names(gene_sets_list)[j], 
               ": ", overlap, " genes (", overlap_pct, "% overlap)\n"))
  }
}

cat("\nHeatmap summary:\n")
cat("Total unique genes across all analyses:", length(all_genes), "\n")
cat("Genes in at least 2 analyses:", sum(genes_in_multiple), "\n")
cat("Distribution across analyses:\n")
distribucion <- table(rowSums(heatmap_data))
for (i in 1:length(distribucion)) {
  cat("  - In", as.numeric(names(distribucion)[i]), "analyses:", distribucion[i], "genes\n")
}

# Check Non-Motor comparisons
cat("\nNon-Motor comparisons summary:\n")
non_motor_summary <- res_all %>%
  group_by(comparison) %>%
  summarise(
    total_genes = n(),
    significant_genes = sum(significant, na.rm = TRUE),
    max_log2FC = max(abs(log2FoldChange), na.rm = TRUE)
  )
print(non_motor_summary)

# Save individual plots for reference
ggsave("Volcano_NonMotor.png", volcano_non_motor, width = 7, height = 6, dpi = 300)
ggsave("Volcano_Motor.png", volcano_motor, width = 7, height = 6, dpi = 300)
ggsave("Heatmap_Overlapping_Genes.png", heatmap_plot, width = 6, height = 8, dpi = 300)

cat("\n=== FILES GENERATED ===\n")
cat("1. Combined_Figure_Publication.png - Main combined figure (A-D)\n")
cat("2. Volcano_NonMotor.png - Individual Non-Motor volcano plot\n")
cat("3. Volcano_Motor.png - Individual Motor volcano plot\n")
cat("4. Heatmap_Overlapping_Genes.png - Individual heatmap\n")

```





# Enriquecimiento

## Homogeneizar nombres de genes

```{r eval=FALSE}
# VarImp viene todo en ensembl
var_imp


# DEG viene con mezcla ensembl - symbol, no todos se pueden traducir




```




## MOTOR

### VarImp
```{r}


convert_gene_symbols <- readRDS("../Papers/convert_gene_symbols")
mart <- useDataset("hsapiens_gene_ensembl", useMart("ensembl"))

# 
# top_n <- 50
# var_imp_top <- var_imp %>% slice_max(order_by = Importance, n = top_n)

DEG_Gene <- genes_DEG_Motor$gene
VarImp_Gene <- var_imp$Variable


genes_unicos <- VarImp_Gene




enrich <- gprofiler2::gost(genes_unicos,
                                 correction_method = "bonferroni",
                                 sources = c("GO", "KEGG", "REAC"),
                                 organism = "hsapiens",
                                 exclude_iea = TRUE)



# Extraer los resultados del enriquecimiento
enrichment_results <- enrich$result

write.csv(as.matrix(enrichment_results), file = "enrich_results.csv", row.names = FALSE)

# saveRDS(enrichment_results, "enrichment_results_motor")


# Filtrar los términos con un valor p significativo (por ejemplo, p < 0.05)
filtered_results <- enrichment_results %>%
  dplyr::filter(p_value < 0.05)


top_results <- filtered_results %>%
  dplyr::filter(source %in% c("GO:BP")) %>%
  arrange(p_value) %>%
  head(25)
  


# Crear un gráfico de tipo "dot plot"
p1 <- ggplot(top_results, aes(x = reorder(term_name, -p_value), y = -log10(p_value))) +
  geom_point(aes(size = intersection_size, color = -log10(p_value)), alpha = 0.7) +
  scale_color_gradient(low = "blue", high = "red") +
  labs(x = "Enriched terms", y = "-log10(p-value)", 
       size = "Number of common names", color = "-log10(p-value)") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 6))

# Guardar en PNG alta resolución
ggsave("dotplot_enrichment_motor.png", plot = p1, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)


p2 <- ggplot(top_results, aes(x = -log10(p_value), y = reorder(term_name, -p_value))) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", aes(fill = -log10(p_value))) +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "blue", high = "red") +
  labs(title = "Enrichment Analysis of Biological Terms", x = "-log10(p-value)", y = "Enriched terms", fill = "-log10(p-value)") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10),  # Ajusta el tamaño de la fuente si es necesario
    plot.title = element_text(size = 14, hjust = 0.5)  # Tamaño reducido y centrado
  )

# Guardar en PNG alta resolución
ggsave("barplot_enrichment_motor.png", plot = p2, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)


# Suponiendo que 'filtered_results' contiene los datos relevantes (p_value, term_size, term_name)
p3 <- ggplot(top_results, aes(x = intersection_size, y = -log10(p_value))) +
  geom_point(aes(size = intersection_size, color = -log10(p_value)), alpha = 0.7) +  # Puntos
  scale_color_gradient(low = "blue", high = "red") +
  labs(x = "Intersection Size", y = "-log10(p-value)", 
       size = "Term Size", color = "-log10(p-value)") +
  theme_minimal() +
  geom_text(aes(label = term_name), vjust = -0.5, hjust = 1.2, size = 2, color = "black")  # Etiquetas

# Guardar en PNG alta resolución
ggsave("pointplot_enrichment_motor.png", plot = p3, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)




p <- gostplot(enrich, capped = FALSE, interactive = TRUE)
p






library(clusterProfiler)
library(org.Hs.eg.db)

# Pasar los IDs GO
ego <- enrichGO(
  gene          = genes_unicos,
  OrgDb         = org.Hs.eg.db,
  keyType       = "ENSEMBL",
  ont           = "BP",
  pAdjustMethod = "BH",
  pvalueCutoff  = 0.05,
  qvalueCutoff  = 0.05
)

# Simplificar (reduce redundancia semántica)
ego_simplified <- simplify(ego, cutoff = 0.7, by = "p.adjust", select_fun = min)



ego_simplified_df <- as.data.frame(ego_simplified)


```

```{r}


convert_gene_symbols <- readRDS("../Papers/convert_gene_symbols")

VarImp_Gene <- var_imp$Variable

# Fusionar y eliminar duplicados

# Crear un gráfico de tipo "dot plot"

  geom_point(aes(size = intersection_size, color = -log10(p_value)), alpha = 0.7) +

p2 <- ggplot(top_results, aes(x = -log10(p_value), y = reorder(term_name, -p_value))) +

  scale_fill_gradient(low = "blue", high = "red") +

  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10),  # Ajusta el tamaño de la fuente si es necesario
    plot.title = element_text(size = 14, hjust = 0.5)  # Tamaño reducido y centrado
  )

# Guardar en PNG alta resolución
ggsave("barplot_enrichment_motor.png", plot = p2, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)


# Suponiendo que 'filtered_results' contiene los datos relevantes (p_value, term_size, term_name)
p3 <- ggplot(top_results, aes(x = intersection_size, y = -log10(p_value))) +
  geom_point(aes(size = intersection_size, color = -log10(p_value)), alpha = 0.7) +  # Puntos
  scale_color_gradient(low = "blue", high = "red") +
  labs(x = "Intersection Size", y = "-log10(p-value)", 
       size = "Term Size", color = "-log10(p-value)") +
  theme_minimal() +
  geom_text(aes(label = term_name), vjust = -0.5, hjust = 1.2, size = 2, color = "black")  # Etiquetas

# Guardar en PNG alta resolución
ggsave("pointplot_enrichment_motor.png", plot = p3, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)




p <- gostplot(enrich, capped = FALSE, interactive = TRUE)

# Pasar los IDs GO
ego <- enrichGO(
  gene          = genes_unicos,
  OrgDb         = org.Hs.eg.db,
```

### Comparación

```{r}

# Preparar datos para comparación
enrich1 <- filtered_results %>%
  mutate(source = "Enrichment_VarImp") %>%
  select(source, term_id, term_name, p_value, intersection_size)

enrich2 <- filtered_results_DEG %>%
  mutate(source = "Enrichment_DEG") %>%
  select(source, term_id, term_name, p_value, intersection_size)

# Combinar y seleccionar términos más significativos
combined <- bind_rows(enrich1, enrich2) %>%
  group_by(term_id) %>%
  filter(n() == 2) %>% # Solo términos presentes en ambos
  ungroup() 


combined <- bind_rows(enrich1, enrich2) %>%
  group_by(term_id) %>%
  # filter(n() == 2) %>% # Solo términos presentes en ambos
  # ungroup() %>%
  group_by(source) %>%
  arrange(p_value) %>% # Ordenar por p-value (ascendente = más significativos primero)
  slice_head(n = 20) %>% # Tomar los 10 primeros (más significativos)
  ungroup()

# Crear dotplot comparativo
ggplot(combined, aes(x = source, y = reorder(term_name, -p_value), 
                    size = intersection_size, color = -log10(p_value))) +
  geom_point() +
  scale_color_gradient(low = "blue", high = "red", name = "-log10(p-value)") +
  labs(x = "Enrichment Source", y = "Term", 
       title = "Comparative Enrichment Analysis") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))



# Crear dotplot comparativo
p <- ggplot(combined, aes(x = source, y = reorder(term_name, -p_value), 
                          size = intersection_size, color = -log10(p_value))) +
  geom_point() +
  scale_color_gradient(low = "blue", high = "red", name = "-log10(p-value)") +
  labs(x = "Enrichment Source", y = "Term", 
       title = "Comparative Enrichment Analysis") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

# Guardar en alta calidad
ggsave("comparative_enrichment.pdf", p, width = 10, height = 8, dpi = 300)
ggsave("comparative_enrichment.png", p, width = 10, height = 8, dpi = 300)



```




## NO MOTOR

### VarImp
```{r}

convert_gene_symbols <- readRDS("../Papers/convert_gene_symbols")
mart <- useDataset("hsapiens_gene_ensembl", useMart("ensembl"))


top_n <- 50
var_imp_top <- var_imp_Non_Motor %>% slice_max(order_by = Importance, n = top_n)


ensembl <- useMart("ensembl", dataset = "hsapiens_gene_ensembl")
gene_map <- getBM(attributes = c("ensembl_gene_id","hgnc_symbol"),
                  filters = "ensembl_gene_id",
                  values = VarImp_Gene,
                  mart = ensembl)

VarImp_Gene_symbols <- gene_map$hgnc_symbol
VarImp_Gene_symbols <- VarImp_Gene_symbols[VarImp_Gene_symbols != ""]  # quitar vacíos

genes_unicos <- unique(c(DEG_Gene, VarImp_Gene_symbols))



enrich <- gprofiler2::gost(genes_unicos,
                                 correction_method = "bonferroni",
                                 sources = c("GO", "KEGG", "REAC", "HP", "TF", "MIRNA", "CORUM", "HPA","WP"),
                                 organism = "hsapiens",
                                 exclude_iea = TRUE)


# Extraer los resultados del enriquecimiento
enrichment_results <- enrich$result

write.csv(as.matrix(enrichment_results), file = "enrich_results_NonMotor.csv", row.names = FALSE)

# saveRDS(enrichment_results, "enrichment_results_NonMotor")


# Filtrar los términos con un valor p significativo (por ejemplo, p < 0.05)
filtered_results <- enrichment_results %>%
  dplyr::filter(p_value < 0.05)


top_results <- filtered_results %>%
  dplyr::filter(source %in% c("GO:BP", "KEGG", "REAC")) %>%
  arrange(p_value) %>%
  head(25)


# Crear un gráfico de tipo "dot plot"
p1 <- ggplot(top_results, aes(x = reorder(term_name, -p_value), y = -log10(p_value))) +
  geom_point(aes(size = intersection_size, color = -log10(p_value)), alpha = 0.7) +
  scale_color_gradient(low = "blue", high = "red") +
  labs(x = "Enriched terms", y = "-log10(p-value)", 
       size = "Number of common names", color = "-log10(p-value)") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 6))

# Guardar en PNG alta resolución
ggsave("dotplot_enrichment_non_motor.png", plot = p1, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)


p2 <- ggplot(top_results, aes(x = -log10(p_value), y = reorder(term_name, -p_value))) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", aes(fill = -log10(p_value))) +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "blue", high = "red") +
  labs(title = "Enrichment Analysis of Biological Terms", x = "-log10(p-value)", y = "Enriched terms", fill = "-log10(p-value)") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10),  # Ajusta el tamaño de la fuente si es necesario
    plot.title = element_text(size = 14, hjust = 0.5)  # Tamaño reducido y centrado
  )

# Guardar en PNG alta resolución
ggsave("barplot_enrichment_non_motor.png", plot = p2, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)


# Suponiendo que 'filtered_results' contiene los datos relevantes (p_value, term_size, term_name)
p3 <- ggplot(top_results, aes(x = intersection_size, y = -log10(p_value))) +
  geom_point(aes(size = intersection_size, color = -log10(p_value)), alpha = 0.7) +  # Puntos
  scale_color_gradient(low = "blue", high = "red") +
  labs(x = "Intersection Size", y = "-log10(p-value)", 
       size = "Term Size", color = "-log10(p-value)") +
  theme_minimal() +
  geom_text(aes(label = term_name), vjust = -0.5, hjust = 1.2, size = 2, color = "black")  # Etiquetas

# Guardar en PNG alta resolución
ggsave("pointplot_enrichment_non_motor.png", plot = p3, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)




p <- gostplot(enrich, capped = FALSE, interactive = TRUE)
p






library(clusterProfiler)
library(org.Hs.eg.db)

# Pasar los IDs GO
ego <- enrichGO(
  gene          = genes_unicos,
  OrgDb         = org.Hs.eg.db,
  keyType       = "ENSEMBL",
  ont           = "BP",
  pAdjustMethod = "BH",
  pvalueCutoff  = 0.05,
  qvalueCutoff  = 0.05
)

# Simplificar (reduce redundancia semántica)
ego_simplified_NonMotor <- simplify(ego, cutoff = 0.7, by = "p.adjust", select_fun = min)








```

### DEG
```{r}

convert_gene_symbols <- readRDS("../Papers/convert_gene_symbols")
mart <- useDataset("hsapiens_gene_ensembl", useMart("ensembl"))


top_n <- 50
var_imp_top <- var_imp_Non_Motor %>% slice_max(order_by = Importance, n = top_n)


ensembl <- useMart("ensembl", dataset = "hsapiens_gene_ensembl")
gene_map <- getBM(attributes = c("ensembl_gene_id","hgnc_symbol"),
                  filters = "ensembl_gene_id",
                  values = VarImp_Gene,
                  mart = ensembl)

VarImp_Gene_symbols <- gene_map$hgnc_symbol
VarImp_Gene_symbols <- VarImp_Gene_symbols[VarImp_Gene_symbols != ""]  # quitar vacíos

genes_unicos <- unique(c(DEG_Gene, VarImp_Gene_symbols))



enrich <- gprofiler2::gost(genes_unicos,
                                 correction_method = "bonferroni",
                                 sources = c("GO", "KEGG", "REAC", "HP", "TF", "MIRNA", "CORUM", "HPA","WP"),
                                 organism = "hsapiens",
                                 exclude_iea = TRUE)


# Extraer los resultados del enriquecimiento
enrichment_results <- enrich$result

write.csv(as.matrix(enrichment_results), file = "enrich_results_NonMotor.csv", row.names = FALSE)

# saveRDS(enrichment_results, "enrichment_results_NonMotor")


# Filtrar los términos con un valor p significativo (por ejemplo, p < 0.05)
filtered_results <- enrichment_results %>%
  dplyr::filter(p_value < 0.05)


top_results <- filtered_results %>%
  dplyr::filter(source %in% c("GO:BP", "KEGG", "REAC")) %>%
  arrange(p_value) %>%
  head(25)


# Crear un gráfico de tipo "dot plot"
p1 <- ggplot(top_results, aes(x = reorder(term_name, -p_value), y = -log10(p_value))) +
  geom_point(aes(size = intersection_size, color = -log10(p_value)), alpha = 0.7) +
  scale_color_gradient(low = "blue", high = "red") +
  labs(x = "Enriched terms", y = "-log10(p-value)", 
       size = "Number of common names", color = "-log10(p-value)") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 6))

# Guardar en PNG alta resolución
ggsave("dotplot_enrichment_non_motor.png", plot = p1, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)


p2 <- ggplot(top_results, aes(x = -log10(p_value), y = reorder(term_name, -p_value))) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", aes(fill = -log10(p_value))) +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "blue", high = "red") +
  labs(title = "Enrichment Analysis of Biological Terms", x = "-log10(p-value)", y = "Enriched terms", fill = "-log10(p-value)") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10),  # Ajusta el tamaño de la fuente si es necesario
    plot.title = element_text(size = 14, hjust = 0.5)  # Tamaño reducido y centrado
  )

# Guardar en PNG alta resolución
ggsave("barplot_enrichment_non_motor.png", plot = p2, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)


# Suponiendo que 'filtered_results' contiene los datos relevantes (p_value, term_size, term_name)
p3 <- ggplot(top_results, aes(x = intersection_size, y = -log10(p_value))) +
  geom_point(aes(size = intersection_size, color = -log10(p_value)), alpha = 0.7) +  # Puntos
  scale_color_gradient(low = "blue", high = "red") +
  labs(x = "Intersection Size", y = "-log10(p-value)", 
       size = "Term Size", color = "-log10(p-value)") +
  theme_minimal() +
  geom_text(aes(label = term_name), vjust = -0.5, hjust = 1.2, size = 2, color = "black")  # Etiquetas

# Guardar en PNG alta resolución
ggsave("pointplot_enrichment_non_motor.png", plot = p3, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)




p <- gostplot(enrich, capped = FALSE, interactive = TRUE)
p
```

# GSEA

```{r}

library(clusterProfiler)
library(org.Hs.eg.db)
library(msigdbr)
library(dplyr)

### 1) Preparar ranking VarImp
varimp_ranking <- var_imp %>%
  arrange(desc(Importance)) %>%
  distinct(Variable, .keep_all = TRUE)

# Mapear Ensembl → Entrez
varimp_map <- bitr(varimp_ranking$Variable,
                   fromType = "ENSEMBL",
                   toType = "ENTREZID",
                   OrgDb = org.Hs.eg.db)

# Unir con importancia
varimp_ranking_mapped <- varimp_ranking %>%
  inner_join(varimp_map, by = c("Variable" = "ENSEMBL"))

# Crear vector nombrado: valores = Importance, nombres = ENTREZID
varimp_vector <- varimp_ranking_mapped$Importance
names(varimp_vector) <- varimp_ranking_mapped$ENTREZID
varimp_vector <- sort(varimp_vector, decreasing = TRUE)

### 2) Colección de genes
msig_c5 <- msigdbr(species = "Homo sapiens", collection = "C5") %>%
  dplyr::select(gs_name, ncbi_gene)

### 3) Ejecutar GSEA
gsea_varimp <- GSEA(geneList = varimp_vector,
                    TERM2GENE = msig_c5,
                    pvalueCutoff = 0.05)

head(gsea_varimp@result)
dotplot(gsea_varimp, showCategory = 20)






### DEG ranking
deg_map <- bitr(genes_DEG_Motor$gene,
                fromType = "SYMBOL",
                toType = "ENTREZID",
                OrgDb = org.Hs.eg.db)

deg_ranking <- genes_DEG_Motor %>%
  inner_join(deg_map, by = c("gene" = "SYMBOL"))

deg_vector <- deg_ranking$log2FoldChange
names(deg_vector) <- deg_ranking$ENTREZID
deg_vector <- sort(deg_vector, decreasing = TRUE)

### GSEA
gsea_deg <- GSEA(geneList = deg_vector,
                 TERM2GENE = msig_c5,
                 pvalueCutoff = 0.05)

head(gsea_deg@result)

dotplot(gsea_deg, showCategory = 20)
cnetplot(gsea_deg)




```

## VarImp

```{r}

# Primero necesitamos un ranking, podemos usar el log2FoldChange
VarImp_ranking <- data.frame(
  gene = var_imp$Variable,
  importance = var_imp$Importance
)

# Convertir a Entrez y ordenar por log2FC
VarImp_ranking$entrez <- mapIds(org.Hs.eg.db,
                            keys = VarImp_ranking$gene,
                            column = "ENTREZID",
                            keytype = "ENSEMBL",
                            multiVals = "first")

VarImp_ranking <- na.omit(VarImp_ranking)
VarImp_ranking <- VarImp_ranking[order(-abs(VarImp_ranking$importance)), ]


# 1. Crear ranking (ya lo tienes)
VarImp_gene_list <- VarImp_ranking$importance
names(VarImp_gene_list) <- as.character(VarImp_ranking$entrez)
VarImp_gene_list <- sort(VarImp_gene_list, decreasing = TRUE)

# 2. Obtener pathways de GO (ejemplo: Biological Process)
msig_go_bp <- msigdbr(species = "Homo sapiens")

# 3. Convertir a lista de pathways
pathways_bp <- split(msig_go_bp$ncbi_gene, msig_go_bp$gs_name)

# 4. Ejecutar fgsea
gsea_VarImp <- fgsea(
  pathways = pathways_bp,
  stats = VarImp_gene_list,
  minSize = 15,
  maxSize = 500
  )

# 5. Resultados ordenados por pval
gsea_VarImp <- gsea_VarImp[order(pval), ]



# Seleccionar top 20 pathways por p-valor
topPathways_VarImp <- head(gsea_VarImp[order(gsea_VarImp$pval), ], 20)

ggplot(topPathways_VarImp, aes(x = reorder(pathway, NES), y = NES, fill = padj < 0.05)) +
  geom_col() +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(x = "Pathway", y = "Normalized Enrichment Score (NES)",
       title = "Top enriched pathways (fgsea)") +
  theme_minimal()



# Elige el pathway que quieras explorar
pathway_of_interest <- topPathways_VarImp$pathway[1]

plotEnrichment(pathways_bp[[pathway_of_interest]], stats = deg_gene_list) +
  labs(title = pathway_of_interest)




ggplot(topPathways_VarImp, aes(x = NES, y = reorder(pathway, NES),
                        size = size, color = padj)) +
  geom_point() +
  scale_color_gradient(low = "red", high = "blue", name = "padj") +
  labs(x = "Normalized Enrichment Score (NES)", y = "Pathway",
       title = "Top pathways - fgsea") +
  theme_minimal()









# --- Barplot NES ---
p1 <- ggplot(topPathways_VarImp, aes(x = reorder(pathway, NES), y = NES, fill = padj < 0.05)) +
  geom_col() +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(x = "Pathway", y = "Normalized Enrichment Score (NES)",
       title = "Top enriched pathways (fgsea)") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 13),
    legend.position = "bottom"
  )

ggsave("fgsea_topPathways_barplot.pdf", p1, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300)
ggsave("fgsea_topPathways_barplot.tiff", p1, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300, compression = "lzw")


# --- Bubble plot NES vs pathway ---
p2 <- ggplot(topPathways_VarImp, aes(x = NES, y = reorder(pathway, NES),
                                     size = size, color = padj)) +
  geom_point() +
  scale_color_gradient(low = "red", high = "blue", name = "padj") +
  labs(x = "Normalized Enrichment Score (NES)", y = "Pathway",
       title = "Top pathways - fgsea") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 13),
    legend.position = "right"
  )

ggsave("fgsea_topPathways_bubble.pdf", p2, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300)
ggsave("fgsea_topPathways_bubble.tiff", p2, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300, compression = "lzw")


# --- Enrichment plot for one pathway ---
pathway_of_interest <- topPathways_VarImp$pathway[1]
p3 <- plotEnrichment(pathways_bp[[pathway_of_interest]], stats = VarImp_gene_list) +
  labs(title = pathway_of_interest)

ggsave("fgsea_pathway_enrichment.pdf", p3, width = 7, height = 5, dpi = 300)
ggsave("fgsea_pathway_enrichment.tiff", p3, width = 7, height = 5, dpi = 300, compression = "lzw")

```



## DEG
```{r}

# Primero necesitamos un ranking, podemos usar el log2FoldChange
deg_ranking <- data.frame(
  gene = genes_DEG_Motor$gene,
  log2FC = genes_DEG_Motor$log2FoldChange,
  padj = genes_DEG_Motor$padj
)

# Convertir a Entrez y ordenar por log2FC
deg_ranking$entrez <- mapIds(org.Hs.eg.db,
                            keys = deg_ranking$gene,
                            column = "ENTREZID",
                            keytype = "ENSEMBL",
                            multiVals = "first")

deg_ranking <- na.omit(deg_ranking)
deg_ranking <- deg_ranking[order(-abs(deg_ranking$log2FC)), ]


# 1. Crear ranking (ya lo tienes)
deg_gene_list <- deg_ranking$log2FC
names(deg_gene_list) <- as.character(deg_ranking$entrez)
deg_gene_list <- sort(deg_gene_list, decreasing = TRUE)


# 4. Ejecutar fgsea
gsea_deg <- fgsea(
  pathways = pathways_bp,
  stats = deg_gene_list,
  minSize = 15,
  maxSize = 500
  )

# 5. Resultados ordenados por pval
gsea_deg <- gsea_deg[order(pval), ]



# Seleccionar top 20 pathways por p-valor
topPathways <- head(gsea_deg[order(gsea_deg$pval), ], 20)

ggplot(topPathways, aes(x = reorder(pathway, NES), y = NES, fill = padj < 0.05)) +
  geom_col() +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(x = "Pathway", y = "Normalized Enrichment Score (NES)",
       title = "Top enriched pathways (fgsea)") +
  theme_minimal()



# Elige el pathway que quieras explorar
pathway_of_interest <- topPathways$pathway[1]

plotEnrichment(pathways_bp[[pathway_of_interest]], stats = deg_gene_list) +
  labs(title = pathway_of_interest)


library(ggplot2)

topPathways <- head(gsea_deg[order(gsea_deg$padj), ], 20)

ggplot(topPathways, aes(x = NES, y = reorder(pathway, NES),
                        size = size, color = padj)) +
  geom_point() +
  scale_color_gradient(low = "red", high = "blue", name = "padj") +
  labs(x = "Normalized Enrichment Score (NES)", y = "Pathway",
       title = "Top pathways - fgsea") +
  theme_minimal()


```
